narrativeText: Without hesitation, you raise your sword high above your head, the medallion still secure around your sword hand, and bring the blade down upon the skull with all the force you can muster. The enhanced weapon strikes true, and the ancient bone shatters into countless fragments that scatter across the altar's surface like broken pottery. The moment of destruction releases a sound unlike anything you have heard before, a baleful moan that seems to come from the very stones of the watchtower itself, rising in pitch until it becomes an unearthly wail that echoes through the chamber and out into the night beyond. As the sound fades, something remarkable occurs. A translucent figure begins to materialise above the shattered remains of the skull, tall and regal, wearing the garments of ancient nobility. This spectral form raises one hand in what might be a gesture of gratitude or farewell, then slowly rises upward, growing fainter with each moment until it vanishes entirely through the tower's ceiling. You understand immediately what you have witnessed, the wraith lord that had been bound to this place, the master of all the creatures that terrorised the surrounding lands, has finally been released from its cursed existence. The village below, the travellers on the roads, all are now safe from the supernatural threat that has plagued them for so long. A deep sense of satisfaction settles over you as you realise the magnitude of what you have accomplished.

choices.choiceText: Rest until morning and use the watchtower to view the journey ahead
Gather your wits and continue on your journey

title: A view forward
narrativeText: You decide to rest through the remaining hours of darkness within the now-cleansed watchtower. The oppressive atmosphere that had weighed so heavily upon this place has lifted completely, replaced by a sense of peace that settles over the ancient stones like a blessing. When the first light of dawn creeps through the tower's windows, you climb the narrow spiral staircase that winds up the interior walls, your footsteps no longer echoing with supernatural dread but with the simple sounds of boots upon worn stone. At the top, you emerge onto the watchtower's circular platform, where the morning sun greets you with warm rays that chase away the last shadows of the night's ordeal. From this elevated vantage point, the entire surrounding landscape spreads out before you like a vast map. To the east, you can see the winding route of the main road as it curves around the base of distant hills, the same well-travelled path that countless merchants and pilgrims have used over the years. To the west, cutting between towering cliffs, you make out the narrow entrance to what must be the Whispering Canyons, the treacherous shortcut the elders spoke of with such concern. The morning air is fresh and clean, carrying the scents of wildflowers and new grass, a marked contrast to the supernatural miasma that had surrounded this place only hours before.

choices.choiceText: Head to the Whispering canyons
Continue your journey on the main road

title: Proceed cautiously through the canyons.
narrativeText: Your resolve firm, you continue deeper into the Whispering Canyons, following the winding path as it leads you between towering walls of ancient stone. The rocky surface beneath your feet remains uneven and treacherous, requiring careful attention with each step to avoid loose stones that might shift beneath your weight. Above you, the canyon walls rise so high that only a narrow ribbon of sky remains visible, casting the passage in perpetual twilight despite the daylight hours. The wind continues to create its phantom voices, echoing off the stone surfaces in ways that sometimes sound remarkably like distant conversations in unknown tongues, or familiar voices calling your name from around the next bend. As you progress further into this natural labyrinth, movement ahead catches your attention. There, standing motionless in the centre of the narrow passage, is a tall figure. Its form appears human in general shape, but it stands perfectly still, as rigid as a statue, facing directly ahead as though you do not exist. The figure blocks the pathway completely, and the canyon walls rise too steeply on either side to offer any possibility of climbing around this obstacle. You must decide how to proceed, for there is no other route forward through this section of the canyons.

choices.choiceText: Approach the figure cautiously
Decide that this may be too risky and return to the main path

title: Approach the figure cautiously
narrativeText: You draw closer with deliberate, quiet steps, your hand instinctively moving near your sword hilt. The figure remains perfectly still, standing within a circle of small earthen mounds that rise knee-high from the canyon floor. As you approach to within ten paces, you can observe that the entity gazes straight ahead, as though you are not present. Its form appears humanoid but unnaturally rigid. The narrow passage between the canyon walls leaves scarcely enough room for two people to pass, and this silent guardian occupies the centre of the gap. The whispers in the wind seem to intensify around the mounds.

choices.choiceText: Try and walk past the entity
Speak to it

title: Try and walk past the entity
narrativeText: You attempt to edge around the motionless figure, keeping close to the canyon wall. The moment you step within arm's reach, the entity moves with startling swiftness, pivoting to block your path entirely. Now it stares directly into your eyes, and you can hear the sound of your own heartbeat in the sudden silence. The earthen mounds surrounding it begin to tremble, loose soil cascading down their sides. In a voice that carries the weight of stone and ancient earth, the entity declares that it is the guardian of this pass. It states that only the servants of Baelmat may proceed beyond this point. The name means nothing to you.

choices.choiceText: Declare yourself a servant of Baelmat
Ask who Baelmat is
State that you don't care what it is get out of your way

title: Speak to it
narrativeText: You call out a greeting to the motionless figure. The entity's head turns towards you with a grinding sound like stone against stone, and you find yourself meeting its penetrating gaze. In that same voice of earth and ancient authority, it declares that it is a guardian of the canyon, and that only those who serve Baelmat may pass beyond this threshold. The wind carries the scent of dry earth and something else, something older and more mysterious. You realise there is no alternative route, no way to continue your journey through the canyon without resolving this encounter.

choices.choiceText: State that you don't care what it is, get out of your way
Ask who Baelmat is
Declare that you are a servant of Baelmat

title: Declare yourself a servant of Baelmat
narrativeText: You proclaim boldly that you are indeed a servant of Baelmat, acting upon his direct orders. The guardian listens, then responds in its resonant voice that you bear no sigil and are not of Baelmat's making. You can hear the accusation in its tone, and the earthen mounds begin to shift more noticeably, small stones rolling down their sides. The guardian's posture changes subtly, and you sense that it is preparing for some form of confrontation. The canyon walls seem to close in around you as the wind picks up.

choices.choiceText: state that you dont actually care who Balemat is and order it to let you pass
Pull out the Medallion and claim you have the sigil

title: Ask who Baelmat is
narrativeText: You enquire about the identity of Baelmat. The guardian responds that Baelmat is the Lord of the inner mountain, and that if you were truly a servant, such knowledge would already be yours. The accusation hangs in the air between you like the canyon's perpetual whispers. The entity's stance becomes more rigid, and you can hear the subtle sounds of loose earth falling from the trembling mounds. Your options for peaceful passage appear to be diminishing with each passing moment.

choices.choiceText: Tell it you meant no disrespect and back away to consider your next move
State that you don't actually care who Balemat is and order it to let you pass

title: State that you don't care what it is get out of your way
narrativeText: You demand that the entity move aside regardless of its purpose or allegiance. The guardian's response is immediate and unsettling, its lips drawing back in what might be considered a smile. Its eyes seem to burn with an inner light, and when it speaks, there is anticipation in its voice as it asks whether you challenge it for the right of passage. The wind carries a new scent now, something metallic and sharp. The canyon itself seems to await your decision.

choices.choiceText: Engage the Guardian
Tell it you meant no disrespect and back away to consider your next move

title: Engage the Guardian
narrativeText: You accept the guardian's challenge and move to engage it in combat. The entity throws back its head and releases what can only be described as laughter, though the sound echoes strangely off the canyon walls. It begins to utter words in an ancient tongue, its head tilting skyward as the incantation grows louder. The ground beneath your feet begins to rumble, and suddenly thorned branches burst forth from each earthen mound. These twisted appendages writhe and twist towards you, their sharp points glinting wickedly. You realise this is not merely a battle against the guardian, but against the very canyon itself.

choices.choiceText: Dodge the branches and advance directly to the Guardian
Strike at the thorned branches with your sword

title: Tell it you meant no disrespect and back away to consider your next move
narrativeText: You offer your apologies for any perceived disrespect and take several cautious steps backwards to gain thinking space. The guardian appears unconcerned by your retreat and returns to its previous state of stillness, though its eyes remain fixed upon you. You have gained a brief respite, but the passage remains blocked, and you know that some decisive action will be required if you wish to continue your journey through the canyon. The whispers in the wind seem to grow more insistent during this temporary stalemate.

choices.choiceText: Check your provisions for useful items and engage the Guardian
Draw your sword and charge the guardian
This may be more than you can handle, retreat to the main road

title: State that you don't actually care who Baelmat is and order it to let you pass
narrativeText: You declare that you have no interest in Baelmat's identity and simply wish to continue your journey. The guardian's response mirrors what you witnessed before, its lips drawing back in that unsettling smile whilst its eyes begin to burn with malevolent light. It asks whether you challenge it for the right to passage, and you realise that your blunt approach has led to the same inevitable confrontation. The wind picks up around you, carrying dust from the trembling mounds.

choices.choiceText: Demand the guardian moves aside

title: Pull out the Medallion and claim you have the sigil
narrativeText: You withdraw the medallion from your belongings and display it prominently, claiming that this serves as your sigil. The guardian's reaction is immediate and violent, releasing a shriek that echoes off the canyon walls like the cry of some terrible bird. It declares that you bear the sign of the prison wardens, and its entire demeanour shifts to one of barely contained rage. The earthen mounds begin to shake more violently, and you can sense that the guardian is preparing for battle. The medallion grows warm against your palm.

choices.choiceText: Prepare for battle

title: Check your provisions for useful items and engage the Guardian
narrativeText: You take a moment to examine your belongings for anything that might prove useful in the coming confrontation. Searching through your pack, you discover that you carry lamp oil amongst your provisions, and you still possess the medallion given to you by the herb woman. You consider how these items might be employed against the guardian, knowing that a combination of resources may prove more effective than relying on your sword alone. The guardian watches your preparations with apparent interest.

choices.choiceText: Decide that neither will be of use and charge the Guardian, sword drawn
Pour oil on your blade and ignite it and enter combat
Wrap the medallion around the handle of your sword and enter into combat

title: Pour oil on your blade and ignite it and enter combat
narrativeText: You withdraw the lamp oil from your pack and carefully pour it along the length of your blade. Using your flint, you strike a spark and ignite the oil, causing flames to dance along the steel. You advance towards the guardian with your burning weapon held ready. The entity appears genuinely alarmed by the sight of your flaming blade and pulls back its head whilst beginning a new incantation. The mounds around it respond by erupting with thorned branches that writhe and advance towards you. The smell of burning oil mingles with the earthy scent of the canyon.

choices.choiceText: Strike at the branches with your burning blade
Avoid the branches and advance directly to the Guardian

title: Strike at the branches with your burning blade
narrativeText: You swing your burning blade at the approaching thorned branches with determined precision. The flames prove remarkably effective, igniting the thorny appendages upon contact and causing them to burn away as you methodically hack through their ranks. The fire spreads from branch to branch, consuming the guardian's defences and filling the air with the acrid smoke of burning thorns. Soon only the guardian itself remains, now bereft of its magical protections and vulnerable to direct attack.

choices.choiceText: Strike at the guardian
Pause to see what the guardian will do

title: Dodge the branches and advance directly to the Guardian
narrativeText: You dodge between the nearest thorned branches and sprint directly towards the guardian, hoping to end the confrontation swiftly. You manage to evade the first wave of attacks, but you can observe more branches gathering around you with each passing moment. The thorny appendages begin to close in, and you realise that if they manage to ensnare you, the consequences will likely prove fatal. You must choose between attempting to retreat to safety or pressing forward to attack the guardian directly, knowing that destroying it will end its enchantments.

choices.choiceText: Attempt to retreat from this nightmare
Press the attack and hack a path to the Guardian, killing it will end it's enchantment

title: Strike at the guardian
narrativeText: You charge the now-defenceless guardian before it can recover or begin a new incantation. Your burning blade finds its mark, striking through the entity's heart with a sound like metal piercing stone. The guardian bursts into brilliant flames and begins to wither, releasing a final scream that echoes through the canyon before crumbling to ash and dust. The threat has been eliminated, and the thorned branches collapse lifelessly to the ground. The canyon falls silent except for the gentle whisper of wind through stone.

choices.choiceText: Rest while basking in the warmth of a convenient camp fire before searching the area
Continue up the canyon

title: Pause to see what the guardian will do
narrativeText: You hesitate for one fatal moment, seeking to understand the guardian's strategy rather than seizing your fleeting chance at victory. The entity's chanting transforms into something far more sinister, its voice dropping to a guttural growl that seems to emanate from the very stones of the canyon. With terrifying swiftness, it thrusts both arms forward, fingers splayed like the talons of some monstrous bird. Thorned tendrils erupt from the earth around you with the violence of a spring flood, their barbed lengths whipping through the air faster than you can follow. They coil around your limbs with crushing force, their razor-sharp points tearing through cloth and flesh alike. You struggle desperately, but each movement only drives the thorns deeper, and more tendrils emerge to bind you ever tighter. The metallic taste of blood fills your mouth as the thorns find your throat. Your vision darkens as the guardian's triumphant laughter echoes off the canyon walls, and you realise with growing horror that your moment of caution has become your final mistake. The thorns constrict like living chains, and your last coherent thought is the bitter recognition of opportunity lost forever.

title: Strike at the thorned branches with your sword
narrativeText: You hurl yourself at the writhing mass of thorns with desperate fury, your blade singing as it cuts through the air in savage arcs. Yet even as you hack at the branches before you, the guardian's trap reveals its true cunning. Thorned roots have been silently threading through the earth beneath your feet, and now they burst upward like the fingers of buried corpses seeking the living. Your boot catches in this hidden snare, and you crash forward into the very horror you sought to destroy. Instantly, a dozen thorned appendages seize upon your fall, wrapping around your arms and legs with the relentless hunger of serpents. The thorns bite deep into your flesh, each barb designed by nature's cruelest intent. You thrash wildly, but every movement tears new wounds, and your sword tumbles from blood-slicked fingers to clatter uselessly on the stone. More branches emerge from the mounds, winding around your chest and throat with methodical precision. Your breath comes in ragged gasps as the thorns tighten their embrace, and through the haze of pain, you hear the guardian's voice speaking words of ancient mockery. The canyon walls seem to close in around you as your strength ebbs away, and in your final moments, you understand the true futility of challenging powers beyond mortal comprehension.

title: Attempt to retreat from this nightmare
narrativeText: You leap backwards, managing to reach a clearer area beyond the immediate reach of the thorned branches. You continue to sprint away from the writhing mass of deadly appendages, your heart racing as terror drives your flight. Having gained distance from the immediate danger, you pause to catch your breath and assess your situation. Though you have escaped the thorns, the guardian still poses a threat, and you must decide whether to attempt a different approach or abandon this route entirely.

choices.choiceText: Take lamp oil from your backpack and use it on your blade to add fire
Free of the writhing mass of doom decide that the well worn road was the better option

title: Avoid the branches and advance directly to the Guardian
narrativeText: You avoid the nearest branches and sprint to finish the guardian. However, the mass of thorns lies thickly now between the guardian and yourself. The thorns snag your clothing and bring you down heavily. You have only one hope left, as the guardian prepares to finish you. You must strike at the thorned branches with your sword.

choices.choiceText: Strike at the thorned branches with your sword

title: Rest while basking in the warmth of a convenient camp fire before searching the area
narrativeText: You take time to rest beside the warmth of a convenient campfire that is the guardians burning body, allowing yourself to recover from the recent confrontation. As you search the immediate area, you discover a small ornate wooden box protruding from one of the earthen mounds. The box appears distinctly out of place in this desolate canyon setting, and its presence stirs your curiosity. The firelight dances across its carved surface, revealing intricate patterns that seem to shift in the flickering glow.

choices.choiceText: Examine the box
Decide that some things are best left alone and continue the journey

title: Continue up the canyon
narrativeText: The canyon path continues its winding course towards Murlore's Spike, narrow but passable for a single traveller. After several hours of steady progress, darkness begins to gather in the canyon depths, and you establish a simple camp for the night. You awaken the following morning refreshed and undisturbed, ready to resume your journey. The air has grown noticeably colder and thinner, and you can sense the imposing presence of the mountain looming somewhere ahead, though the canyon walls still block your view of its peaks.

choices.choiceText: Break camp and head on to the mountain

title: Examine the box
narrativeText: You examine the ornate box carefully, checking for any signs of traps or magical wards before opening it. The box appears to be crafted from simple wood despite its elaborate decoration, and you detect no obvious dangers. Inside, you discover a small stone tablet bearing an arcane inscription in an unfamiliar script. Initially, you judge it to have little value, but when you turn the tablet over, you find an inscribed illustration of Murlore's Spike. The image clearly depicts three distinct levels: a lower level, a middle section, and a smaller upper level. You place the tablet in your pack, recognising it as a potentially valuable map of the mountain's interior.

choices.choiceText: Pocket the tablet and proceed on your journey.

title: Set up a temporary camp and rest for a while
narrativeText:
You decide that the sheltered cave provides an ideal opportunity to rest and recover your strength before continuing the arduous journey ahead. Using the dry areas deeper within the cave, you establish a small but comfortable temporary camp, spreading your bedroll on the sandy floor and arranging your equipment where it will stay dry.

The constant sound of dripping water and the muffled roar of the waterfall outside create a surprisingly soothing atmosphere as you settle down to rest. The cave's protection from wind and weather makes it feel almost cosy, despite its sparse surroundings.

However, your peaceful slumber is interrupted when you wake abruptly in the dim cave environment. A large wolf stands silently near the cave entrance, its powerful form outlined against the faint light filtering through the waterfall's spray. The creature's eyes gleam with reflected light as it regards you with calm intelligence.

The wolf displays no immediate signs of aggression or hostility, but its presence fills the confined space with an aura of wild, unpredictable danger. Its thick fur appears wet from the cave's moisture, and you can hear its steady breathing even over the ambient sounds of dripping water. This is clearly a fully grown, experienced predator that has survived many seasons in these harsh mountain conditions.

choices.choiceText: Stay calm and observe
Draw your Sword and attack it
Draw your sword but remain ready to defend

title: Stay calm and observe
narrativeText:
You remain perfectly still, your breathing steady and controlled. The wolf's amber eyes reflect the dim light filtering through the cave entrance, studying you with intelligent wariness. Its thick grey coat bristles slightly at the shoulders, yet its stance shows no aggression, merely curiosity.

The steady drip of water echoes around the stone chamber behind you, punctuating the tense silence. The beast's ears twitch at each droplet's fall, but its gaze never wavers from your face. You recognise this moment of mutual assessment, understanding that your next action will determine whether this encounter ends in violence or peaceful resolution.

choices.choiceText: Give it some of your provisions as a gesture of no threat
Draw your sword and shout at it to make it go away

title: Draw your Sword and attack it
narrativeText:
Without hesitation, you draw your sword and launch an immediate attack upon the wolf. However, your hasty aggression proves to be a fatal miscalculation. This creature has survived countless hunts and territorial disputes in these unforgiving mountains, and it recognises the threat you represent with lightning-fast instincts.

The wolf reacts to your attack with well-practiced, predatory movement that speaks of years spent as both hunter and hunted. Before you can properly ready yourself for combat or establish a defensive stance, the powerful animal leaps directly at you with explosive speed and precision.

The wolf's powerful jaws close around your neck before you can bring your weapon to bear effectively. As consciousness fades and life ebbs away, your final realisation is that patience and wisdom might have served you better than rash violence. In these wild places, hasty decisions often prove to be the hardest and most final lessons of all.

title: Draw your sword but remain ready to defend
narrativeText:
Your blade slides from its scabbard with a whispered ring of steel, the sound sharp against the cave's stone walls. You position yourself in a defensive stance, weight balanced on the balls of your feet, ready to react rather than attack.

The wolf's ears flatten momentarily at the metallic sound, its lips curling to reveal white fangs in a warning growl. The low rumble reverberates through the damp chamber, mixing with the endless percussion of water droplets above. Yet the beast reads your posture correctly, recognising preparation rather than threat.

After several heartbeats of tense consideration, it turns its broad head away, pads silently towards the cave entrance, and disappears into the misty spray beyond the waterfall.

choices.choiceText: Having avoided unnecessary conflict pack your things and be on your journey.

title: Give it some of your provisions as a gesture of no threat
narrativeText:
You reach slowly into your pack, your movements deliberate and unthreatening. The dried meat tears easily between your fingers, releasing its smoky aroma into the cool cave air. You toss the portion gently across the stone floor, where it lands with a soft thud several paces from the wolf's position.

The creature's black nose twitches as the scent reaches it, head lowering to investigate your offering. It sniffs the meat thoroughly before taking it delicately between its teeth, tail giving a single, measured wag. A soft woof echoes through the chamber, almost conversational in tone, before the wolf retreats toward the entrance.

Its departure is unhurried, dignified, leaving you alone with the knowledge that wisdom has prevented unnecessary bloodshed.

choices.choiceText: Having avoided unnecessary conflict pack your things and be on your journey.

title: Draw your sword and shout at it to make it go away
narrativeText:
Your blade clears its sheath in one swift motion, the steel singing as it cuts through the damp air. Your voice booms through the stone chamber, harsh and commanding, demanding the beast's retreat. The sound crashes against the cave walls, multiplying into echoes that merge with the constant drip of water from above.

The wolf's golden eyes narrow, ears pinning back against its skull as a deep growl rises from its chest. Yet your display of dominance achieves its purpose. The creature backs away slowly, never turning its back to you, maintaining eye contact until it reaches the cave's threshold.

Only then does it pivot and disappear into the mist beyond, leaving you victorious but alone in the echoing silence.

choices.choiceText: Having avoided unnecessary conflict pack your things and be on your journey.

title: Politely ask the woman about the mountains.
narrativeText: You approach the herb woman with courteous respect and politely inquire about the mountains you seek. Her smile deepens, creating gentle furrows across her time-worn face, whilst her perceptive eyes continue to study you with growing intensity. The morning air around the cottage seems to hold its breath as she speaks. 'The spirits of the hills, they told me,' she says with quiet certainty, her voice carrying both wonder and solemnity, 'you may be the one.' Her words drift through the misty air like an ancient prophecy finally spoken aloud. She speaks candidly of perils that lie ahead, dangers that even someone of your apparent capability may struggle to avoid, yet she expresses cautious hope that you might indeed be destined to end the terrible blight that has settled upon this land like a curse. From beneath her earth-stained apron, she draws forth a medallion that radiates comforting warmth, its polished surface catching what little light filters through the mist. 'For when darkness may be upon you,' she intones with grave purpose, placing the precious object into your outstretched palm, 'for an ending that may not be your own, choose wisely.' The medallion's gentle heat seeps through your skin, carrying with it a sense of protection and ancient power.

choices.choiceText: Ask about the charms secret
politely press her on knowledge of the mountain

title: Tell him you are going to Murlore's Spike
narrativeText: You tell the man plainly that you are heading towards Murlore's Spike. His grin widens noticeably, revealing yellowed teeth stained by years of rough living, and he releases a low chuckle that carries a mocking edge. The sound of his amusement mingles with the crackling of the campfire between you. Another adventurer to their doom, he says, the words delivered with the casual tone of someone who has witnessed this pattern many times before. His voice drops slightly as he makes his proposition. For a small coin, he explains, he and his silent companion will guide you to the very edge of the treacherous swamp that must be crossed to reach the mountain, but they will venture no further than that boundary. The way he phrases this makes it clear that the swamp represents a barrier even these hardened men will not cross. You understand this is a chance to acquire guides who know the terrain, men who can show you the proper entry point into dangerous territory. However, the weight of the morning air around the campfire feels heavy with unspoken intentions. The silence of his companion, who has not uttered a single word since you arrived, adds an unnerving quality to the encounter. The second man's hand rests with casual readiness near the worn hilt of a blade at his belt, his eyes watching your every movement with the fixed attention of a predator assessing potential prey. The smoke from the campfire drifts between you, carrying the smell of burning wood and something else, something less pleasant that you cannot quite identify. Behind you, the main road stretches onwards through increasingly dense forest, whilst ahead, somewhere beyond the smoke and the watching men, lies the path to Murlore's Spike and whatever fortune or doom awaits there.

choices.choiceText: Agree and pay him the coin
State that you dont need protection

title: Tell him you have no need for protection and travel on
narrativeText: You inform the man that you have no need for his protection and continue walking. He simply smiles in response, a chilling, predatory expression on his face. 'Going to Murlore's Spike then, eh?' he says, his voice low and knowing. 'You'll still need to know where to enter the swamp, and that requires a guide.' He then returns to his companion, and they both follow your progress with a fixed gaze as you continue on your way. The sound of their muffled conversation and the crackling of the campfire fade behind you as the road winds onward.

choices.choiceText: Continue on your journey
Agree to pay

title: Agree and pay him the coin
narrativeText: You agree to his terms and hand the man a coin. He and his companion fall in beside you, and for hours, you walk in an uneasy silence as the distant, forbidding silhouette of Murlore's Spike grows closer. The forest around you begins to darken, and the air becomes thick with the scent of damp earth and decay. The ground to your left grows darker and damper, giving way to the treacherous, low-lying ground of the swamp. The men halt and the first man points toward a narrow gap in the trees, marked by two large, moss-covered stones. He advises you to enter there, stating that a path exists, but warning you not to stray from it under any circumstances.

choices.choiceText: Thank them for their time and enter the swamp
Thank them and set a fire and prepare before entering

title: Continue on your journey
narrativeText: You choose to continue on your journey. The man returns to his companion, and they both watch your departure with a chilling intensity as you proceed along the road. The road begins to wind, taking you deeper into the forest, where the light is dim and shadows are long. After a time, you become aware of a distinct rhythmic sound behind you: footsteps following you. The sound is not loud, but it is steady and deliberate, and the hairs on the back of your neck begin to rise. You must decide whether to turn and confront whoever is following you or to quickly hide and wait for them to pass.

choices.choiceText: Turn and see who it is
Round an approaching bend and hide

title: Turn and see who it is
narrativeText: You spin around, your hand already on your weapon. The two men from the campfire are right behind you. Their movements are quick and predatory, and they hold a short, curved sword and a long, serrated dagger, both weapons drawn and ready. Their intent is unmistakable, and you realise with a sinking feeling that you have been lured into a trap. The road is narrow, hemmed in by dense trees and rocky outcrops, offering no room for escape. You are forced to prepare for a desperate fight for your life.

choices.choiceText: Face them where you stand
Attempt to outrun them

title: Round an approaching bend and hide
narrativeText: You quickly round a bend in the road and dive into the thick, dense foliage, holding your breath and remaining absolutely still. The air is heavy with the scent of pine needles and damp earth. The two men round the bend moments later, their voices hushed as they search for you. They peer into the dark shadows where you are concealed but do not spot you. Frustrated, one of them shouts, "'You aren't coming back anyway, we would have saved you the time!'" before they retreat back the way they came. You wait until the sound of their footsteps fades into the distance and emerge, continuing along the road, uncertain of how you will find your way to Murlore's Spike. As you walk, the road gives way to a small break in the trees to your left, marked by two large, rounded boulders. Peering in, you see what appears to be a path leading into a dark, foreboding swamp in the direction of the mountain.

choices.choiceText: Enter the path through the swamp

title: Face them where you stand
narrativeText: You turn to face the two men, ready for a fight. They are clearly prepared for this encounter and have done this many times before. They quickly split, moving to your left and right, and draw daggers. Their intent is unmistakable. In a coordinated move, they both hurl their weapons at you. You manage to fend one away, but the other strikes you squarely in the chest. A sharp, searing pain radiates from the wound as you drop to your knees. They quickly close the distance, pulling small, weighted coshes from hidden pockets. They strike you repeatedly, their blows falling with cold, dispassionate efficiency until you fall, stunned and dying. As darkness begins to claim you, you feel their hands rummaging through your pockets, pulling the few valuables you possess from your belt and pack. You hear one of them say, "'We've just saved you a journey before the inevitable.'" Your final moments are filled with the metallic tang of blood in your mouth and the fading sensation of the cold, hard earth pressing against your cheek. The sound of their footsteps retreating is the last thing you hear before a deep, profound silence falls over you, a silence that will last for all eternity.

title: Attempt to outrun them
narrativeText: You turn and sprint, attempting to outrun the two men. However, weighed down by your heavy adventuring gear and with the uneven ground beneath your feet, you quickly realise that outrunning them is impossible. You round a bend in the road, and for a fleeting moment, are out of their sight, but you know they will be on you in moments. You must decide whether to dive for cover and hide in the tangled undergrowth, hoping to evade them, or to draw your sword and face them, preparing for the inevitable confrontation.

choices.choiceText: Dive for cover and hide
Draw your sword and face them

title: Thank them and set a fire before entering
narrativeText: You thank the men and begin to gather kindling and dry leaves to prepare a fire, a common ritual before entering dangerous territory. The scent of pine and moss fills the air. It quickly becomes clear that they are not intending to leave. You look at them, and one gives you a chillingly knowing look, saying, "'You're not coming back anyway.'" They both draw daggers and begin to advance, their intentions now completely clear. You must decide whether to draw your sword and prepare to defend yourself on solid ground or to retreat into the swamp, hoping that the treacherous terrain will allow you to lose them.

choices.choiceText: Draw your Sword and prepare to defend
Retreat into the swamp hoping to lose them

title: Thank them for their time and enter the swamp
narrativeText: You offer a polite word of thanks to the men and step onto the path leading into the swamp. The air immediately grows heavy and thick with the smell of stagnant water and decaying vegetation. As you glance back, you see them standing at the entrance, their figures silhouetted against the trees, watching you disappear into the dense, muddy foliage. It becomes clear that they are not going to follow you into this cursed and dangerous place. A deep feeling of unease settles over you as you continue further into the swamp, knowing that you must now traverse this perilous land on your own.

choices.choiceText: Continue further into the swamp

title: Enter the path through the swamp
narrativeText: You push through the gap between the rounded boulders and commit yourself to this treacherous route. The path beneath your feet becomes increasingly soft and treacherous, each step releasing the rich, organic smell of decomposing vegetation mixed with stagnant water. Twisted roots snake across the ground, threatening to catch your boots, whilst overhead, moss-draped branches form a canopy that filters the light into an eerie green twilight. The air grows thick and humid, clinging to your skin like a damp shroud. After some time, you encounter a massive gnarled tree stump, its surface blackened and twisted by age and decay. The wood creaks softly in the humid air, and you can hear the gentle plop of water droplets falling from the overhanging branches into unseen pools. It is then that you hear it, a woman's voice crying out from somewhere to your left, deeper into the swamp's treacherous embrace. The sound cuts through the oppressive atmosphere like a blade, filled with such raw anguish and desperation that it sends a chill down your spine despite the swamp's warmth. The cry echoes briefly before being swallowed by the dense vegetation, leaving only the sound of your own breathing and the distant croaking of unseen amphibians.

choices.choiceText: Call out
Ignore the sound and continue on the path

title: Call out
narrativeText: You call out to the young woman. The cry in response becomes more desperate, a series of short, frantic sobs, but there is no distinct reply. The oppressive atmosphere of the swamp seems to press in around you as you realise the sound is coming from off the main path, deeper into the clinging mud and tangled roots. A sense of unease tightens in your gut; you instinctively feel that it may well be a trap. The anguished sounds are almost too much to bear, and you are faced with a difficult decision: to venture off the questionable safety of the path to investigate or to steel yourself and continue your journey.

choices.choiceText: The sound is off the track into the swamp, you figure it is a trap, continue on your journey
The anguished sounds are too much venture to investigate

title: Ignore the sound and continue on the path
narrativeText: You continue on the path through the swamp, leaving the memories of the girls cries behind you. The air grows heavier and the light dimmer as you proceed deeper into the swamp's heart. Twisted shadows seem to writhe and dance in the dense trees, and unsettling, half-heard sounds echo all around you, carried on the humid breeze. The path eventually leads you out of the swamp's gloom and to the base of Murlore's Spike, a dark and imposing mountain with steep, sheer sides and a complete lack of vegetation. A profound sense of dread settles over you as you stand before the mountain's cold, forbidding presence. Two paths lead from here, winding around the base. The path to the left is well-trodden and appears to have been used recently, whilst the path to the right looks far less used, rugged, and dangerous.

choices.choiceText: Take the left path
Take the right path

title: It's too much to bear go and investigate
narrativeText: The anguished cries are too much to bear, so you plunge off the path and into the swamp's embrace to investigate. The initially shallow, muddy ground quickly deepens, making your progress slow and laborious as thick, clinging muck grasps at your legs. The air hangs thick with the scent of stagnant water and decay. You finally arrive at a small patch of slightly firmer ground, a small clearing where a figure of a young girl is chained to a gnarled, moss-covered tree. Around this small island of solid ground, the swamp becomes a viscous, clinging quagmire, making a direct approach seem treacherous. Two distinct paths lead toward her. To the left, a path appears well-trodden and recently used, while to the right, a less-used path winds through denser foliage. The girl’s head hangs low, and her frail body is wracked with soft sobbing noises. She appears pale and utterly exhausted, looking very close to death.

choices.choiceText: She needs immediate help
Call out to her
Look to see if there is a safe way to her

title: Head to her to help
narrativeText: You advance to help her, but with each step, the swamp grows more glutinous and clinging, making your movements increasingly difficult. As you close within a few feet, the girl's head slowly rises with a sickening creak of bone. It's then that you see the horrifying truth: she has been a desiccated corpse for a very long time. A jewelled band gleams on her skull, its stones catching the murky light. Her mouth opens, and an overpowering stench of rot and decay assaults your senses, causing a wave of extreme nausea that leaves you dizzy and disoriented. A sharp, piercing pain shoots through your head, and a profound sense of confusion takes hold, making it difficult to think clearly as the treacherous mud tightens its grip on you.

choices.choiceText: Use your shirt to mask the smell and try and retreat
Hurl your sword as a spear at the creature to finish it

title: Call out to her
narrativeText: You call out to her, and her sobbing weakens. Her breathing becomes increasingly shallow and laboured, and you realise her life is fading fast. However, you also know that rushing in blindly would be foolish and could lead to your own demise. You must decide whether to act on impulse and help her immediately, or to take a moment to determine a safe path to her.

choices.choiceText: There is no more time help her immediately
Aware her life is at risk it would be no use dying yourself, determine a safe path to her.

title: Look to see if there is a safe way to her
narrativeText: Using your better judgement, you take a moment to assess the situation and find the best route to the girl. Straight ahead, the swamp becomes a thick, dangerous mire. Circling to the right offers little improvement, though some low-hanging branches might provide a handhold. To the left, however, you notice some slightly raised, drier ground that might be passable. Her life is leaving her rapidly, and you must make a decision before it is too late.

choices.choiceText: You have to get to her, go straight
Circle left and approach from the drier area
Circle right and use the branches as a hand rail

title: Use your shirt to mask the smell
narrativeText: You grab the hem of your shirt and pull it up to your face to block the overwhelming stench. In the process, your hand grasps the medallion, lifting it into the air. Immediately, the waves of pain and confusion lift, replaced by a sudden, sharp clarity. With a renewed sense of purpose, you draw your sword with the intention of using it as leverage to assist your movement through the clinging muck.

choices.choiceText: Continue forward and end this creatures life
Retreat from the creature

title: Hurl your sword as a spear at the creature to finish it
narrativeText: In a last-ditch effort, you hurl your sword as if it were a spear. It strikes the creature in the chest with a dull thud, but its desiccated body barely reacts. The creature was already dead. The powerful, unseen force that controls it is unaffected, and you feel the last wisps of hope leave you. This is the last desperate action of a mind overwhelmed by panic, and you realise that your misplaced effort has left you without a weapon and at the mercy of whatever ancient power dwells within the swamp.

choices.choiceText: The last breath

title: Continue forward and end this creatures life
narrativeText: With grim determination, you force yourself through the final few steps, your boots squelching in the adhesive swamp mud with each laboured movement. The putrid stench that emanates from the corpse fills your nostrils, but you steel yourself against the overwhelming urge to retch. Drawing your sword with both hands, you raise it high above the kneeling figure and bring it down in a clean, decisive arc. The blade severs the desiccated neck with a sound like breaking parchment, and the head tumbles away into the dark water with a soft splash. As it falls, the ornate metallic band encircling the skull strikes a half-submerged root and shatters with a crystalline chime that echoes across the silent swamp. The moment the arcane symbols on the band break apart, the overwhelming stench that has been poisoning the air vanishes completely, replaced by the cleaner scents of moss and standing water. In that instant of profound silence, you hear something that makes your blood run cold, then warm with recognition. A young woman's voice, clear and achingly grateful, whispers words of desperate relief, speaking of freedom from an enforced task that has held her spirit captive for untold years. Her voice seems to rise from the very air around you, filled with such profound gratitude that it brings moisture to your eyes. Then, as suddenly as it came, the voice fades, and a deep, peaceful silence settles over the swamp, broken only by the gentle lapping of water against the exposed roots and the distant call of a night bird welcoming the return of natural order.

choices.choiceText: Retrace your steps back to the main path

title: Retreat from the creature
narrativeText: You successfully pull yourself away from the horror in front of you, the swamp's grip weakening as you retreat. You quickly retrace your steps back to the main path, your mind reeling at the sheer malevolence that could have created such a dismal thing. You are shaken but alive, and you continue on your journey.

choices.choiceText: Retrace your steps back to the main path

title: Circle left and approach from the drier area
narrativeText: You move in a wide arc to your left, seeking the firmer ground that appears to rise slightly above the surrounding bog. Your boots find purchase on what seems to be a small ridge of packed earth and decomposed vegetation, and you can hear the reassuring crunch of drier material beneath your feet rather than the ominous squelching that dominates the rest of the swamp. Moving with careful deliberation, you advance towards the kneeling figure, using fallen branches and exposed roots to test each step before committing your full weight. As you draw closer, perhaps an arm's length away, you gain a clearer view from this new angle. The girl's long, matted hair cascades forward like a dark curtain, concealing her face from anyone approaching directly ahead, but from this side position, the terrible truth becomes unmistakable. What you initially mistook for a living person is, in fact, a corpse in an advanced state of preservation. Her skin has the grey-brown hue of ancient leather, stretched taut over prominent bones, and you can see that she has been dead for a considerable time, perhaps years or even decades. Encircling her skull, a metallic band set with dark jewels catches what little light filters through the swamp's canopy, its arcane symbols etched deep into the tarnished surface. As you watch in growing horror, her desiccated head begins to lift with the slow, deliberate movement of something that should not move at all.

choices.choiceText: Retreat immediately from the horror
Draw your sword and charge the last few feet to end her

title: Circle right and use the branches as a hand rail
narrativeText: You circle to the right, using the branches of gnarled trees to steady yourself through the ever-deepening swamp. As you close within a few feet, your movements quickly become impossible. The girl's head rises, and it is horrifyingly clear that she has been a desiccated corpse for a very long time. A jewelled band gleams on her head, and her mouth opens, releasing an overpowering stench of rot and decay. The stench causes you to gag, and you release your grip on the branch. Feeling extreme nausea, you collapse, and your arms sink into the thick, adhesive swamp mud, which pulls you down relentlessly.

choices.choiceText: The last breath

title: The last breath
narrativeText: The putrid miasma that flows from the undead creature overwhelms every sense you possess, filling your nostrils with the concentrated essence of decay and corruption. The stench is so powerful that it seems to have physical weight, pressing down upon you like a suffocating blanket woven from the very essence of death itself. Your throat closes involuntarily, and violent retching leaves you gasping desperately for clean air that simply does not exist in this accursed place. Your limbs grow heavy and unresponsive, as though the toxic vapours are seeping into your very bones and poisoning your strength from within. You try to lift your arms, to push yourself away from this nightmare, but they respond with agonising slowness, and each movement requires tremendous effort that you no longer possess. The world begins to spin around you as consciousness slips away like water through your fingers, and you feel your body becoming an unbearable weight that you can no longer support. You collapse forward into the treacherous embrace of the swamp, and the adhesive mud welcomes your still form with a wet, sucking sound that echoes briefly across the silent waters. As your vision darkens and your final breath escapes in a weak sigh, you sink slowly into the ancient quagmire that has claimed so many before you. The swamp closes over your head with barely a ripple, and the oppressive silence that follows is broken only by the distant croaking of unseen creatures who have witnessed yet another soul lost to the hungry depths.

title: Take the left path
narrativeText: You choose the leftward path that winds around the mountain's base. Your footing remains secure on this well-trodden route, and you make steady progress along the packed earth trail. After walking approximately three hundred paces, the path curves inward, and a natural gorge opens in the mountainside to your right. The gorge extends perhaps twenty feet into the rock face, not particularly deep but clearly artificial in its precise edges. At the far end of this stone cleft, a magnificent gateway has been carved directly into the living rock of Murlore's Spike itself. The centrepiece is an enormous door constructed from dark ironwood, its surface smooth and unmarked by the elements. The door stands firmly closed, sealed tight within its stone frame. Both the door's surface and the surrounding rock face bear intricate carvings, arcane symbols that seem to have been etched with masterful precision. The symbols catch and hold shadows in their depths, creating patterns that shift subtly as you move your position.

choices.choiceText: Approach and see if you can open the doorway
Approach and examine the symbols

title: Take the right path
narrativeText: You choose the rightward path, but your progress soon becomes laborious as the route narrows considerably. Loose stones shift beneath your feet with each step, and larger boulders force you to squeeze between them or climb over their weathered surfaces. As you advance further along this treacherous route, the unmistakable shapes of old animal carcasses begin to appear scattered along the path. The air grows thick with the stench of death and decay, a smell so potent it catches in your throat. Eventually, you reach a sheer cliff face that blocks any further progress. High above, perhaps thirty feet up the rock wall, a dark cave mouth yawns open. The accumulated bones and carcasses below, combined with the overwhelming reek of decomposition, make it clear that this cave served as a waste chute. Among the bleached animal bones, you can discern the unmistakable shapes of human remains. The cliff face offers no handholds or footholds that would make climbing feasible, and the cave entrance remains impossibly out of reach. This path leads nowhere.

choices.choiceText: Return to the other path.

title: Approach and see if you can open the doorway
narrativeText: You approach the heavy Ironwood door. It is closed and sealed tightly against the rock, with no handle or latch visible on its dark, unyielding surface. There is no obvious way of opening it. You consider your options: turn back to see if the other path might have offered an alternative entry, or examine the arcane symbols carved into the door and surrounding rock for clues that might reveal a solution.

choices.choiceText: Return and see if the left path offered a way in
Examine the symbols on the door to see if there are any clues

title: Approach and examine the symbols
narrativeText: You step closer to examine the symbols. Those carved into the stone frame around the doorway depict images of death and strife, with a large keystone showing a powerful glyph hanging menacingly above the land. The symbols carved into the doorway itself are smaller but more intricate. There is a hand wrapped in thorns, a serpent with a body made of thorns, and a fire with billowing smoke that looks like thorns. Above all of these is a single, unsettling eye with no pupil, just an empty hole. As you reach out to touch one of the symbols, it glows with a faint green light, beckoning your touch.

choices.choiceText: Press the serpent
Press the hand
Press the flame

title: Press the serpent
narrativeText: You press your palm against the serpent symbol carved into the door. The moment your skin makes contact with the stone, a ghastly screech tears through the gorge, echoing off the canyon walls with unnatural intensity. The ground beneath your feet begins to tremble, and you hear the distinctive sound of earth being displaced. Serpents emerge from the rocky soil around you, their scales glinting as they rise from hidden burrows. The creatures form a loose circle, their heads swaying hypnotically as they focus their attention upon you. Their hissing fills the air with a threatening chorus.

choices.choiceText: Draw your sword and defend yourself
Evade the snakes and try for an easier access

title: Press the hand
narrativeText: You place your hand firmly against the thorned hand symbol. A bone-chilling screech reverberates through the gorge, and you watch in horror as the carved hand tears itself free from the door's surface. The stone appendage lunges forward and clamps around your throat, its thorned bindings digging through your skin like living barbs. You can feel the sharp points piercing flesh, drawing blood as they tighten their grip. Your breathing becomes laboured as the supernatural hand's strength far exceeds that of any mortal grasp.

choices.choiceText: Pull away from the hand
Prise the hand off your neck
Use your sword to cut the fingers away

title: Press the flame
narrativeText: You press your palm against the flame symbol carved into the door. The carving immediately begins to glow with warm orange light, as though actual fire had been kindled within the stone itself. The flame symbol maintains its radiance, casting flickering shadows across the other symbols on the door. The warmth radiating from the carved flame reaches your face, a welcome contrast to the cool mountain air. This seems to be a positive response, suggesting that the flame symbol was the correct initial choice in whatever sequence the door requires.

choices.choiceText: press the serpent
Press the hand

title: Draw your sword and defend yourself
narrativeText: You draw your sword with swift, practised movements. Though the serpents initially caught you off guard, your rapid response allows you to regain control of the situation. Your blade finds its mark repeatedly as you strike with precision, each cut dispatching another attacker. You move with calculated efficiency, stepping aside from lunging fangs whilst delivering fatal blows in return. The metallic ring of steel echoes through the gorge as you methodically eliminate each serpent. Soon, the rocky ground is littered with their motionless forms, and the immediate threat has passed.

choices.choiceText: Go back and try again
Decide that you don't want to risk this again and see if there is another way in

title: Evade the snakes and try for an easier access
narrativeText: Startled by the sudden appearance of the serpents, you attempt to evade their attacks and retreat to safety. Your reflexes prove woefully insufficient against creatures perfectly adapted to this rocky terrain. The first serpent's fangs sink deep into your leg, and you can feel the burning venom immediately begin its work, setting your nerves ablaze with liquid fire. Your leg becomes a useless weight within moments, sending you crashing onto the jagged stones. As you struggle to rise, more serpents converge upon your helpless form. Fangs puncture your arms, your torso, your neck, each bite delivering fresh agony as different venoms mingle in your bloodstream. Your vision blurs as the toxins attack your nervous system, whilst your muscles convulse uncontrollably. You can taste copper and bile as blood fills your mouth. The serpents continue their methodical work, striking repeatedly until your body ceases its futile struggles. Your final moments are spent in excruciating paralysis, conscious but unable to move or cry out, as the creatures slowly consume what remains of your life.

title: Go back and try again
narrativeText: You return to the door to attempt the symbol sequence once more. The four arcane carvings remain as before, each waiting to respond to your touch. The thorned hand rests motionless in its carved recess. The serpent symbol appears unchanged from its original state. The flame carving sits ready to be activated again. Above them all, the empty-socketed eye continues to stare out from the door's surface, its carved void seeming to watch your every movement.

choices.choiceText: Press the serpent
Press the hand
Press the flame

title: Pull away from the hand
narrativeText: You attempt to wrench yourself backwards, away from the grasping stone hand. The supernatural appendage's grip proves overwhelmingly powerful, and your desperate struggle only serves to drive the thorns deeper into the tender flesh of your throat. You can feel each individual barb tearing through muscle and sinew as the appendage tightens its hold with methodical precision. Warm blood flows freely down your neck and chest as the thorns burrow ever deeper. Your windpipe begins to collapse under the relentless pressure, reducing your breathing to desperate, gurgling gasps. The pain is excruciating as tendons snap one by one, whilst the thorns continue their savage work, seeking vital arteries beneath your skin. You taste the metallic flood of blood filling your mouth as internal damage mounts. Your vision darkens at the edges as oxygen starvation takes hold, but consciousness remains cruelly intact. The final moments stretch into an eternity of agony as you feel your throat being systematically crushed, vertebrae grinding against each other before the inevitable, sickening crack signals the end of your torment.

title: Prise the hand off your neck
narrativeText: You attempt to work your fingers between the stone hand and your throat, trying to prise the supernatural grip loose. The appendage possesses strength far beyond that of living flesh, and your efforts seem futile against its crushing power. The thorns embedded in your neck make any movement agonising, threatening to tear deeper if you struggle too vigorously. Despite the overwhelming odds, you detect a slight loosening in the grip, suggesting that persistence might yet yield results.

choices.choiceText: Continue to prise the hand off
Draw your sword and cut the fingers away

title: Use your sword to cut the fingers away
narrativeText: Summoning what strength remains to you, you draw your sword and hack desperately at the stone fingers clamped around your throat. Your blade strikes true, chipping away chunks of animated stone until the supernatural hand's grip finally fails. The severed digits crumble to dust, and the remaining portion of the hand retreats rapidly back into the door's surface, leaving only smooth carving behind. You collapse to your knees, gasping for air as blood trickles from the thorn wounds on your neck.

choices.choiceText: Try Again
This is too dangerous try and find another way in

title: Continue to prise the hand off
narrativeText: You continue your desperate efforts to prise the stone hand from your neck, but the slight progress you achieved earlier proves woefully insufficient against the supernatural force crushing your windpipe. Your breathing deteriorates from laboured gasps to barely perceptible wheezes as your strength abandons you with each passing moment. The thorns embedded in your throat work deeper with every futile movement, creating fresh channels of agony as they slice through increasingly vital structures. You can feel your pulse hammering frantically against the constricting grip, each heartbeat driving more blood through the puncture wounds. The metallic taste of your own blood fills your mouth as internal bleeding intensifies. Your vision begins to fragment into dancing spots of light and shadow as oxygen deprivation ravages your brain. Still, mercilessly, consciousness clings to you, ensuring you experience every excruciating detail as the grip tightens incrementally. You feel cartilage beginning to buckle and compress, the delicate architecture of your throat collapsing piece by agonising piece. Your final coherent sensation is the grinding of bone against bone before the vertebrae finally surrender with a wet, splintering crack that echoes through your skull as life departs your broken form.

title: press the serpent
narrativeText: You press your hand against the serpent symbol, and it immediately begins to glow with the same warm radiance as the flame. Both carvings now shine with steady light, their combined illumination casting intricate shadows across the door's surface. The successful activation of two symbols in sequence suggests you are following the correct pattern. The remaining hand symbol and the eye above await your next decision in this mystical sequence.

choices.choiceText: press the hand

title: Press the hand
narrativeText: You press your palm against the hand symbol carved into the door. A terrible screech fills the gorge, and the previously glowing symbols suddenly fade to darkness. The carved hand tears itself free from the door with violent force, lunging towards your throat. Its thorned bindings dig into your flesh with savage intensity, drawing blood as they tighten around your neck. The supernatural appendage's grip threatens to crush your windpipe as you struggle against its inhuman strength.

choices.choiceText: prise the hand from your neck
Use your sword to cut the fingers off
Pull away from the hand

title: press the hand
narrativeText: You press your hand against the hand symbol, and it begins to glow with the same radiance as the flame and serpent. All three lower symbols now shine with steady light, and the eye symbol above them begins to emit its own eerie luminescence. However, despite this apparent success, the massive door remains firmly sealed. The eye's glow seems to pulse expectantly, suggesting that one final action is required to complete the sequence and gain entry to the mountain's interior.

choices.choiceText: Press the eye

title: Press the eye
narrativeText: You place your hand upon the eye symbol, and immediately all the glowing symbols begin to fade. A deep cracking sound emanates from within the door itself, as though ancient mechanisms long dormant are finally stirring to life. The massive ironwood barrier creaks slowly open, revealing a gap just wide enough for you to squeeze through. From the darkness beyond comes a powerful stench of decay and ages-old corruption, the accumulated miasma of centuries seeping out to assault your senses.

choices.choiceText: Enter the mountain

title: Enter the mountain
narrativeText: You squeeze through the narrow opening and enter the mountain's depths. The darkness that initially greets you gradually gives way to an eerie, pale green luminescence emanating from patches of glowing moss that cling to the cavern walls. This unnatural phosphorescence provides just enough illumination to reveal the true scale of the chamber you have entered. The cavern stretches away before you like a vast underground cathedral, its ceiling lost in shadows far above. What strikes you most profoundly is the chamber's obvious purpose: this is a mustering hall designed to accommodate thousands upon thousands of warriors. Neat rows of stone formations rise from the floor, clearly intended as assembly points for organised ranks of soldiers. Weapon racks stand empty along the walls, their metal fixtures gleaming dully in the moss-light, waiting for armaments that have never been placed. The entire space carries an air of expectant emptiness, as though it awaits an army that has not yet been created. To your left and right, smaller cave openings lead deeper into the mountain's bowels. Far ahead, barely visible in the ghostly illumination, you can discern three doorways. The leftmost of these three appears to stand open, whilst the others remain sealed.

choices.choiceText: Examine the side caverns
Walk over and examine the doors

title: Examine the side caverns
narrativeText:
You approach the first of the side caverns. You light your lantern to see deeper within it, the light dancing across its entrance. Though smaller than the main chamber, this space still stretches beyond the reach of your flame. The walls bear carefully carved stone hollows, each one precisely shaped to hold specific items. Ancient wood racks rest within these alcoves. Their construction suggesting they once displayed weapons and armor, but now stand empty. 
You continue your exploration through the remaining side chambers. Several contain corroded iron cages. Their bars thick enough to restrain creatures of considerable size. Yet these two stand vacant. As you approach the rearmost cavern, something about it strikes you as different. 
This chamber spans a greater width than the others. And at its centre sits a flat-topped boulder. The stone surface bears intricate carvings, and small wooden figures adorn it like chess pieces. Stone seats encircle this table, positioned as though awaiting council members or commanders. What do you do?

choices.choiceText: Enter the last chamber and examine it more
Go to the doors

title: Walk over and examine the doors
narrativeText: You make your way across the cavern floor towards the three distant doorways, your lantern casting shifting shadows that seem to dance between the ancient stones. The doors prove to be massive constructions, each one standing twice your height and built to dwarf any who might approach them. No markings or inscriptions indicate their destinations. You examine each portal carefully and discover that the middle and rightmost doors are securely locked, their keyholes gaping wide and empty like hollow eyes. The leftmost door hangs slightly ajar, offering a tantalising glimpse of darkness beyond, but it lacks any handle or grip. The gap between door and frame appears just wide enough for a person to squeeze through, yet the massive portal is far too heavy to shift with finger strength alone.

choices.choiceText: Use your sword to lever the door open, it could break it
Search for something that may help

title: Enter the last chamber and examine it more
narrativeText: You step into the rearmost chamber and raise your lantern to examine the carved boulder more closely. The flat stone surface reveals itself to be an intricate map of the local region, with every hill, valley, and settlement marked in precise detail. Carved figures populate this stone tableau, depicting various generals positioned at the head of vast undead armies. The strategic plan becomes clear as you trace the formations: a coordinated assault designed to encircle and overwhelm the scattered hamlets and villages. Behind each army, additional figures represent what appear to be caged wagons, their purpose darkly implied. A fine layer of dust covers everything, undisturbed patterns that suggest no living hand has moved these pieces for many seasons. The stone seats around the boulder remain pushed back from the table, as though the last council simply walked away and never returned.

choices.choiceText: Search the room further
Head back to the doors

title: Search the room further
narrativeText:
Your systematic search of the chamber reveals little beyond the strategic display, safe for what appears to be a discarded tome lying forgotten near one of the stone seats. You retrieve the book and open its leather-bound covers, only to find pages filled with text in an arcane script that dances beyond your comprehension. 
As you turn through the parchment leaves, however, a detailed illustration of the mountain itself emerges. The drawing reveals Merlot's spike divided into three distinct levels. A lower realm depicting the disturbing images of humans in various stages of torment. The middle level, where you currently stand, shown as a vast army staging ground. You can see that the middle section has something behind this vast staging chamber, though. Something behind the middle door. Something that doesn't look very good at all. More tables are depicted with figures lying prone upon them. You have no idea what this could be. 
The final upper levels, they are smaller, and it seems deliberately without detail on this map. The book offers no further insights that you would find useful. Its remaining pages are either blank or filled with more incomprehensible text

choices.choiceText: Head back out and examine the doors

title: Use your sword to lever the door open, it could break it
narrativeText: You withdraw your sword and carefully position the blade within the narrow gap between door and frame, using it as a lever to apply pressure against the massive portal. Despite your concerns about damaging the weapon, the door begins to yield under your efforts, shifting just enough for you to gain purchase with your fingertips, then your entire hands. You manage to widen the opening further until it reaches a width sufficient for passage, though you hear a distinct mechanical click as though some hidden mechanism has engaged. The door refuses to move beyond this point, but the gap now provides adequate space for you to slip through into whatever lies beyond.

choices.choiceText: Push your lantern through the gap and see what is ahead
Enter the doorway

title: Search for something that may help
narrativeText: You conduct a thorough search of the immediate area, examining every shadow and crevice that might conceal some tool or mechanism to aid in opening the stubborn door. Your lantern light reveals nothing useful, no forgotten implements or hidden levers that might serve your purpose. The vast cavern maintains its austere emptiness, offering no assistance beyond what you carry with you. The echoes of your searching footsteps eventually fade into silence, leaving you to confront the reality that your sword remains the only option for forcing passage through the partially opened door.

choices.choiceText: Use your sword

title: Push your lantern through the gap and see what is ahead
narrativeText: You extend your lantern through the gap, pushing it ahead of you to illuminate whatever lies beyond the door. The flickering light reveals a steep stone staircase descending sharply into the mountain's depths, its steps worn smooth by countless feet over untold years. Just beyond the threshold, a section of stone has risen slightly above the surrounding floor, creating an uneven surface. Empty torch brackets line the walls at regular intervals, their iron fittings corroded with age. A cloying smell of decay and ancient corruption rises from the depths below, so potent that it makes your eyes water and catches in your throat. From the darkness far below comes the steady, echoing drip of water falling into unseen pools.

choices.choiceText: Continue through the door to the pathway beyond
Step Carefully over the threshold and enter the doorway

title: Enter the doorway
narrativeText: You squeeze your body through the narrow gap, feeling the cold stone frame brush against your shoulders as you pass into the space beyond. As you step forward onto the raised stone section of the floor, you suddenly understand the meaning of that mechanical click you heard when opening the door. The sound was a trap setting into motion. The stone beneath your foot shifts slightly under your weight, and immediately you hear the grinding sound of metal moving somewhere above you in the darkness. You have just enough time to look up before the massive blade, though ancient, proves that age has not dulled its effectiveness. The last thing you hear is the wet sound of steel meeting flesh, and then darkness claims you forever.

title: Descend to the lower level
narrativeText: You navigate the final steps with particular care, lifting your foot high to clear a raised threshold that spans the width of the staircase. As you pause to examine your surroundings, your lantern light catches the gleam of metal above the doorway, revealing a massive blade suspended in readiness to fall upon any unwary visitor. Beyond this ominous trap, broad stone steps continue their descent towards a carved archway that frames the bottom of the passage. Through this opening, you glimpse a hewn rock corridor stretching into deeper darkness. Rusted torch brackets cling to the walls like me tallic barnacles, and the floor beneath your feet crunches softly with the accumulated bone dust of ages. Multiple doorways punctuate the walls along the corridor's length, each one a black mouth leading to unknown chambers. At the far end of this subterranean passage, the outline of a much larger chamber awaits, its contents hidden in shadow.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the doorways along the corridor
Head directly to the large chamber at the end

title: Investigate the doorways
narrativeText:
You approach the first doorway along the corridor. The ironwood door hangs askew, its frame twisted and broken, allowing you to peer into the cell beyond. Cautiously, you step inside. The stone walls are covered with countless scratch marks, made by unseen claws or tools, some deep enough that you can feel their grooves with your fingertips. Dark stains cover the floor stones, and the metallic scent of old blood mingles with the musty odour of long decay. In the far corner, a skeleton slumps against the wall, still wearing the remnants of tattered clothing. 
You find nothing here and head back out. 
As you examine the other cells along the corridor, you discover similar grisly scenes, some containing multiple sets of remains. 
Beneath the bones in one cell, you spot a leather pouch that has somehow survived the passage of time. From deeper within the cellblock, you can hear persistent scratching sounds that echo off the stone walls.

choices.choiceText: Search the leather pouch carefully
Investigate the scratching sounds
Leave this cursed place and head to the end chamber

title: Approach the end chamber
narrativeText: You walk slowly down the bone-dust corridor, your footsteps creating small puffs of powdered remains that tickle your nostrils. The corridor opens into a vast torture chamber, its high ceiling lost in shadow above you. Along the walls, you can make out the shapes of various torture instruments, their metal surfaces gleaming dully in the dim light. The centre of the chamber is dominated by a massive stone altar, its surface stained dark with substances you prefer not to contemplate. Carved channels run from the altar's surface to drain holes at its base, and you can hear the faint sound of dripping echoing from somewhere beneath. Above the altar, suspended by heavy chains, hangs a desiccated corpse clothed in what were once fine robes, now reduced to tatters. A tarnished silver circlet sits upon its withered skull, and around its neck dangles a large iron key on a heavy chain. As you observe this grisly scene, the corpse's eyes suddenly snap open, glowing with an eerie green light that casts dancing shadows on the chamber walls.

choices.choiceText: Draw your sword and prepare for battle
Try to speak to the undead creature
Back out and consider your next move

title: Search the leather pouch
narrativeText: You kneel beside the bones and carefully examine the leather pouch, testing its weight in your hands before opening it. The leather creaks softly as you work the drawstrings loose, and the contents rattle quietly inside. Within the pouch, you discover several copper coins that have turned green with age, their surfaces rough and pitted. There is also a small piece of flint alongside a piece of steel, both items showing signs of use but appearing to be in working condition. Finally, you find a scrap of parchment with writing that has faded beyond recognition, the ink having run and smudged until only ghostly traces remain. While none of these items appears particularly valuable, you recognise that such basic supplies could prove useful in dire circumstances.

choices.choiceText: Take the items and investigate the scratching sounds
Take the items and head to the main chamber

title: Combat with the undead guardian
narrativeText: The corpse drops from its chains above the altar with a heavy thud, landing silently on the stone floor below. It moves with unnatural speed, its limbs flowing like water as it approaches. Its claws are extended like talons, yellowed bone protruding from rotted fingertips, and it lunges at you with inhuman grace.
