Your sword strikes pass through rotted flesh with wet, tearing sounds, but the creature shows no reaction to pain. Black ichor seeps from the wounds, dripping to the stone at your feet. It speaks in an ancient tongue, the words scraping from its throat like stones grinding together, echoing off the chamber walls. Its skeletal hands grasp for your throat, the touch cold as winter stone, whilst its empty eye sockets burn with that unnatural green fire. You must decide how to defeat this relentless foe.

choices.choiceText: Aim for the chains holding the key
Try to sever its head
Attempt to push it onto the altar spikes

title: Speak to the undead guardian
narrativeText: You call out to the hanging corpse, your voice echoing through the vast chamber. It looks at you intensely, its head turning with a slow, deliberate movement that sends the chains above creaking. When it speaks, its voice is like grinding stone, each word scraping through the air with ancient authority. It claims to be the former gaoler of this cursed place, stating that it guards the way to the army above. The creature's voice carries the weight of centuries, resonating from deep within its desiccated chest. It demands to know your purpose, the green glow in its empty sockets flickering with suspicion. Around its neck, the key glints temptingly in the pale light, the iron surface reflecting the chamber's eerie illumination as it sways with each word. You must decide how to respond to this guardian of the depths.

choices.choiceText: Tell it you serve the mountain's master
Offer to free it from its eternal duty
Demand it give you the key

title: Investigate the scratching sounds
narrativeText: You follow the scratching sounds to their source, finding a cell door that stands slightly ajar, its rusted hinges creaking softly in some unfelt draught. From within the dark confines of the cell comes a rustling, scurrying sound, as though dozens of small creatures are moving about in the shadows. As your light penetrates the darkness, you can see numerous pairs of eyes that glow with a sickly green luminescence, like diseased emeralds reflecting your torch flame. The creatures are rats, but their fur hangs in mangy patches, revealing sections of yellowed bone beneath. Their teeth chatter together in an unnatural rhythm, creating a sound like dozens of tiny castanets. When your light falls upon them fully, they turn as one towards the doorway, their chittering growing louder and more agitated. The mass of undead vermin begins to surge towards you, their collective movement creating a sound like dry leaves being stirred by wind.

choices.choiceText: Pour lamp oil and use flint to create fire
Slam the cell door shut and flee
Use your sword to fight them

title: Aim for the chains holding the key
narrativeText: You decide to target the chain around the gaoler's neck, hoping to claim the key without destroying the creature entirely. The undead entity launches itself at you with terrifying speed, its claws raking through the air where your head was mere moments before. You throw yourself sideways, feeling the rush of displaced air as its talons whistle past your ear. Rolling across the stone floor, you come up swinging, your blade aimed at the thick iron links encircling its throat. The sword connects with a resounding clang that reverberates through the chamber, sending vibrations up your arms. The creature shrieks in fury and lunges again, this time grasping for your sword arm. You wrench yourself backwards, stumbling over loose stones as its fingertips brush your sleeve. Your second strike comes from below, angled upward at the chain's weakest point. Metal screams against metal as your blade bites deep into the ancient links. With a sharp crack like breaking bone, the chain snaps apart completely. The iron key tumbles to the floor with a series of metallic chimes that echo off the chamber walls. What happens next comes as a complete shock, the moment the key leaves its neck, the gaoler's eyes suddenly dim and flicker out like extinguished candles. Its entire form goes rigid, then crumbles backwards to strike the altar with a hollow thud. It becomes as motionless as carved stone, and you realise with surprise that the key was not merely its treasure, but somehow its very essence and purpose for existing.

choices.choiceText: Grab the key and flee up the stairs
Hack the head from the inanimate creature and take the key back to the main cavern.

title: Try to sever its head
narrativeText: You swing for the creature's neck. The flesh is rotten, your blade slices through the putrid tissue with a wet, tearing sound. Half of the neck remains intact, black sinew and bone still connecting the creature's head. The rotted flesh gives way easily, yet the creature shows no pain. It grasps your sword arm with fingers like iron bands, the cold touch of decay seeping through your sleeve to your skin. In one motion, it pushes you back, then pulls you forward with inhuman strength. Your arm rips from its joint with a sickening crack, white-hot agony shooting through your shoulder. Before you can recover, the burning pain overwhelming your senses, you feel your other arm grasped in its relentless grip. The creature pulls your arms apart slowly, methodically, the sound of stretching sinews echoing through the stone chamber. Your ribs strain, your breathing becomes desperate gasps, the metallic taste of blood fills your mouth. Your end has come.

title: Attempt to push it onto the altar spikes
narrativeText: You charge at the undead gaoler. It sidesteps with inhuman grace, moving like mist around your desperate attack. Its hands strike and grasp at your shoulders, bony fingers digging deep into muscle. The pressure builds inward, compressing your chest until the air leaves your lungs in ragged gasps. Its strength is unnatural, supernatural, the grip unbreakable as iron shackles. You cannot break free, your struggles proving futile against its ancient power. It has done this many times before, each victim meeting the same inevitable fate. It slowly walks you forward through the chamber, your feet dragging helplessly across the blood-stained stone. Past the carved drainage channels, towards the altar where iron spikes gleam wetly in the torchlight. Even slower now, with deliberate, torturous precision, it guides your body onto the waiting points. The first spike pierces your back, then another finds its mark, each one sliding through flesh with practised accuracy. Your end has come.

title: Tell it you serve the mountain's master
narrativeText: You declare your allegiance to the master. It drops from its chains above the altar, landing silently on the stone floor. It advances to see you more clearly but without threat, moving with eerie, fluid grace. It speaks words in the ancient tongue, syllables like grinding stone scraping through the chamber air. You cannot respond correctly, the alien language beyond your understanding. Its suspicion turns to certainty, empty eye sockets seeming to pierce your very soul. It recognises you as an intruder, an impostor who dares to invoke its master's name. It is too close now, its unnatural speed having closed the distance whilst you fumbled for words. Its grip pins your arms to your sides with crushing force, the stench of tomb-decay and old blood filling your nostrils. Its maw opens and closes on your head, jaw unhinging with wet, crackling sounds. The fetid breath washes over you, blackened teeth scraping against bone as darkness claims your vision. Your end has come.

title: Offer to free it from its eternal duty
narrativeText: You speak of ending its torment, your words carrying through the chamber with genuine intention. The gaoler listens with that terrible stillness of the undead, its green-glowing eyes fixed upon you. When it responds, its voice grinds through the air like millstones, ancient and implacable. It states that free will belongs to the living, and that it is merely an extension of its master's will, bound by forces beyond mortal comprehension. The creature explains that you must be brought to death or service, that such is the way of this place, and that it will not be freed from its eternal duty. The finality in its voice echoes through the chamber, carrying the weight of countless years. The chains above creak ominously as it shifts position, and you realise that negotiation has failed. You must now decide to attack the creature.

choices.choiceText: Attack the creature

title: Demand it give you the key
narrativeText: You boldly demand the key, your voice ringing with authority through the chamber. The gaoler's eyes flare with rage, the green glow intensifying to a burning emerald fire that casts harsh shadows on the walls. It drops from its chains immediately above the altar, landing with a bone-jarring thud that sends vibrations through the stone floor. There are no words now, only violent action as the ancient fury of the undead takes hold. It moves faster than any living being could, a blur of rotted flesh and tattered robes rushing toward you. You swing your sword in desperation, the blade whistling through the air as you try to ward off its approach. The sound of metal cutting through the stale air fills the chamber, and you realise you must now defend yourself against this powerful creature that has been goaded beyond reason.

choices.choiceText: Defend yourself

title: Pour lamp oil and use flint to create fire
narrativeText: Working with swift efficiency, you pour lamp oil across the stone floor of the cell, creating a barrier between yourself and the approaching swarm. The oil spreads in dark pools, its pungent aroma mixing with the stench of decay. You strike your flint against the steel, sending bright sparks cascading towards the oil. The moment the sparks touch the liquid, flames spread rapidly across the floor with a whooshing sound. The undead rats, caught in the advancing wall of fire, release piercing shrieks that echo off the stone walls. The flames consume their unnatural forms, reducing them to ash and smoke in moments. The air fills with acrid smoke that makes you cough, but when it clears, the threat has been completely eliminated, leaving only charred remains on the scorched stone floor.

choices.choiceText: Continue exploring the lower level
Head to the main chamber

title: Slam the cell door shut and flee
narrativeText: You throw your full weight against the cell door, slamming it shut with a resounding crash that reverberates through the corridor. The rusted hinges protest with a grinding screech, but they hold firm against the impact. Immediately, you can hear the rats hurling themselves against the barrier from within, their claws scratching frantically at the metal surface while their chittering reaches a frenzied pitch. The door shudders under their assault but remains securely closed. You retreat quickly down the corridor, your hurried footsteps echoing off the stone walls as the sounds of scratching and chittering gradually fade behind you. Although you have escaped the immediate danger, you gain nothing from this encounter save the knowledge that some threats in this place are best left undisturbed.

choices.choiceText: Head to the main chamber

title: Use your sword to fight them
narrativeText: You draw your sword from its sheath, the steel ringing as it clears the scabbard. The undead rats pour forth from the cell like a putrid tide of corruption, their collective chittering rising to a maddening crescendo that threatens to shatter your sanity. Your blade cuts through the first wave of attackers, black ichor spraying as you slice several of the creatures apart, but for every rat you destroy, three more surge forward from the darkness. They swarm up your legs with horrifying speed, their needle-sharp teeth piercing your flesh whilst their rotting claws tear through fabric and skin alike. The stench of decay fills your nostrils as they climb higher, their diseased bites burning like liquid fire. You feel warm blood streaming down your limbs as their weight drags you inexorably to the bone-strewn floor. More rats pour from hidden crevices in the walls, covering your face and torso in a writhing carpet of decomposing fur and exposed bone. Your screams are muffled by their bodies as they gnaw through muscle and sinew with methodical precision, their chittering rising to a triumphant crescendo as they feast upon your still-living flesh. You remain conscious far longer than mercy would allow, feeling every tear of their teeth as they strip away pieces of your body whilst you yet draw breath. The ancient stones grow slick with your life essence as the swarm devours you piece by piece, leaving you aware and suffering until the very end. Your final sight is of more rats emerging from the shadows, drawn by the scent of fresh carnage, ready to pick clean whatever morsels remain. Even death offers no reprieve, for your bones will join the countless others in these cells, a testament to the horrific fate that awaits all who disturb the denizens of this nightmare realm.

title: Return to main cavern with the key
narrativeText: With the iron key now in your possession, you retrace your steps through the bone-littered corridor and carefully climb the fifty-three stone steps back to the upper level. You squeeze through the narrow gap in the doorway and emerge into the main cavern once more. The middle door awaits your attention, and when you insert the key into the large lock, it fits perfectly. The mechanism turns with a satisfying click, and you can now access the middle level of this underground complex.

choices.choiceText: Unlock the door

title: Back out and consider your next move
narrativeText: You back out of the chamber, your footsteps careful and measured as you retreat into the corridor beyond. You consider your situation, weighing the options before you with growing urgency. The key must open one of the main doors, that much is certain, but obtaining it will require facing the ancient guardian within. Your choices are limited, each path fraught with its own perils. The creature hangs motionless above the altar, but you know that any approach will rouse it to terrible action. You must decide whether to draw your sword and enter ready for immediate combat, steel ringing as you prepare for battle, or pull the medallion into the open and advance to engage, hoping that the ancient symbol might give you some advantage against the undead horror.

choices.choiceText: Draw your sword and enter ready for combat
Pull the medallion into the open and advance to engage.

title: Pull the medallion into the open and advance to engage.
narrativeText: You re-enter the chamber, the medallion held openly before you, its surface catching what little light filters through the darkness. The creature is down from its chains and advancing toward the door, its movements fluid and predatory. The moment it sees the medallion, everything changes. It recoils as if struck, and a piercing scream tears from its throat that echoes through the chamber like the cry of the damned. 'The mark of the banishment!' it shrieks, the words scraping through the air with obvious terror and recognition. The creature staggers backward, its composure shattered, ancient fear replacing its previous confidence.

choices.choiceText: Use it's confusion to take its head from it's shoulders
The creature is large and fast, use the confusion to go for the key

title: Hack the head from the inanimate creature and take the key back to the main cavern.
narrativeText: You deftly strike at your opponent, your blade cutting through the air with precision and purpose. The steel cleaves through the creature's neck cleanly, separating its head from its shoulders with a wet, decisive sound. The ancient horror falls to the stone floor and lies still, never to rise again, its green-glowing eyes fading to empty darkness. The threat ended, you bend down and take the iron key from the chain around its neck, the metal still warm from the creature's unnatural life force. With your prize secured, you turn and begin the journey back to the main hall, your footsteps echoing through the chamber as you leave the destruction behind. The key weighs heavy in your hand, a tangible symbol of your victory over the undead guardian.

choices.choiceText: Return to the main hall

title: The Central Torture Chamber
narrativeText: You retrace your steps through the echoing corridors, the iron key heavy in your palm, its cold metal warming slightly under your touch. Upon reaching the main hall, you examine it carefully beneath the flickering torchlight. The key's substantial size and ornate design clearly mark it as belonging to the middle door, the central entry that has remained sealed throughout your exploration. You approach the imposing portal, inserting the key into the massive lock, where it slides home with a satisfying click that resonates through the chamber. The door swings inward on surprisingly well-oiled hinges, their smooth motion suggesting recent maintenance despite the ancient surroundings. As you step through the threshold, your boots echo against stone whilst you detect no immediate traps or dangers. The scene that unfolds before you reveals a vast central cavern, its ceiling lost in shadow above. Iron tables dominate the centre of this accursed space, each one fitted with rusted shackles and restraints at strategic points that speak eloquently of their dark purposes. Torture implements of every conceivable design hang from hooks mounted along the tables' edges, their surfaces bearing the unmistakable stains of old blood that no amount of time could fully cleanse. Above each table, hollow iron spikes dangle menacingly from wire hoses that snake upward like metallic serpents into the darkness of the vaulted ceiling. Though the chamber appears empty of living souls, a stone altar commands attention at the far end, its presence both ominous and compelling as it stands partially obscured by dancing shadows cast by your torch.

choices.choiceText: Approach the stone altar at the far end
Investigate one of the iron torture tables

title: The Sacrificial Altar
narrativeText: You approach the stone altar that dominates the chamber's far end, your footsteps echoing softly against the polished floor. The altar itself is carved from black basalt, its surface gleaming with an oily sheen that seems to absorb rather than reflect your torchlight. Within a carved basin at its centre lies a ceremonial dagger, its blade gleaming with an unnatural lustre despite the chamber's perpetual gloom. The weapon appears freshly honed, as though awaiting immediate use. Ancient runes cover the altar's sides in intricate patterns, their meanings dark and foreboding. You recognise several as binding spells, words of power designed to trap souls and compel unwilling obedience. A particularly disturbing carving depicts what appears to be a robed priest surrounded by snarling wolves. The figure stands over a table where a human form lies arched in agony, a spike protruding from its mouth whilst the priest's hands are raised in apparent blessing or invocation. Beneath this macabre scene, a single word is carved in the ancient tongue: 'Blessing'. Behind the altar stands a throne-like chair carved from the same black basalt, its arms shaped like grasping claws that seem to reach toward the torture tables. The chair's positioning grants its occupant a perfect view of every activity in the chamber below, suggesting this was a place of observation and command rather than mere ceremony.

choices.choiceText: Examine the ceremonial dagger closely
Try to read the binding runes on the altar
Investigate the throne-like chair

title: The Instruments of Torment
narrativeText: You turn your attention to the torture implements arrayed along this particular table, and the sight that greets you chills your blood despite your growing familiarity with this chamber's horrors. The instruments are crafted with diabolical precision, each tool designed with the sole purpose of inflicting maximum agony whilst keeping victims conscious and alive for as long as possible. Brands for searing flesh bear intricate patterns that would leave permanent marks of ownership upon survivors. Flensing knives with serrated edges designed to peel skin in precise strips hang alongside bone screws whose threading suggests they were meant to be turned slowly, deliberately. Devices whose purposes you prefer not to contemplate fill the remaining hooks, their twisted forms speaking to a creativity in cruelty that defies human imagination. The metal surfaces bear the unmistakable patina of frequent use, their edges still sharp despite countless applications. Several of the instruments show fresher bloodstains, the crimson still bright enough to suggest recent employment. The methodical arrangement of these tools speaks to a systematic approach to torture that somehow seems worse than random brutality, for it suggests both purpose and terrible expertise behind the suffering inflicted within these walls. Each implement has been placed with care, positioned for easy access during the conduct of whatever dark rituals transpire upon this accursed table.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the ledger more
Grab a flensing knife as an additional weapon

title: The Torture Tables
narrativeText: You select one of the iron tables for closer examination, approaching its grisly construction with mounting dread. Heavy iron shackles are built directly into the table's surface at precisely measured intervals corresponding to wrists, ankles, and throat, their positioning suggesting considerable experience in restraining struggling victims. The metal bears the scratches and wear marks of countless desperate attempts at escape, each groove a testament to futile struggle against unbreakable bonds. Fresh blood stains darken the table's surface, the crimson still bearing a wet sheen that confirms your worst fears about this chamber's continued use. This is no abandoned relic of past horrors, but an active centre of ongoing torment. The hollow iron spike suspended directly above this table shows scratches around its tip, evidence of repeated insertion and withdrawal. Wire hoses connect it to unseen machinery hidden in the ceiling's darkness, suggesting some manner of feeding or draining mechanism. A leather-bound ledger rests beside the table, its pages yellowed with age but filled with recent entries. Your blood runs cold as you read the final notation, written in careful script: 'Subject 247: Accepted the Master's blessing at dawn'. The matter-of-fact tone of this horrific record-keeping somehow makes it even more disturbing than if it had been written with obvious malice.

choices.choiceText: Read through more entries in the ledger
Examine the hollow iron spike more closely
Examine the instruments of torture

title: The Summoning Trigger
narrativeText: You extend your hand toward the ceremonial dagger that rests within the altar's carved basin, and immediately the medallion hanging against your chest begins to radiate heat as though reacting with violent hatred to your intended action. The moment your fingers make contact with the blade's hilt, the weapon responds with a crackling discharge of black sparks that dance along its length like malevolent lightning. The sound it produces is neither wholly electrical nor entirely magical, but something altogether more unsettling that seems to bypass your ears and resonate directly within your bones. The medallion's heat intensifies until it becomes almost painful against your skin, clearly warning you of some dire consequence should you proceed. From somewhere near the torture tables comes the unmistakable sound of a human whimper, soft and desperate, cutting through the chamber's oppressive atmosphere like a knife. The voice carries the quality of one who has suffered beyond the limits of endurance yet still clings to life with desperate tenacity.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the sound
Drop the dagger and back away from the altar

title: The Binding Runes
narrativeText: You lean closer to study the ancient runes carved with meticulous precision into the altar's obsidian sides, your torchlight casting dancing shadows that make the symbols seem to writhe and shift before your eyes. Most are inscribed in the old tongue, their meanings dark and foreboding as they speak of binding souls to eternal servitude and compelling absolute obedience from the unwilling. The craftsmanship is exquisite yet utterly malevolent, each character carved with reverent care by hands that served powers best left unnamed. A particularly disturbing relief depicts what appears to be a robed priest surrounded by a pack of snarling wolves, their eyes wild with unnatural hunger. The figure stands over a sacrificial table where a human form lies arched in agony, a spike protruding grotesquely from its mouth whilst the priest's hands are raised in apparent blessing or invocation. Beneath this macabre tableau, a single word is carved in letters that seem to pulse with their own malevolent life: 'Blessing'. As you continue examining these blasphemous inscriptions, a low chanting begins to echo from the chamber's darkest recesses, voices speaking words in tongues that predate human civilisation. A cry of alarm and fear suddenly pierces the air from near one of the torture tables, and you look over to see a young girl huddled on the floor in the shadows, her presence having escaped your notice until this moment. Your heart sinks as you realize you cannot reach her in time, for something is approaching from the darkness with deliberate, predatory intent.

choices.choiceText: Draw your Sword and prepare for what may be next
Hide and see what happens

title: The High Priest's Chair
narrativeText: You approach the throne-like chair that stands in commanding position behind the altar, its imposing presence suggesting both authority and malevolent purpose. The seat is carved from the same black basalt as the altar itself, its surface polished to an obsidian gleam that reflects your torchlight in unsettling ways. The chair's arms are shaped like grasping claws, their talons extending outward as though reaching toward the torture tables that fill the chamber below. Every detail of its construction speaks to its purpose: this is clearly a seat of observation, positioned to grant its occupant an unobstructed view of every activity conducted within the chamber. You can almost imagine the figure who would sit here, watching with cold satisfaction as victims suffered upon the tables below, perhaps making notes in the ledgers or offering occasional commands to subordinates. As you cast your eyes over the chamber from this elevated vantage point, you begin to understand the full scope of what transpired here. The methodical arrangement of tables, the precise positioning of implements, the careful documentation of procedures, all suggest a highly organised operation designed to extract maximum suffering from its victims. From somewhere near the tables comes a small cry of fear, barely audible yet piercing in its desperation, reminding you that whatever horrors this chamber has witnessed may not yet be concluded.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the noise
Sit in the chair to get better perspective

title: The Conversion Records
narrativeText: You open the leather-bound ledger and begin reading through its grisly entries, each page documenting with clinical precision the various methods employed to break prisoners' will and spirit. The handwriting is neat and methodical, suggesting an educated mind that took scholarly pride in recording every detail of the suffering inflicted within these walls. Each entry follows a similar pattern: initial resistance, application of specific tortures, psychological manipulation, and eventual capitulation. The final choice offered to each victim was invariably the same: accept death as a merciful release, or accept 'the Master's blessing to be free of pain'. Those who chose the latter were apparently impaled by the iron spikes suspended above each table, their life force slowly drained whilst some manner of dark essence filled the empty vessel of their bodies. The process appears to transform them into willing servants, their original personalities utterly consumed by whatever malevolent force has claimed them. As you read these horrific accounts, a human whimper reaches your ears from somewhere in the chamber's depths, followed immediately by the distinct sound of a heavy door closing with a resonant clunk. The main entrance has sealed itself, trapping you within this chamber of horrors with whatever lurks in the shadows. The timing cannot be coincidental; your presence has clearly been detected, and whatever dwells here has decided to make itself known.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the sound
Draw your sword and prepare for what may be next

title: Investigate the sound
narrativeText: You move cautiously toward the source of the sound, your footsteps muffled against the stone floor as you navigate between the torture tables. In the deeper shadows cast by the chamber's uneven torchlight, you discover the source of the whimpering: a young girl, perhaps sixteen or seventeen years of age, shackled to one of the tables with heavy iron restraints. Her clothes are torn and dishevelled, bearing evidence of her recent capture and rough handling, but she appears to have been taken only recently. Though clearly terrified and traumatised by her ordeal with the gaoler, she remains alert and aware, her wide eyes darting frantically between you and the altar behind. She has been prepared for whatever conversion ritual awaits within this chamber, but the process has not yet begun in earnest. Her breathing comes in quick, panicked gasps, and she flinches at every sound, suggesting she has witnessed enough of this place's horrors to understand her fate without yet having endured the worst of them. Her gaze fixes upon the altar with obvious terror, and when she speaks, her voice trembles with desperate urgency: 'End coming now.' The words carry the weight of recent prophecy rather than delirium, as though she has been told exactly what awaits her and knows the appointed time draws near. As if summoned by her words, guttural chanting begins to echo from the chamber's darkest recesses, voices speaking in harmonies that predate human speech and suggest intelligences that have never known mercy or compassion.

choices.choiceText: Free the girl and find a way out
Hide and see what happens
Draw your sword and prepare

title: Fatal Hesitation
narrativeText: You release the ceremonial dagger and step quickly back from the altar, the weapon clattering against the stone basin as the black sparks that danced along its length gradually diminish and fade. The moment you break contact, the oppressive atmosphere seems to shift, as though some malevolent attention has been drawn to your presence by your brief interaction with the accursed blade. From the chamber's darkest recesses comes the sound of three distinct howls, their tones neither wholly wolf nor entirely human, but something altogether more unsettling that speaks of creatures caught between states of existence. The howls are accompanied by the soft padding of massive paws against stone, their rhythm suggesting predators of considerable size moving with deliberate purpose. A voice emerges from the darkness, speaking with cold authority: 'Another comes, always will there be others.' The words carry the weight of inevitability, as though countless souls have stood where you now stand, facing the same terrible choices. You hear the entrance doorway swing shut behind you with the finality of a tomb sealing, the heavy iron hinges groaning under the weight of your entrapment. From the shadows emerges three large undead wolves, their eyes glowing with the same sickly light that illuminates the chamber's darkest corners. Behind them appears a tall figure clad in white robes, its face hidden within a deep hood yet radiating an aura of ancient malevolence. The figure regards you with what might be amusement and speaks again: 'We need a new gaoler.' It barks a command in a language that predates human civilisation, and the wolves begin their advance with measured steps. Meanwhile, the figure moves with casual indifference toward a nearby torture table, where a girl crouches beside it, her presence having escaped your notice until this moment of ultimate revelation.

choices.choiceText: Draw your weapon and prepare to fight three wolves
Pull your medallion out into the open

title: Hidden Behind the Throne
narrativeText: You drop to a crouch behind the throne, your heart pounding so loudly that you fear it might give away your position to whatever approaches from the chamber's depths. The black basalt chair provides adequate concealment whilst allowing you to observe the unfolding scene through gaps between its carved armrests. From the darkness emerge three large undead wolves, their movements fluid yet unnatural, as though they exist in defiance of the natural order. Their eyes burn with the same malevolent light that seems to permeate this accursed place, and their silent approach speaks of predators who have moved beyond the need for conventional hunting behaviours. Behind them follows a tall figure draped in white robes, its face hidden within a deep hood yet radiating an aura of ancient authority and malevolence. They move with deliberate purpose toward the source of the whimpering you heard earlier, suggesting they are well aware of the girl's presence and have come specifically to claim her. As this terrible procession unfolds before you, you feel an unexpected warmth emanating from the medallion pressed against your chest beneath your clothing. The sensation spreads through your body, bringing with it a sense of strength and confidence that seems at odds with your desperate circumstances. Your position behind the throne grants you a tactical advantage: you could strike the leftmost wolf down quickly if you rushed it before the pack becomes fully aware of your presence, potentially disrupting their formation and buying precious time for both yourself and the girl they clearly intend to capture.

choices.choiceText: You have advantage use it to charge them
Try to sneak behind them to increase your advantage
Stay hidden and observe

title: Pull heavily on the spike mechanism
narrativeText: Curiosity overcoming caution, you grasp the iron spike and pull downward, perhaps to test its strength or understand its mechanism. To your surprise, the spike moves smoothly downward with well-oiled precision, immediately producing a sound so revolting that your stomach lurches in response. The noise combines wet sucking with a high mechanical whine, as though the device is designed to both penetrate and drain in a single terrible motion. You release the mechanism in shock, stepping back as thick black ichor begins to drip steadily from the spike's tip, each drop striking the stone floor with an audible splatter that echoes through the chamber. The substance appears to move with its own malevolent life, spreading across the stone in patterns that suggest intelligence rather than simple fluid dynamics. The sight and smell of this unholy substance makes you feel physically ill, your body's natural revulsion warning you that you have disturbed something that should have remained dormant. As you struggle to maintain your composure, a cry of fear reaches your ears from somewhere near the torture tables, confirming what you had begun to suspect: you are not alone in this chamber of horrors. As you begin to turn toward the sound, determined to investigate its source, the glint of metal catches your attention from behind the throne. A small metal box sits partially concealed in the shadows, its surface bearing intricate engravings that suggest it may contain something of significant importance to whatever dark rituals transpire within these walls.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the sound
The cry want threatening check the box first

title: Draw your sword and prepare for what may be next
narrativeText: You rise to your full height and draw your sword in one fluid motion, the familiar weight of the blade providing some measure of comfort in this chamber of unspeakable horrors. The steel rings against its scabbard as you pull it free, the sound echoing through the vast space like a challenge hurled into the darkness itself. The moment your weapon clears its sheath, the guttural chanting that has been building in intensity reaches a crescendo, a single voice speaking in harmonies that seem to bypass your ears and resonate directly within your bones. From the shadows around the altar emerge three undead wolves, their eyes burning with malevolent intelligence as they move with the fluid grace of predators who have transcended the limitations of mortal flesh. Behind them follows a tall figure draped in white robes, its face hidden within a deep hood yet radiating an aura of ancient authority that speaks of centuries spent in service to powers best left unnamed. The figure regards you with what might be curiosity or amusement, its head tilting slightly as though assessing your potential value. When it speaks, its voice carries the weight of absolute certainty: 'We need a new gaoler.' The words are spoken without emotion, as though your fate has already been decided and this confrontation is merely a formality to be observed. It barks a sharp command in a language that predates human civilisation, and the wolves begin their advance with measured steps, their formation suggesting considerable tactical experience. Meanwhile, the robed figure turns its attention to what appears to be its original purpose, moving with casual indifference toward a corner where a young girl crouches in terror, her whimpering barely audible above the sound of approaching doom.

choices.choiceText: Ready yourself to defend
Climb a table to gain an advantage of higher ground
Three undead wolves is bad odds, retreat to the door

title: Free the girl and find a way out
narrativeText: You begin working frantically at the heavy iron shackles that bind the girl's wrists to the cold stone wall, your fingers searching for any mechanism that might release the ancient locks. The metal feels rough and corroded beneath your touch, yet refuses to yield despite your persistent efforts. As you strain against the unyielding bonds, the girl slowly lifts her head, and you hear the soft sound of a single tear dropping onto the stone floor below. Her voice barely rises above a whisper as she speaks three words that send ice through your veins: 'He is here.' You spin round, drawing yourself upright to face whatever approaches, only to discover that three undead wolves have surrounded you in complete silence, their decaying forms positioned at precise intervals around the torture table. Standing in their midsts, towering over you, stands a tall figure draped in white robes, its presence so close you can hear the whisper of fabric as it moves. Without warning, it brings down its arm with tremendous force, and the world explodes into darkness as the blow sends you crashing to the stone floor.

choices.choiceText: there are no more options

title: Hide and see what happens
narrativeText: You dart swiftly across the chamber floor, seeking shelter behind the nearest torture table where shadows offer concealment from whatever emerges from the darkness beyond the altar. Your breathing sounds unnaturally loud in the oppressive silence as you crouch low against the cold stone. From the black void behind the altar's imposing bulk, you hear the deliberate footfalls of something approaching, each step echoing with purpose across the chamber's stone floor. A tall figure emerges into the dim light, its white robes flowing like grave shrouds, whilst three huge undead wolves pad silently at its feet, their movements coordinated and predatory. Against your chest, your medallion radiates an intense warmth that seems to pulse with each beat of your heart, and strength flows through your limbs as if the ancient metal recognises the evil before you. The macabre procession advances steadily towards the whimpering girl, their attention focused entirely upon their intended victim. From your concealed position, you realise that the leftmost wolf stands within striking distance, its decaying flank exposed and vulnerable should you choose to launch a surprise attack.

choices.choiceText: Draw your sword and declare battle
Launch your attack on the nearest wolf first
Try to silently get behind the group and attack from the rear
The odds are not good try to sneak to the rear and escape this nighmare

title: Launch your attack on the nearest wolf first
narrativeText: Your blade slices through the air with deadly precision, catching the undead wolf completely unawares as it focuses its attention upon the terrified girl. The steel bites deep into the creature's flanks, penetrating the rotting flesh with a wet, tearing sound that echoes off the stone walls. Immediately, the tall figure whips round to face you, its white robes billowing as it turns, and from beneath its hood comes a shriek of pure rage that reverberates through every corner of the chamber. The sound pierces the air like the cry of some ancient predator, filled with fury at this unexpected interference. Black ichor begins to seep from the wounded wolf's side, dripping onto the stone floor with soft, irregular splashes. The other two wolves remain motionless for a heartbeat, as if awaiting commands, whilst the robed figure's attention shifts entirely to you, its stance suggesting barely contained violence ready to be unleashed.

choices.choiceText: Immediately strike the figure
Switch to attack the next nearest wolf

title: Try to silently get behind the group and attack from the rear
narrativeText: Moving with deliberate stealth across the cold stone floor, you advance through the chamber's shadows whilst the white-robed figure remains completely absorbed in its grisly work with the captive girl. The three undead wolves position themselves in a loose circle around their intended victim, their decaying forms utterly still as they await their master's commands, their attention fixed so intently upon the scene that they fail to detect your careful approach from behind. Each footstep you place with meticulous care. As you draw closer to the grotesque tableau, your medallion begins to radiate heat against your chest, the metal growing so warm it seems to burn through your clothing, as if the ancient artefact recognises the profound evil unfolding before it and responds with barely contained rage. The tall figure reaches down with deliberate movements, its pale hands grasping the girl's shoulders, and at its touch the heavy iron shackles that have bound her to the torture table simply fall away, clanking onto the stone floor with metallic echoes that ring through the chamber.

choices.choiceText: Attack the nearest wolf
Attack the tall figure

title: The odds are not good try to sneak to the rear and escape this nightmare
narrativeText: Recognising the overwhelming odds arrayed against you, you begin backing towards the chamber's main entrance, your eyes never leaving the three undead wolves as they slowly turn their heads to track your movement across the stone floor. Their glowing eyes follow your retreat with predatory interest, whilst the white-robed figure continues its methodical advance towards the helpless girl, seemingly unconcerned by your attempt to flee. Your feet carry you swiftly across the uneven stones, each step echoing loudly in the cavernous space despite your efforts at stealth. When you reach the heavy wooden door through which you first entered this accursed place, your hands grasp desperately at the iron handle, pulling and pushing with increasing panic. The door remains immovable, sealed as securely as if it were part of the stone wall itself, the metal cold and unyielding beneath your desperate grip. Behind you, the soft padding of paws on stone announces that the wolves have begun their approach, their movements silent yet purposeful. As you turn back to face the chamber, you discover that the creatures have already covered half the distance between you, their positions forming a coordinated pattern that blocks any possible escape route through the chamber itself.

choices.choiceText: No more options

title: Draw your sword and declare battle
narrativeText: Drawing your sword with a sharp ring of steel against scabbard, you fill your lungs and release a thunderous battle cry that echoes from every stone surface in the chamber, announcing your defiance to every evil presence within these walls. Your voice reverberates with fierce determination as you charge forward across the uneven stone floor, your weapon raised high and gleaming in the dim light. The three undead wolves immediately abandon their advance towards the girl, wheeling round as one to face this new threat, their decomposing forms moving with supernatural coordination. With speed that defies their decayed appearance, all three creatures launch themselves through the air simultaneously, their combined assault striking you like a battering ram. The coordinated impact proves devastating, your sword spinning from your grasp to clatter across the stone floor whilst powerful jaws clamp down upon your arms and shoulders. The creatures' grip is inexorable, their strength far beyond that of any living wolf, and despite your struggles they begin dragging your helpless form across the chamber floor towards where the tall figure continues its methodical work upon the captive girl.

choices.choiceText: no more options

title: Climb a table to gain an advantage of higher ground
narrativeText: With a swift bound, you vault onto the nearest torture table, the cold stone surface providing solid footing beneath your boots whilst granting you the tactical advantage of higher ground. From this elevated position, you survey the advancing threat as the three undead wolves begin to close the distance between you, their movements coordinated yet cautious as they adjust to your new position. The creatures' glowing eyes fix upon you with predatory focus, whilst their master continues its grisly work behind them, seemingly unconcerned by your defensive manoeuvres. Against your chest, your medallion responds to the approaching evil, the metal growing increasingly warm until it seems to pulse with heat against your skin through the fabric of your clothing. The ancient artefact's growing intensity suggests some connection to the supernatural forces arrayed against you, its warmth spreading through your torso like liquid fire. The wolves draw closer still, their paws making soft scratching sounds against the stone floor as they position themselves around the base of your makeshift fortress, and you sense that the moment for decisive action rapidly approaches.

choices.choiceText: Pull your medallion into the open
Quickly pull off our medallion and wrap it around your sword hand

title: No more options
narrativeText: The undead wolves surround you completely, their decaying forms positioned at strategic points that eliminate any possibility of movement or escape, whilst their supernatural strength proves more than sufficient to pin you helplessly to the cold stone floor. From this prone position, you watch in horror as the tall figure approaches the terrified girl, its white robes trailing across the chamber floor with soft whispers of fabric against stone. With deliberate, almost ritualistic movements, it grasps her shoulders, and the heavy iron shackles that have bound her fall away at its touch, clanking onto the floor with metallic echoes. The creature lifts her slight form with ease, carrying her to a nearby torture table where fresh restraints snap closed around her limbs with decisive clicks that reverberate through the chamber. When it selects an instrument from the array of tools beside the table, the girl's screams begin, piercing the air with such intensity that they seem to penetrate your very soul. At the height of her agony, the figure leans close to her ear, its voice carrying clearly across the stone chamber: 'The pain is eternal, or do you accept the master's blessing for it to end?' Through her shrieks, you hear her weak voice cry out that she will accept whatever release is offered. The figure moves swiftly to the overhead spike mechanism, driving the iron point down into her throat with mechanical precision. The horrible sucking sound that follows seems to draw the very life from her body whilst black ichor flows into her lifeless form. When her eyes spring open again, they hold no trace of humanity, only the cold emptiness of the undead. The figure turns its attention towards you, its voice carrying clearly across the chamber: 'Another joins us, and now for another.'

title: Pull your medallion into the open
narrativeText: Drawing the medallion from beneath your clothing, you hold the ancient artefact high where its metal surface catches what little light filters through the chamber. The moment it appears, the three wolves release howls that echo from every stone surface, their voices combining in a discordant chorus that speaks of supernatural recognition and rage. The tall figure immediately abandons its grisly work with the girl, turning its full attention towards you and the glowing metal in your raised hand. From beneath its hood, a voice emerges like the whisper of grave earth: 'You bring that to this sacred place. You are not the one we seek.' The white-robed form begins advancing towards your position, its movements deliberate and threatening, whilst the wolves' howls continue to reverberate through the chamber. Your medallion pulses with intense heat against your palm, the metal so warm it nearly burns your skin, as if the ancient artefact recognises the profound evil approaching and responds with its own power. The figure draws closer with each passing moment, its presence seeming to fill the chamber with an oppressive weight that presses down upon you like a physical force.

choices.choiceText: Hold the medallion on high as a protective ward
Wrap the medallion around your sword hand and prepare to fight
Tell the figure to call off the wolves or you will use the medallion

title: Hold the medallion on high as a protective ward
narrativeText: Raising the medallion high above your head like a protective talisman, you point its gleaming surface directly towards the advancing wolves, hoping that the ancient artefact's power might serve as a barrier against their supernatural malevolence. The tall figure responds with a sound that might once have been laughter, though now it emerges as something closer to the rustle of dried leaves or the whisper of wind through empty skulls. Its voice carries clearly across the chamber, filled with contempt and dark amusement: 'You know nothing, child.' With a sharp command that echoes off the stone walls, the figure gestures towards you, and immediately the three wolves abandon any hesitation. They surge forward as one coordinated force, their combined assault overwhelming your defensive stance in an instant. The medallion provides no protection as powerful jaws clamp around your arms and neck, their grip like iron shackles that resist every attempt to break free. You feel your medallion arm being shaken with tremendous violence, the joint wrenching and tearing until your shoulder separates with a wet, grinding sound that mingles with your own cry of agony. The wolves maintain their iron grip as they drag your broken form across the stone floor towards where the tall figure waits beside the torture table, your blood leaving a dark trail on the ancient stones beneath you.

choices.choiceText: Accept your fate

title: Attack the tall figure
narrativeText: Moving with absolute silence across the chamber floor, you advance through the shadows whilst the three undead wolves maintain their vigil around the terrified girl, their attention completely focused upon their master's methodical preparations. The tall figure works with ritualistic precision, its white robes rustling softly as it reaches towards the captive with deliberate movements. As you draw closer to the grotesque scene, your medallion responds to the profound evil surrounding you, the ancient metal growing steadily warmer against your chest until it pulses with heat that seems to match the rhythm of your own heartbeat. The artefact's intensity suggests it recognises the supernatural malevolence before you and responds with barely contained fury. Your target remains completely absorbed in its grisly task, reaching down to grasp the girl's shoulders with pale, long-fingered hands. At the figure's touch, the heavy iron shackles that have bound her to the torture table simply fall away, their metal clanking onto the stone floor with echoes that ring through the chamber's oppressive silence.

choices.choiceText: Remove the medallion and wrap it around your sword hand and attack
Pull out the medallion and attack the figure
Press you attack now while you still have the advantage

title: Immediately strike the figure
narrativeText: Your assault upon the wounded wolf has granted the tall figure precious moments to prepare for your attack, and as you press forward with your blade raised, it strikes back with surprising force and coordination. Its blow catches you squarely in the chest, lifting you from your feet and hurling you backwards across the chamber where you crash into the nearest torture table with bone-jarring impact. The collision sends instruments scattering across the stone floor with metallic clatters and rings that echo through the cavernous space. Against your chest, your medallion radiates intense heat from the close encounter with such profound evil, the metal so warm it seems to burn through your clothing whilst pulsing with barely contained energy. From your new position sprawled beside the overturned table, you see the three undead wolves advancing with coordinated precision, their movements suggesting they act with shared purpose under their master's will. Behind them, the tall figure looms like a spectre of death itself, its white robes flowing as it prepares to resume its grisly work. The metallic taste of blood fills your mouth from the impact, whilst your ribs ache with each breath you draw in the chamber's oppressive atmosphere.

choices.choiceText: Prepare to meet the advancing wolves
Quickly move to atop a table for advantage of height
Try to dodge the wolves and strike at the tall figure

title: Switch to attack the next nearest wolf
narrativeText: Having struck a blow against the first wolf, you shift your weight, pivoting to bring your blade to bear against the nearest of its companions. The metallic scent of your own blood mingles with the stench of decay that clings to these undead creatures, but you press forward nonetheless. In the chaos of combat, you fail to notice that your initial strike has made no lasting impression upon your first opponent, the dead flesh sealing around the wound without so much as a growl of pain. The creature feels nothing, for death has already claimed it once. Behind you, it recovers its footing with unnatural grace, its lifeless eyes fixed upon your exposed back. Your second swing connects with satisfying precision, the blade biting deep into the side of another wolf's neck. The steel parts the putrid flesh with ease, slicing clean through muscle and sinew that should have provided resistance but offers none. Even as you savour this small victory, the recovered wolf launches itself at you from behind, its considerable weight slamming into your shoulder and sending you stumbling sideways across the uneven stone floor.

choices.choiceText: Recover to your feet and continue the fight
Get on top of a nearby table and prepare to defend

title: Recover to your feet and continue the fight
narrativeText: You gather yourself quickly, finding your balance once more upon the cold stone. Your breathing comes in sharp, controlled bursts as you steady your grip upon your weapon's hilt. The third wolf, positioned now to your left, crouches low for a moment before launching itself through the air towards you. You begin to turn, raising your blade to meet this fresh assault, but the cunning of these creatures becomes apparent as the other two wolves coordinate their attack with devastating precision. Three bodies of snarling death converge upon you simultaneously, their combined weight and momentum proving far too much to withstand. You crash heavily to the ground, the impact driving the breath from your lungs. Powerful jaws, cold as winter iron, clamp down upon your neck and arms with methodical efficiency. These creatures do not seek to kill you outright, but rather to render you helpless, and in this grim purpose they succeed completely. Pinned beneath their crushing weight, unable to move so much as a finger, you are forced to bear witness to the unspeakable horror that unfolds before you. The robed figure returns to its dark business with casual indifference to your plight. It approaches the girl with deliberate steps, lifting her trembling form as the shackles that bound her fall away with a metallic clatter. Upon a nearby table, fresh restraints snap shut around her limbs with mechanical precision. The figure selects an instrument from among the array of terrible tools, and begins its methodical work. Her screams pierce the chamber's stale air, each cry more desperate and agonising than the last. The sound echoes off the stone walls until it seems to come from everywhere at once. At the crescendo of her suffering, the figure leans down and speaks in a voice like grinding stone: 'The pain is eternal, or do you accept the master's blessing for it to end?' Through her anguish, she manages to whisper her acceptance. Without hesitation, the figure reaches upward to grasp the iron spike suspended above, driving it down through her throat in one swift motion. The horrible sucking sound that follows seems to draw the very essence from her body as black ichor flows like liquid shadow into her now-lifeless form. Her eyes snap open, no longer her own. 'Another joins our ranks,' the figure intones with satisfaction, then turns its attention to you. 'And now, for another.'

title: Prepare to meet the wolves
narrativeText: The medallion at your chest thrums with what can only be described as fury, its metal growing warm against your skin through the fabric of your shirt. The sensation is both comforting and deeply unsettling, as though the artifact shares your rage at the scene before you. You have precious little time to contemplate this strange phenomenon, however, for your moment's pause to gather yourself has provided the undead wolves with the opportunity they needed to coordinate their assault. Moving with the uncanny synchronisation of a pack that shares a single malevolent will, all three creatures launch themselves at you as one. Their combined weight strikes you like a battering ram, driving you backwards and down onto the unforgiving stone floor. The impact sends sharp jolts of pain through your spine and ribs. Two of the beasts waste no time in clamping their jaws around your arms, their teeth sinking deep enough to draw blood but not deep enough to sever tendons, a calculated restraint that speaks to their unnatural intelligence. The third wolf, largest of the three, positions itself carefully before wrapping its stinking maw around your neck. Its breath carries the putrid scent of long-decayed meat and something far worse, something that speaks of corruption beyond mere death. These creatures harbour no intent to kill you quickly, for they serve a master who has need of the living. Instead, they seek only to render you completely immobile, to hold you fast whilst their master completes his work. In this grim objective, they achieve complete success. You lie pinned beneath their considerable bulk, every muscle straining futilely against bonds of flesh and bone that will not yield.

choices.choiceText: A show for you

title: Try to dodge the wolves and strike at the tall figure
narrativeText:
.Desperation drives you forward as you launch yourself once more towards the robed figure, attempting to weave between the undead wolves that guard their master with such devotion. Your boots scrape along the stone floor as you try to find purchase for this final gambit, knowing that success or failure will likely determine not only your fate, but that of the helpless girl as well. 
For a heartbeat it seems you might succeed in your desperate ploy. Then you feel the first set of teeth sink into your leg followed quickly by another clamping down on your right thigh. The wolves fangs penetrate through cloth and leather finding the flesh beneath with practiced precision. Their bite is not a wild frenzied attack of living predators but the calculated assault of creatures that have been trained to capture rather than kill. You struggle against their grip thrashing and pulling with every ounce of strength you possess but these beasts know their craft well. 
They release their hold upon one limb only to immediately clamp down on another working their way methodically up your body with the patience of creatures that have no need to breathe no pulse to quicken with excitement or fatigue. 
Bit by bit they work their way towards your arms and throat each repositioning bringing them closer to their goal of complete immobilisation. Despite your fierce resistance their systematic approach proves devastatingly effective. They do not seek to end your life merely to render you utterly helpless before their masters will.

choices.choiceText: A show for you

title: A show for you
narrativeText:
Pinned beneath the crushing weight of the undead wolves, their powerful jaws clamped immovably around your arms and neck. You can do nothing but watch as a terrible scene unfolds before you. The tall figure in white robes approaches the terrified girl with methodical deliberation, grasping her shoulders with hands that cause the heavy iron shackles to fall away as though they were made of nothing more substantial than morning mist. It lifts her slight form and places her upon the nearby table where fresh restraints snap closed around her limbs with decisive clicks that echo throughout the chamber. From an array of cruel instruments, the figure selects its tools and begins its grim work. Her screams pierce the stale air, each cry more desperate than the last, the sound bouncing off stone walls until the entire chamber seems to vibrate with her anguish. At the height of her suffering, the figure leans close and speaks in a voice like grinding stone. 'The pain is eternal, or do you accept the master's blessing for it to end?' 
Through her agony, she manages a weak acceptance. Without hesitation, the figure reaches for the iron spike suspended above, driving it through her throat in one swift motion. The horrible sucking sound that follows seems to draw her very essence away as black inchor flows into her now lifeless form. Her eyes snap open, no longer her own. 'Another joins, our ranks', the figure intones, then turns its attention towards you. 'And now for another.'

title: Remove the medallion and wrap it around your sword hand and attack
narrativeText: Without hesitation, you tear the medallion from around your neck and wind its chain tightly around your sword hand, the metal links biting into your skin with surprising warmth. The moment the artifact makes contact with your weapon, a surge of something that can only be described as righteous fury courses up the blade's length. The steel itself seems to thrum with newfound power, and you can feel the medallion's anger flowing into the very metal of your sword, transforming it from mere weapon into something far more potent. The undead wolves sense this change immediately, their dead eyes fixing upon you with what might be mistaken for concern if such creatures were still capable of fear. They turn as one to face this new threat, abandoning their stalking approach in favour of a direct assault. The first wolf reaches you with supernatural swiftness, but your enhanced blade meets it with devastating effect. The moment your sword makes contact with the creature's flesh, the result is nothing short of explosive. The wolf's putrid hide ruptures outward from the point of impact, sending fragments of decayed tissue splattering across the chamber floor as the beast collapses in a lifeless heap. Without pause, you continue your motion, rotating on your heel to bring the blade around in a wide arc towards where the second wolf should be. Your instincts prove correct, and once again the explosive contact between blessed steel and unhallowed flesh results in immediate destruction. The sound of the impact echoes off the stone walls like thunder, and the second creature joins its companion in true death at last. The third wolf, perhaps sensing the fate of its pack-mates, attempts to leap over your guard, but its trajectory carries it directly into one of the torture tables with a resounding crash. Seizing the opportunity, you vault atop the same table and grasp the iron spike suspended overhead. With all your strength, you drive the wicked point deep into the creature's skull, and immediately the same horrible sucking sound that you have heard before begins to emanate from the wound. The wolf's unnatural animation drains away like water from a broken vessel, leaving nothing but lifeless carrion behind. Breathing heavily from your exertions, you turn to face the robed figure, who now stands mere metres away. To your horror, you see that it holds the girl aloft, its pale fingers wrapped around her head whilst her feet dangle helplessly in the air. Her eyes are wide with terror and pain, the whites showing clearly in the torchlight. The figure's voice, when it speaks, sounds like millstones grinding grain to dust: 'She is so innocent,' it observes with what might be mistaken for regret. 'You may take her and leave this place if it is your wish.' With a casual gesture of its free hand, the chamber doors swing open with a groaning of ancient hinges, revealing the passage by which you entered this nightmare.

choices.choiceText: Saving one soul from this nightmare would be worth it all, agree
Demand the girl is released and it faces you in combat
State you will leave it if it gives you the key to the final door.
Be damned to this creature you charge it

title: Pull out the medallion and attack the figure
narrativeText: Your fingers fumble frantically at your shirt collar as you reach for the medallion, the fabric rustling with the urgency of your movements. The sound, though slight, carries clearly in the chamber's oppressive silence, and proves to be your undoing. The wolves' ears prick at this telltale whisper of cloth against skin, their dead eyes swiveling towards you with predatory interest. There is no time for subtlety now. You immediately launch yourself forward, medallion clutched in one hand whilst your other grips your sword's familiar weight. Your plan is simple: close the distance before the creatures can react, strike down their master whilst they are still processing your sudden appearance. It is a desperate gambit, but desperation is all you have left. The wolves, however, possess a swiftness that defies their apparent bulk. Moving with coordination that speaks of shared purpose, all three launch themselves at you as one. Their timing is perfect, their trajectory calculated to intercept your charge at precisely the right moment. The impact sends you crashing to the stone floor, your sword spinning away across the chamber to clatter uselessly against the far wall. Before you can even attempt to rise, the stinking maws of the wolves clamp down upon your arms and neck with implacable force. Their breath carries the charnel house stench of decay and worse things, whilst their teeth find the gaps in your clothing with practiced ease. The robed figure approaches your prone form with unhurried steps, its attention focused particularly upon the hand that clutches the medallion. When it speaks, its voice carries a tone of genuine outrage: 'You dare bring that filth into this sacred place?' The words seem to hang in the air like a physical presence. The figure raises one pale hand and barks a single command in a language you do not recognise. The wolf that holds your medallion-bearing wrist responds immediately, beginning to shake its massive head back and forth with violent intensity. The pain is beyond description as ligaments stretch and tear, bone separating from bone with wet, grinding sounds. With a final, savage wrench, your arm is torn completely from your shoulder, the limb cast aside like refuse whilst blood pools beneath you on the ancient stone. The figure looks down at your mutilated form with what might be satisfaction. 'Damaged, but still serviceable,' it muses aloud, its tone clinical and detached. With strength that belies its emaciated appearance, it hoists your weakened body onto the nearest table, where restraints snap shut around what remains of your limbs with mechanical precision. The metallic clink of an instrument being selected reaches your ears, followed by the sensation of cold steel being driven into what remains of your ruined shoulder.

title: Saving one soul from this nightmare would be worth it all, agree
narrativeText: To your astonishment, the robed figure actually honours its word, loosening its grip and allowing the girl to fall heavily to her knees upon the cold stone floor. Her limbs tremble with exhaustion and trauma as she attempts to crawl away from her captor, each movement a visible struggle against the ordeal she has endured. The sound of her ragged breathing fills the chamber as she fights to put distance between herself and the nightmare she has just escaped. When she finally manages to gather enough strength to rise unsteadily to her feet, she stumbles towards you like a drowning person reaching for salvation. You move quickly to support her weakened form, feeling the tremor in her limbs as you guide her backwards toward the chamber's exit. The ancient door begins its slow, inevitable closure, the grinding of stone against stone echoing through the chamber like a funeral dirge. As the gap narrows to mere inches, the figure's voice cuts through the diminishing space with prophetic certainty: 'There will be others, always there will be others who seek what should remain hidden.' The finality of the door sealing shut resonates through the stones beneath your feet, marking the end of this particular horror whilst promising that such evils are never truly finished. You support the girl's weight as you begin the long journey back through the mountain's twisting passages, her survival a small victory against the overwhelming darkness that pervades this accursed place. Together, with your support you both make the journey back to the hamlet, and on arriving her faily run to greet you both weeping with joy at her safe return. The elders give you a pouch of gold and present you with a fine home within the hamlet, a hero to a few, a saviour of one, your tales will be told for generations to come.

title: Demand the girl is released and it faces you in combat
narrativeText: The figure's response is immediate and horrifyingly efficient. 'Release her then,' it states with chilling matter-of-factness, and before you can even begin to process what you have witnessed, its pale hand drives forward with unnatural speed, penetrating through the girl's chest as though flesh and bone offered no more resistance than morning mist. The wet sound of impact echoes sickeningly through the chamber as her eyes widen in shock and pain. The sight shocks you to your very core, but rage quickly overwhelms your horror, and you charge forward with a roar of fury, your sword raised high above your head. The figure calmly withdraws its bloodied hand from the girl's lifeless form and begins muttering words of evil incantation, syllables that seem to burn the very air they pass through. As the arcane incantation reaches its crescendo, the figure's hand becomes wreathed in supernatural flames that dance with colours no earthly fire has ever possessed. With a casual flick of its wrist, a spray of this otherworldly fire erupts towards you, engulfing your form in agony beyond mortal comprehension. This is not the honest flame of torch or hearth, but something far more malevolent, something that burns not just flesh but spirit itself. You collapse to your knees, rolling desperately across the stone floor in a futile attempt to extinguish the impossible fire, but the arcane flames cling to you like liquid hatred, growing stronger rather than weaker with each passing moment. As your movements become increasingly sluggish and your vision begins to dim, the figure's voice reaches you through the haze of supernatural pain: 'There will be more, forever there will be more who think themselves heroes.' Darkness finally claims you as the cursed flames continue their merciless work.

title: State you will leave it if it gives you the key to the final door.
narrativeText: The robed figure studies you with the calculating gaze of a merchant evaluating a particularly interesting proposition, its pale eyes seeming to peer directly into your soul and weigh the contents they find there. When it finally speaks, its voice carries a tone of grudging respect mixed with sardonic amusement. 'Your wish for fortune burns bright within you, and such naked greed is truly admirable in its honesty. But if it is chances you seek rather than certainty, then go and speak with the lord of the mountain himself, for my voice alone holds the key to your desires.' The figure raises one skeletal hand and speaks two words in the ancient tongue, words that seem to resonate through the very stones of the chamber itself. Immediately, you hear the distant groaning of metal and stone as the chamber door begins to swing open behind you, followed by the harsh grating sound of the third and final door opening  in the mountain's bowels. You begin to back slowly towards the exit, never taking your eyes from the figure as you retreat step by careful step. Just as you reach the threshold, the door begins its slow, inexorable closure, but through the narrowing gap you catch one final, horrifying glimpse of the figure's true nature. With casual brutality, it grasps the girl and thrusts her onto the conversion table with practised efficiency, and immediately her screams begin to fill the chamber with sounds that will haunt your dreams for whatever remains of your lifetime. The door seals with a resonant thud, cutting off her cries but unable to erase them from your memory. The silence that follows feels somehow worse than the screaming, for it speaks of horrors completed and souls forever lost to darkness.

choices.choiceText: Enter the final door
You were extremely lucky it's best you leave this place while you still can

title: Be damned to this creature you charge it
narrativeText: Without a moment's hesitation, you explode into motion, launching yourself forward with every ounce of strength and fury you possess. Your sudden decision catches the figure completely off-guard, for it was clearly expecting negotiation rather than immediate violence. Startled by your swift action, it releases its grip on the girl, allowing her to crumple to the floor as it leaps sideways to avoid your descending blade. But your strike proves true despite its evasive manoeuvre, the keen edge of your sword biting deep into the creature's thigh. The moment your steel makes contact with its unnatural flesh, the leg begins to wither and blacken as though your blade carried some form of holy blessing. The figure's inhuman shriek of agony reverberates off the chamber walls as it collapses onto its remaining functional knee, twisting awkwardly to face you whilst clutching at the rapidly deteriorating limb. Through gritted teeth that gleam like old ivory in the torchlight, it hisses words that stop you dead in your tracks: 'If glory and riches are truly what you seek, then you cannot afford to kill me, for I alone possess what you desire.' You pause with your sword raised for what should be the killing blow, every instinct screaming at you to finish this abomination, yet curiosity and greed war against righteous fury in your mind. 'Speak quickly,' you demand, your blade trembling with barely contained violence. The figure's response comes through gasps of pain but carries the weight of absolute truth: 'My voice is the final key that opens the way to treasures beyond imagining. Strike me down, and my death seals that final door forever, leaving all the mountain's riches forever beyond the reach of mortal hands.'

choices.choiceText: Strike it dead and leave with the girl
Strike another limb from the figure and demand it speaks the words

title: Enter the final door
narrativeText: You stand upon the threshold of what can only be described as the mountain's inner sanctum, your heart pounding with the anticipation of one who has come so far through such perilous trials. Before you stretches a wide ceremonial staircase carved from the same dark stone as the rest of this accursed place, each step rising with measured precision towards what appears to be the culmination of your long journey. The craftsmanship speaks of immense age and purpose, as though these stairs were built to accommodate processions of great importance in ages long past. Twenty yards ahead, the stairs terminate at a broad, flat section of flooring, beyond which stand a pair of ornate metal gates that gleam dully in the chamber's ambient light. The gates themselves are works of dark artistry, their iron framework twisted into patterns that seem to shift and writhe when viewed from the corner of your eye. To your left, a smaller opening breaks the chamber's otherwise symmetrical design, its entrance framed by carved stone that suggests it once served some ancillary purpose. As you pause to assess your surroundings, you realise with growing confidence that if anyone had been present in this chamber, the resonant sound of the great door's opening would certainly have alerted them to your presence. The profound silence that greets you speaks either of complete abandonment or of guardians so confident in their security that they feel no need for vigilance. The choice before you is clear: you may attempt to move with stealth towards the side opening to investigate what lies beyond, strain your ears to listen for any sound that might betray hidden dangers lurking in the shadows above, or simply proceed directly to the ornate gates with the bold assumption that you have the chamber to yourself.

choices.choiceText: Try to move silently to the opening
Listen for anything that may alert you to whats up there
Nobody is there, go to the gate

title: You were extremely lucky it's best you leave this place while you still can
narrativeText: The full horror of what you have witnessed within the mountain's depths strikes you with overwhelming force, leaving you shaken to your very core by revelations that no mortal mind was ever meant to process.
