The rational part of your consciousness, the part that has kept you alive through countless adventures and perils, screams with urgent clarity that discretion is not merely the better part of valour in this instance, but quite possibly the only path that leads to continued existence. Your hands shake as you gather what few possessions remain to you, each item feeling strangely heavy as though weighted down by the accumulated evil you have witnessed. With deliberate haste, you make your way back through the twisting passages of Murlore's Spike, each step carrying you further from the unspeakable horrors that lurk in its deepest chambers and closer to the blessed light of the outside world. Even as you emerge into the clean mountain air and feel the sun's warmth upon your face, you cannot escape the knowledge that you carry within you now. The sound of that child's screams has burned itself permanently into your memory, and you know with grim certainty that they will echo through your dreams until the very last day of your natural life. Yet you are alive, which is more than can be said for countless others who have ventured into that accursed place seeking glory and gold. Perhaps survival itself is a form of victory, even if it comes at the cost of innocence lost and sleep forever troubled by the knowledge of what evil truly looks like when it wears a civilised mask.

title: Strike it dead and leave with the girl
narrativeText: Without a moment's hesitation, you bring your blade down in a decisive arc, the steel singing through the torch-lit air of the chamber. The sword cleaves through the figure's remaining flesh and bone, separating its head from its body with a wet, final sound that echoes off the stone walls. The severed head strikes the floor with a dull thud and rolls several feet before coming to rest against the base of a torture table, whilst the figure's body collapses where it kneels, crumpling like a puppet with severed strings. The corpse lies motionless upon the blood-stained stone, its limbs splayed at unnatural angles. The girl, who has been cowering nearby throughout this terrible confrontation, scrambles to her feet with what little strength remains to her. She moves towards you on unsteady legs, her breath coming in ragged gasps, her eyes wide but no longer filled with the absolute terror that had possessed them only moments before. The chamber around you falls silent save for the guttering of torches in their sconces and the girl's laboured breathing. The instruments of torture that line the walls cast long shadows in the flickering light, dark reminders of the horrors that have been perpetrated within these stone confines. You turn your attention to the girl, assessing her condition. She is weak, visibly shaken by her ordeal, but she is alive and the immediate threat has been eliminated. Together, you begin to make your way towards the chamber door, your lantern held high to light the path ahead. She leans heavily upon your arm for support, each step requiring careful attention as you navigate around the various implements and furnishings that clutter the torture chamber's floor. The doorway ahead promises escape from this place of suffering, leading back into the passages that will eventually return you both to the surface and the world beyond this cursed mountain.

choices.choiceText: A soul saved

title: A Soul Saved
narrativeText: The long journey back through the mountain's treacherous passages feels like a pilgrimage from darkness into light, each step carrying you and the girl further from the unspeakable horrors you have witnessed and closer to a world where mercy still exists. Your companion remains weak from her ordeal, leaning heavily upon your strength, but there is something in her eyes now that was not there before, a spark of hope that had been all but extinguished by her captivity. The path down from the mountain seems easier somehow, as though the ancient stones themselves are eager to speed your departure from their cursed embrace. When the familiar sights of the hamlet finally come into view, the girl beside you begins to weep, not from sorrow but from the overwhelming relief of one who had never expected to see home again. The reunion with her family transcends anything you have ever witnessed, their joy so profound and genuine that it seems to illuminate the very air around them. Parents, siblings, and extended family surround her with embraces and tears of thanksgiving, their gratitude towards you expressed in words that seem inadequate to convey the depth of their emotion. The village elders, when they hear the full tale of your journey and the dangers you faced, look upon you with something approaching reverence. Their offer comes not as a surprise but as a recognition of something that has fundamentally changed within you during your time in the mountain's depths. They ask if you would consider accepting the role of warden to these mountains, guardian of the high passes and protector of all who dwell in the shadow of the peaks. In that moment of decision, you understand that your quest has transformed you in ways far more profound than mere material gain ever could. You came to these mountains seeking gold and riches, treasure that would secure your future and make your fortune. Instead, you have discovered something infinitely more precious: the forging of your true character, the transformation of a simple adventurer into something approaching legend. Your name will be remembered not for the wealth you claimed, but for the lives you saved and the courage you showed when darkness threatened to overwhelm the light.

title: Strike another limb from the figure and demand it speaks the words
narrativeText: With a roar of frustrated rage that echoes off the chamber walls, you bring your blade down upon the figure's remaining functional leg, the steel biting through unnatural flesh with the same devastating effect as before. The limb immediately begins to wither and blacken, the corruption spreading up from the wound like some form of divine retribution made manifest. The figure's screams reach a new pitch of agony as it finds itself completely immobilised, reduced to a writhing torso that can do nothing but lie helpless upon the cold stone floor. You stand over its prone form, your sword point pressed against its throat, and make your demand with the authority of one who holds absolute power over life and death: 'Speak the words that will open the doors, or I shall continue to remove pieces of you until there is nothing left but your capacity to suffer.' The figure's response begins as another shriek of pain, but gradually transforms into something far more chilling, a sound that might charitably be called laughter. When it finally speaks, there is something almost triumphant in its voice: 'Very well, you have left me little choice in the matter.' It utters a single word in the ancient tongue, but rather than the sound of opening doors that you expected to hear, you are greeted instead by the grinding slam of the torture chamber's exit sealing itself shut. The figure's laughter grows stronger now, fed by your evident dismay: 'Then end me if you must, foolish mortal, but know that in doing so you seal your own tomb! You have played directly into my hands, and now we shall see who truly understands the nature of power in this place.' The realisation of your situation settles over you like a burial shroud, as you understand that the figure has outmanoeuvred you completely, turning your very aggression into the instrument of your own destruction.

choices.choiceText: Accept it has outwitted you and strike the final blow
Drag it to a torture table to extract the words
Push your medallion into it's forehead and demand obedience

title: Accept it has outwitted you and strike the final blow
narrativeText: The full scope of your predicament becomes clear as you survey the sealed chamber, the weight of the figure's final gambit settling upon you like a physical burden. There is no way forward now, no path that leads to escape or redemption, for the creature has locked you both within this chamber of horrors with no possibility of egress. Yet as you contemplate your situation, a different kind of resolve begins to build within your chest, one that speaks not of defeat but of a deeper understanding of what heroism truly means. You look down at the mutilated figure writhing at your feet and realise that whilst it may have trapped your body, it cannot imprison your soul or corrupt the righteousness of your purpose. With grim determination, you raise your sword high above your head and speak words that ring with absolute conviction: 'You may have outwitted me in the matter of our escape, but you shall not be permitted to continue your evil work upon others who might follow.' The blade falls with all the force of your righteous anger behind it, separating the creature's head from its shoulders in one clean stroke that echoes through the chamber like a bell tolling the end of an age. As the thing's unnatural life finally ebbs away, you turn to look upon the girl who has been caught up in this tragedy through no fault of her own. Together, you are trapped in this place of death and darkness, with no hope of rescue and no possibility of escape. Yet there is a strange peace in knowing that you have struck a blow against the forces of evil that will resonate far beyond these walls. The land above is safer now for your sacrifice, even if the world will never know the price that was paid for its security. In time, perhaps others will discover what transpired here and understand the true meaning of courage in the face of insurmountable odds. For now, silence serves as your epitaph, but it is the silence of heroes who chose duty over survival, and honour over escape.

title: Drag it to a torture table to extract the words
narrativeText: Grasping the figure by its remaining limbs, you drag its writhing form across the stone floor towards the nearest torture table, the same apparatus that has witnessed so much suffering within these walls. Despite its injuries, the creature proves surprisingly resistant to your efforts, its supernatural strength making even this simple task more difficult than it should be. When you finally manage to hoist it onto the table and secure its remaining limbs with the iron restraints, you expect to see fear or at least concern in its pale eyes. Instead, the figure begins to laugh, a sound so utterly devoid of human warmth that it seems to drain the very heat from the air around you. When it finally speaks, its voice carries the casual confidence of one who holds all the advantages despite appearing to be at your mercy. 'Your imagination serves you poorly if you think there is anything in this chamber capable of causing me sufficient pain to compel my cooperation,' it says with obvious amusement. 'You may choose to end my existence or attempt to torture me into compliance, but both options will provide me with equal entertainment. I have experienced agonies that would drive mortal minds to madness, and I have inflicted sufferings upon others that dwarf anything you might devise in your limited creativity. Do your worst, little hero, and discover how inadequate your threats truly are against one who has already transcended the boundaries between life and death.' The casual dismissal in its voice makes it clear that you have entered a contest where your opponent holds advantages you cannot even begin to comprehend, let alone counter effectively.

choices.choiceText: Pull the metal spike down and thrust it into its throat
Push your medallion into it's forehead and demand obedience

title: Push your medallion into it's forehead and demand obedience
narrativeText: Drawing the medallion from around your neck, you press the warm metal directly against the figure's pale forehead, feeling the artifact pulse with sudden, intense heat as it makes contact with the creature's unnatural flesh. The effect is immediate and devastating. The figure's inhuman shriek of pure agony echoes off the chamber walls as waves of what can only be described as concentrated hatred flow from the medallion into its very being. The metal grows so hot in your grasp that you can smell your own flesh beginning to burn, yet you maintain your grip with iron determination. Through gritted teeth, you deliver your ultimatum with all the authority of one who commands divine power: 'The pain you feel now is eternal and unending, but accept my blessing and speak the words to open the doors, and I will grant you the mercy of a swift end!' The figure's response is beyond anything you could have imagined, its body convulsing with spasms that seem to originate from the very core of its being. For long minutes, you maintain the contact despite the agony it causes your own flesh, watching as the creature writhes in torment that clearly exceeds even its considerable capacity for suffering. Finally, when its screams reach a crescendo that threatens to burst your eardrums, it manages to gasp out two words in the ancient tongue. Immediately, you hear the grinding sound of the chamber door swinging open, followed by the distant crash of the final door yielding to the same compulsion. 'Acceptance,' you state with grim satisfaction, and bring your sword down upon its neck with a stroke that severs head from shoulders cleanly. Before your eyes, the decapitated figure begins to shrivel and collapse, its flesh dissolving into the same black ichor that stains so many surfaces in this chamber of horrors. The evil that animated it has finally been purged by the righteous power of your medallion, leaving nothing behind but the memory of justice served and a pathway to freedom finally opened.

choices.choiceText: Take the girl to the main hall and tell her how to get home
The final door is open but the girl may not survive the journey home. Leave this place

title: Take the girl to the main hall and tell her how to get home
narrativeText: With infinite care and patience, you guide the girl through the mountain's twisting passages towards the main chamber, her weakened condition requiring frequent pauses for rest along the way. Each step seems to cost her tremendous effort, the trauma of her ordeal having drained not just her physical strength but her spiritual reserves as well. When you finally reach the main chamber, the sight of the open final door seems to restore some small measure of hope to her hollow eyes, as though the promise of escape makes survival seem possible once again. Recognising that she will need every advantage you can provide for the perilous journey ahead, you share some of your remaining provisions with her, watching as she attempts to eat despite her obviously damaged state. The water you offer seems to help more than the food, and you make certain she drinks as much as her weakened stomach can manage. Knowing that the path down the mountain will be treacherous for one in her condition, you carefully describe the route she should take, pointing out landmarks and warning her of the specific dangers she is likely to encounter. Your instructions are detailed and precise, for you understand that her survival may depend upon remembering every word you speak in this moment. When you finish your explanation, she manages to speak for the first time since her rescue, her voice barely above a whisper but carrying genuine gratitude: 'Thank you for giving me back my life. I know the path will be difficult, but knowing that someone cared enough to save me gives me strength I thought I had lost forever.' Her heartfelt words of blessing follow you as you make your own choice about what comes next, whether to accompany her to safety or pursue whatever awaits beyond the final door.

choices.choiceText: Enter the final door

title: Pull the metal spike down and thrust it into its throat
narrativeText: Looking up towards the chamber's vaulted ceiling, you spot the iron spike suspended directly above the torture table, its wicked point gleaming dully in the torchlight. With desperate determination, you reach up and grasp the metal implement, feeling its weight and balance as you prepare to turn the figure's own instrument of evil against its creator. The spike feels warm to the touch, as though heated by the countless acts of horror it has witnessed, but you force yourself to ignore the nauseating implications and focus solely upon the task at hand. In one swift motion, you drive the wicked point deep into the figure's throat, watching as its eyes widen with what might actually be genuine horror for the first time since you entered this accursed chamber. The moment the spike penetrates its flesh, that familiar and terrible sucking sound begins to emanate from the wound, the same ghastly noise that has accompanied so many deaths in this place of suffering. You can actually see the unnatural life being drawn out of the creature, its essence flowing into the spike like water into a drain. Through the horrible wet sounds of its dissolution, you hear it attempt to speak, managing only a single, barely comprehensible word that gurgles out past the metal lodged in its throat before it expires completely. As the last traces of animation leave the figure's form, the chamber door swings open with a grinding of ancient mechanisms, the sound of freedom made manifest in stone and metal. You turn immediately to check on the girl, finding her collapsed but breathing, tears streaming down her face as she realises that her nightmare has finally ended. 'Thank you,' she manages to whisper through her sobs, the simple words carrying more gratitude than the most eloquent speech could convey. Looking at her weakened condition and considering your own exhaustion, you realise that whilst you have achieved a great victory against evil, and the door to the chamber is open, the final door reamains closed, although you cannot continue, you can save the girl.

choices.choiceText: Take her home

title: Grab a flensing knife as an additional weapon
narrativeText: You approach the nearest table where an array of wicked implements lies arranged with methodical precision. Your fingers close around the handle of what appears to be a flensing knife, its blade honed to razor sharpness and still bearing dark stains from previous use. The weight of the weapon feels substantial in your grip, its balance suggesting a tool designed for terrible efficiency rather than mere intimidation. As you lift the blade from the table's surface, a sound reaches your ears that chills your blood more than any sight in this chamber of horrors: the unmistakable whimper of human suffering, weak and desperate, emanating from somewhere near one of the torture tables. The sound speaks of pain beyond endurance, of a soul pushed to the very limits of what flesh and spirit can bear. Even as this pitiful cry fades to silence, a distinct mechanical sound echoes through the chamber from the direction you entered, the heavy clunk of the main door closing with deliberate finality. This is not the sound of a trap springing shut, but rather the measured closure of someone entering the chamber with purpose. The timing suggests that whoever approaches has their own business in this accursed place, business that may not bode well for any unfortunate souls already present. The knife feels heavier in your hand now, no longer merely a potential weapon but perhaps your only means of defence against whatever dark purpose brings the chamber's master to his domain. You stand poised between two equally pressing demands upon your attention: the desperate need to investigate the source of that pitiful whimpering, knowing that another human soul suffers nearby, and the equally urgent requirement to prepare yourself for the arrival of whatever malevolent force now approaches through the sealed entrance.

choices.choiceText: Investigate the sound
Draw your sword and prepare for what happens next

title: Sit in the chair to get better perspective
narrativeText: The throne-like chair beckons with promises of a commanding view over the entire chamber, and you settle yourself into its carved embrace, hoping to gain some strategic advantage through elevation and perspective. The stone feels surprisingly warm beneath you, as though it has recently been occupied, and the arms of the chair are carved into shapes that your fingers recognise as grasping claws even before your eyes confirm their form. For a brief moment, you survey the scene spread before you with the detached observation of a military commander studying a battlefield, taking note of exits, obstacles, and potential defensive positions. Then the trap reveals itself with mechanical precision. Metal restraints, previously hidden within the chair's carved recesses, snap shut around your ankles with the sound of closing mantraps. Similar bonds clamp down upon your arms before you can even begin to react, whilst a collar of cold iron closes around your throat with enough pressure to restrict your breathing without completely cutting off your air supply. The craftsmanship is diabolical in its efficiency, each restraint positioned to render struggle not merely futile but actively painful. From somewhere behind the throne comes a low, rumbling growl that speaks of hunger and predatory intelligence. The sound multiplies, becoming a chorus of such growls as three undead wolves emerge from the shadows, their dead eyes reflecting what little light filters through the chamber. They move with the confident gait of creatures that know their prey is already secured, their massive forms positioning themselves where you can see them clearly but cannot hope to reach them. Following in their wake comes a tall figure draped in white robes that might once have been pristine but are now stained with substances you prefer not to identify. When the figure speaks, its voice carries the authority of one long accustomed to having its words obeyed without question: 'Another visitor, just as there always will be others who come seeking what they should not seek.' Its pale hands gesture towards one of the torture tables with casual familiarity, drawing your horrified attention to a girl who lies crumpled on the stone floor beside it, her form barely moving with the shallow breathing of one who clings to life by the thinnest of threads.

choices.choiceText: Learn the secrets of the chamber

title: Investigate behind the throne
narrativeText: The mysterious sound can wait, for the ornate box positioned behind the throne commands your immediate attention with its promise of hidden treasures. Your curiosity proves stronger than your caution as you approach the container, noting the intricate workmanship of its metal fittings and the precious stones inlaid into its wooden surface. Such craftsmanship speaks of contents valuable enough to warrant this level of protection, and your imagination fills with possibilities of gold, gems, or perhaps even more exotic prizes. The lock, however, proves to be a masterwork of security in its own right. Your fingers work at the mechanism with practiced skill, but the internal workings resist every attempt at manipulation. The complexity of the device suggests that whoever created this box possessed knowledge of security measures far beyond what one might expect in such a place. Minutes pass as you struggle with the stubborn lock, your attention completely focused upon the puzzle before you, your awareness of your surroundings narrowing to nothing beyond the immediate challenge of gaining entry to this tantalising mystery. It is precisely this focused attention that proves to be your downfall. When you finally abandon your efforts and turn to locate the source of the earlier sound, you discover that the three undead wolves have approached you with the silent stealth that only the dead can achieve. They stand arranged in a perfect semicircle around your position, cutting off any hope of retreat, whilst behind them looms the tall figure in white robes, its presence somehow making the very air feel colder. The figure's voice, when it speaks, carries a tone of genuine amusement: 'Greed, beautiful greed, brings me yet another visitor, just as there always will be others who cannot resist the lure of forbidden treasure.' Its pale hand rises, and it speaks a single word in a language that predates human civilisation. The response is immediate and overwhelming. All three wolves launch themselves at you with perfect coordination, their combined weight driving you flat against the stone floor before you can even begin to raise your hands in defence. Their powerful jaws close around your neck and arms with the mechanical precision of well-oiled traps, and they begin to drag your helpless form across the chamber towards where their master has taken his position beside a torture table. There, illuminated by the flickering torchlight, you see a girl lying shackled to the floor, her eyes wide with the particular terror of one who has witnessed horrors beyond human comprehension. The figure looks down at both of you with evident satisfaction. 'A show for you both,' it declares with the tone of a gracious host welcoming guests to an entertainment.

choices.choiceText: A show for you

title: Stay hidden and observe
narrativeText: Wisdom overcomes the impulse to action, and you press yourself deeper into the concealing shadows, forcing yourself to remain motionless despite every instinct screaming at you to intervene. Your breathing becomes shallow and controlled, each exhale carefully measured to avoid the slightest sound that might betray your presence to the creatures below. The stone floor beneath you feels cold against your chest and legs, whilst your muscles begin to ache from maintaining your rigid position, but you dare not shift even slightly for fear of discovery. From your hidden vantage point, you watch with growing horror as four figures approach the captive girl with the methodical purpose of those who have performed this ritual many times before. Three of the figures are the undead wolves you have encountered before, their movements coordinated and purposeful as they form a loose perimeter around the torture table. The fourth figure, tall and draped in white robes that billow slightly in the chamber's stagnant air, moves with the authority of one who commands absolutely and expects to be obeyed without question. The robed figure reaches down and lifts the girl with surprising gentleness, almost as though handling something precious and fragile. At his touch, her shackles fall away with a series of metallic clanks that echo off the stone walls like funeral bells. She does not resist, cannot resist, as he repositions her upon a different table, one equipped with its own set of restraints that snap shut around her limbs with the sound of closing coffin lids. The figure moves to a nearby array of instruments, selecting one with the care of an artisan choosing the proper tool for delicate work. What follows defies description, for the human mind recoils from witnessing such deliberate infliction of suffering. Her scream, when it finally comes, is pure agony given voice, a sound that seems to tear through the very fabric of the world itself. The cry echoes from the chamber walls until it seems to come from everywhere at once, building upon itself until the air itself seems to vibrate with her pain.

choices.choiceText: Charge the group before it can hurt her further
Use her pain to hide your movements and sneak up on them
Once they have finished with her they will leave and you can continue

title: Once they have finished with her they will leave and you can continue
narrativeText: The terrible logic of survival overrides every decent impulse in your heart as you force yourself to remain hidden, telling yourself that intervention would only result in two deaths instead of one, that preservation of your own life serves some greater purpose that justifies this moment of cowardice. You watch through fingers pressed against your eyes, unable to look away entirely yet unable to bear witness to the full horror of what unfolds below. The tall figure works with methodical precision, its movements speaking of long practice in the art of inflicting suffering whilst keeping its victim alive and conscious throughout the ordeal. Each instrument is selected with care, applied with calculated pressure designed to maximise pain whilst avoiding any injury that might bring merciful unconsciousness or death. Her screaming escalates beyond what you thought possible for human vocal cords to produce, becoming a sound that transcends mere noise to become something that strikes directly at the soul itself. The echoes bounce from wall to wall until the entire chamber seems to vibrate with her anguish. At the crescendo of this symphony of suffering, the figure leans down close to her face, its voice cutting clearly through her hysteria: 'You may choose for the pain to continue eternally, never diminishing, never ending, or you may accept the master's blessing and be freed from pain forever. What is your choice?' Through her shrieks of agony, you hear her broken voice crying out her acceptance of whatever salvation is being offered, her words barely recognisable as human speech through the damage done to her throat. The figure nods with what might be satisfaction and reaches upward to grasp the iron spike suspended above the table. With one swift, practised motion, it drives the wicked point down through her throat. The word 'Acceptance' falls from its lips like a benediction as the horrible sucking sound begins. You watch in fascinated revulsion as the very life force seems to drain from her body, only to be replaced by something far darker. Black ichor flows like liquid shadow into her corpse, filling the spaces where blood and breath once sustained life. Her eyes snap open, but they are no longer her own, no longer human. The sight proves too much for your stomach to bear, and you find yourself retching uncontrollably, the sound of your distress echoing through the chamber despite your desperate attempts to muffle it. When you finally regain control of yourself, gasping and wiping bile from your lips, you discover that three pairs of dead eyes are staring directly at you. The wolves have surrounded your hiding place with the silent efficiency of seasoned hunters, and as one they leap upon your prone form, driving you flat against the stone floor. From the direction of the torture table comes a voice like grinding millstones: 'Another visitor, just as there always will be others who cannot resist the call of this sacred place.'

title: The Guarded Gate
narrativeText: The moment your boot touches the threshold of the chamber, the figure that you had initially mistaken for another piece of ancient debris springs into motion with startling vitality. What rises before you is unmistakably an undead warrior, its form preserved by the same dark forces that permeate this mountain's depths. The creature's armour, though corroded by countless years of service, still bears the distinctive markings of its original rank and station, whilst the twin blades it wields gleam with an edge that speaks of supernatural maintenance. When it speaks, its voice carries the grinding quality of stone against bone, yet the words are clear and purposeful: 'You seek passage to the sacred sanctum beyond, state your identity and purpose, that I might determine whether you are worthy to proceed or die.' The way it phrases this challenge makes it clear that this is not merely a formality but a test with potentially fatal consequences for those who answer incorrectly. The creature's bearing suggests that it retains the discipline and intelligence it possessed in life, transformed but not diminished by whatever dark ritual granted it continued existence. Its eyes, glowing with an eldritch light that seems to pierce through flesh and bone to examine your very soul, await your response with the patience of one who has all eternity to complete its duties. The weight of decision settles upon you as you realise that your next words may determine not only whether you gain passage to whatever lies beyond, but whether you leave this chamber alive at all.

choices.choiceText: There's no way you know what to tell it draw your sword and fight it
Tell it that the Gaoler sent you with a message
Tell it that the priest sent you with a conversion report of the girl
Tell it you are lost and need directions

title: There's no way you know what to tell it draw your sword and fight it
narrativeText: You draw your sword, and the guard begins to advance. You must decide whether to back out to the landing for more space or immediately advance and engage it.

choices.choiceText: Back out to the landing for more space
Immediately advance and engage

title: Tell it that the Gaoler sent you with a message
narrativeText: Your words strike the undead guardian like a physical blow, and instantly its hollow features contort with inhuman rage. 'Hideous lie!' it shrieks, the sound echoing through the stone chamber like grinding metal. Its voice rises to a crescendo of fury as it continues, 'Gaoler does not know the word!' The creature's ancient armour clanks and rattles as it surges forward, twin blades gleaming wickedly in the dim torchlight. The metallic scrape of steel against stone fills the air as the weapons drag briefly across the floor before rising into ready position. The guardian's advance is relentless, each footstep a thunderous impact that reverberates through the stone beneath your feet..

choices.choiceText: Back out and prepare to meet it in open space
Advance and engage it

title: Tell it that the priest sent you with a conversion report of the girl
narrativeText: The undead guard regards you with hollow sockets that somehow still convey malicious amusement. From its throat comes a sound like dried leaves scraping against stone, a mockery of human laughter that echoes off the chamber walls. The rasping voice that follows carries the stench of decay, barely audible above the ambient dripping from somewhere in the darkness beyond. 'The girl is ours?' it asks, each word drawn out with deliberate, unsettling emphasis. The creature's head tilts at an unnatural angle, waiting for your response whilst its bony fingers drum a slow, rhythmic tattoo against the pommel of its weapon.

choices.choiceText: Answer yes the girl is ours
Tell it no, the priest did not turn her and she is dead

title: Tell it you are lost and need directions
narrativeText: The guard regards you with its eyeless sockets, and you can almost sense the gears of thought turning within its decayed skull. Long seconds pass in uncomfortable silence, punctuated only by the sound of your own breathing echoing off the stone walls. Finally, the creature speaks in its rasping voice: 'We need a new Gaoler and high priest.' The words hang in the air like a death sentence, and suddenly you understand that your ruse has not merely failed, but has revealed something far more dangerous about this place. Before you can process the full implications of its statement, the guard moves with shocking swiftness. Its sudden rush catches you completely off guard, powerful hands seizing you and hurling you backward through the doorway. Your body crashes into the mystical gate behind you, but instead of solid impact, you feel yourself sinking into its surface as though it were deep marsh mud. The barrier hardens around you instantly, holding you fast in its supernatural grip. The guard steps closer, and methodically begins to strike your trapped head with its bony fists until consciousness abandons you.

choices.choiceText: sealed fate

title: Back out and prepare to meet it in open space
narrativeText: You step backwards through the guardroom's threshold, your boots scraping against the worn stone as you seek the tactical advantage of open ground. The undead warrior follows with measured determination, its twin blades beginning a hypnotic rotation that transforms them into gleaming scythes of death. The whistling sound of steel cutting through air builds to an ominous crescendo. Without warning, the creature's motion changes, its arms snapping forward with inhuman speed. Both blades fly through the air towards you like deadly missiles. Your sword rises in desperate defence, successfully deflecting the first spinning weapon with a resounding clash of metal. The second blade, however, finds its mark. The impact against your shoulder sends shockwaves of agony through your body, the force sufficient to hurl you backwards onto the unforgiving stone floor. Your sword arm falls useless to your side as the guardian closes the distance with terrible purpose. Heavy blows rain down upon your head with mechanical precision. As darkness begins to claim your vision, the creature's grinding voice reaches your fading consciousness: 'We need a new Gaoler and a new high priest'.

choices.choiceText: A sealed fate

title: Advance and engage it
narrativeText: The guardroom's confines work against you, its narrow width constraining your movement whilst the high ceiling seems to mock your earthbound limitations. You surge forward to meet the undead warrior, your blade singing through the air in a determined attack. The creature responds with a display of supernatural skill, its twin weapons whirling in perfect synchronisation to create an impenetrable barrier of gleaming steel. The sound of spinning metal fills the chamber like the drone of angry hornets. Your sword arcs downward in a powerful strike aimed at the guardian's skull, but the creature shifts with fluid grace. Your blade meets one of its spinning weapons in a shower of sparks, the impact jarring your arms and sending your sword skittering across the stone floor. The metallic clatter echoes mockingly as your primary weapon slides beyond reach. The guardian advances with methodical certainty, forcing you to take stumbling steps backwards. In this moment of desperation, you remember the medallion resting against your chest, though it remains frustratingly inert, offering no warmth or sign of power when you need it most.

choices.choiceText: Pull out your medallion and demand it halt
Retreat to the open area

title: Yes
narrativeText: The guard's voice rises to an exultant shriek that reverberates through the stone passages. 'Fresh blessing! I must see the remnants of its pain!' The words seem to energise the creature, and you hear the scrape of metal against stone as it shifts its stance. You tell it that she waits at the bottom of the stairs, requiring permission to enter. The guard's eagerness is palpable now, you can hear the quickening shuffle of its movements and the excited clicking of bone against bone as it prepares to depart. Without further hesitation, it moves past you towards the stairs, its footsteps creating hollow echoes that fade into the depths below.

choices.choiceText: Strike it from behind
Charge it to knock it over

title: Tell it no, the priest did not turn her and she is dead
narrativeText: The undead guard fixes its hollow gaze upon you, and for a moment the chamber falls silent save for the distant dripping of moisture on ancient stone. When it speaks, its voice carries a note of cruel satisfaction that cuts through the stale air. 'She may be dead, but you are not!' The words seem to energise the creature, and before you can react to the obvious threat, it explodes into motion with terrifying speed. Your feet barely have time to shift backward before the guard's charge connects, its bony shoulder driving into your chest with crushing force. The impact hurls you backward through the doorway, your body sailing helplessly through the air until you collide with the mystical barrier behind you. To your horror, instead of striking solid iron, you sink into the gate's surface as though it were thick, viscous mud. The magical barrier hardens instantly around your form, trapping you completely within its supernatural grasp. The guard approaches slowly now, savouring its victory, and begins to rain down blows upon your trapped head until darkness mercifully claims you.

choices.choiceText: A sealed fate

title: sealed fate
narrativeText: Consciousness returns slowly, accompanied by the sharp bite of cold metal against your wrists and ankles. Your head throbs with each heartbeat, and as your vision clears, you realise you are secured to a broad stone table in the torture halls, above your head, one of the hollow iron spikes. You are completely shackled here, and your first thought is that there is no priest now to do anything to you, leaving you wondering what may happen. A shodow crosses your vison and the undead guard looms over you, but something has changed in your time unconscious. The creature now wears flowing white robes instead of its warrior's garb, and there is a ceremonial quality to its bearing that was absent before. 'The lord has taught me the words,' it intones with reverence, its hollow voice carrying new authority. With deliberate ceremony, it places what appears to be the crystal gate key beside your bound hands, close enough that you can feel the cold crystal surface against your fingers. 'This will be yours shortly,' the creature continues, its words heavy with dark promise, 'You are my first, I will make mistakes, I will learn, you will be blessed'. The sound that follows, metal being drawn slowly across stone in preparation, leaves no doubt about your impending fate.

title: Strike it from behind
narrativeText:
The undead guard walks past you towards the stairway with eager anticipatory steps. Its attention fixed entirely upon the promised sight of a freshly converted soul, waiting below. You recognise this fleeting moment as perhaps your only chance to eliminate the creature before it discovers your deception. Moving with absolute silence, you draw your sword from its scabbard, the familiar weight settling into your grip as you position yourself behind the unsuspecting guardian. With all the force you can muster, you thrust the blade forward, driving the steel deep into the guardians back, precisely where a living being's heart would beat within a chest. 
The sword penetrates cleanly through the ancient armor and desiccated flesh, the tip emerging from the creature's torso in a spray of dust and fragments of dried tissue. 
For a heart beat, you allow yourself to believe the strike has succeeded. Then understanding crashes down upon you with terrible clarity. The guard was already dead, had been dead for untold years and possesses no heart to pierce, no vital organs whose destruction might end its unnatural existence. 
The creature twists round with shocking speed, its movements fluid despite its blade still being lodged in its torso and wrenches your sword from your grasp with such force that you feel the bones in your wrists grind painfully together. 
Now you stand defenseless before an enraged, undead warrior. Trapped in the narrow guard room with no weapon and no avenue of escape. The guard begins its retaliation immediately. Both of its ancient blades rising and falling with mechanical precision. You feel the cold steel tearing into your flesh again and again. Each strike driving you backwards, each impact, sending waves of agony through your body. 
The sensations begin to fade as blood loss takes its inevitable toll, consciousness slipping away like water through cupped hands. The last thing you perceive, before darkness claims you entirely, is the cold stone floor rising to meet your face.

title: Charge it to knock it over
narrativeText: You surge forward with all your strength, your shoulder connecting with the creature's spine between its shoulder blades. The impact sends a jarring shock up your arm, but your momentum proves sufficient. The guard hurtles forward into the mystical barrier with tremendous force. To your astonishment, instead of striking solid stone, the creature simply disappears into the gate's surface as though it were plunging into thick, viscous mud. The magical barrier hardens instantly around the guard's form, trapping its entire left side within the now-solid surface. Only its right arm and head remain free, thrashing wildly. The creature's howls of rage and frustration fill the chamber, a sound that seems to shake dust from the ancient stones above.

choices.choiceText: The noise will alert others kill it
Demand it tell you how to get past the gate and you will release it

title: The noise will alert others kill it
narrativeText: Taking careful aim to avoid contact with the treacherous gate surface, you raise your blade high above your head. The sword descends in a clean arc, meeting little resistance as it passes through the creature's decayed neck. The guard's head tumbles to the stone floor with a wet thud, whilst its body slumps forward, finally still. Dark fluid seeps from the severed neck, creating a spreading pool on the flagstones. Now you face a choice that may determine your path forward through this cursed place. You could search the completely lifeless remains for a key that might unlock your passage, or investigate the guardroom behind you for some mechanism to bypass the mystical barrier.

choices.choiceText: Examine the completely dead guard for a key
Search the guardroom for a way to open the gate

title: Demand it tell you how to get past the gate and you will release it
narrativeText: The guard raises its head with considerable effort, the movement accompanied by the grinding of bone against stone. For several long moments it regards you in silence, as though weighing your offer against some internal calculation. Finally, its voice rasps out like wind through dry grass: 'Very well, in there, bring the stone'. You return to the guardroom, your footsteps echoing in the confined space. At first glance the chamber appears barren, but as you turn towards the entrance, your eyes fall upon a carved shelf set into the wall beside the door. Upon this shelf rests a remarkable object: a large, perfectly spherical stone of deep purple hue, so translucent that light seems to move within its depths like trapped lightning. As you lift it carefully from its resting place, you discover intricate golden runes inscribed around its circumference, and in its centre, a depression shaped precisely like a human handprint.

choices.choiceText: Put your hand on the markings
Take the stone to the guard

title: Examine the completely dead guard for a key
narrativeText: You walk over to the guard, carefully avoiding contact with the gate. You peer around the body and shift bits of armour. You find nothing. You must now go and look in the guard room.

choices.choiceText: Go and look in the guard room

title: Search the guardroom for a way to open the gate
narrativeText: You step across the threshold into the guardroom, your footfalls muffled by centuries of accumulated dust. The chamber stands empty, devoid of furniture or obvious mechanisms. Disappointed, you begin to turn back towards the passage when something catches your eye. Carved directly into the stone wall beside the entrance is a shallow shelf, cunningly worked to appear as natural erosion unless viewed from precisely this angle. Upon this hidden shelf sits an extraordinary stone sphere, deep purple in colour and translucent as the finest crystal. When you carefully lift it from its resting place, the stone feels surprisingly warm in your palms. Golden arcane runes flow around its circumference in elegant script, whilst at its centre lies a depression shaped exactly like a human hand, complete with ridges for each finger and thumb.

choices.choiceText: Place your hand on the hand arking

title: Put your hand on the markings
narrativeText: You gently put your hand on the marking, not knowing what to expect. Nothing happens. The guard's head, twisted back to see, says, 'Bring to me, you don't have the Lord's approval'.

choices.choiceText: Take it to the guard
Approach the guard and demand it tells you how to use it.

title: Take the stone to the guard
narrativeText: You approach the trapped guard cautiously, your sword held at the ready. A swift strike with your blade's pommel knocks the creature's weapon from its grasp, the metal clattering as it skitters across the stone floor into the darkness. The guard regards the mystical stone in your hands with something approaching desperation. 'I have no choices,' it whines, its voice carrying a note of genuine anguish beneath the usual sepulchral tones. With trembling fingers, it places its skeletal hand upon the stone's surface. You feel rather than see the creature twist its wrist slowly to the left, and immediately the stone grows warm in your grip. The gate before you begins to vibrate, a deep thrumming that you feel in your bones. Then, like heavy curtains being drawn apart, the barrier begins to separate, revealing the passage beyond as twin sheets of flowing, liquid stone. But as the opening widens, the guard loses its balance and tumbles backward, the precious stone still clutched in its bony grasp.

choices.choiceText: Grab the stone
Try to dive through the gate
Strike the guard with your sword

title: Approach the guard and demand it tells you how to use it.
narrativeText: It rasps short breaths, a laugh, of sorts. 'You do not have the lord's permission, bring it to me, I will free us both'.

choices.choiceText: With no other choice, take it to the guard

title: Grab the stone
narrativeText: You lunge forward desperately, your fingers outstretched towards the falling stone. But the guard's hand has already slipped away from the mystical markings, and the magical barrier responds instantly to the broken connection. The gate snaps shut with tremendous force, catching your upper arm as it solidifies. Pain shoots through your shoulder as stone that moments before was fluid as water now grips your limb like the strongest iron shackles. The guard, no longer trapped, pulls itself upright and regards you with what might be contemplation if dead things could truly think. Its hollow voice carries an almost thoughtful quality as it speaks: 'We need a new Gaoler and high priest.' You watch in growing alarm as the creature retrieves one of its fallen blades. The last thing you see before darkness claims you is the weapon's pommel descending towards your skull.

choices.choiceText: A fate sealed

title: Try to dive through the gate
narrativeText: Without hesitation, you dive towards the widening opening in the mystical barrier. Your hands close around the falling guard's form, and using your momentum, you pivot in a complete turn whilst pulling the creature with you. The motion accelerates your body through the gap just as the guard is thrust back through the barrier to its original position. The moment the creature's hand loses contact with the stone, the gate snaps shut with a sound like thunder, trapping the guard once more within the magical surface. Its howls of renewed rage and frustration echo through both chambers, the sound growing increasingly frantic. Recognising that such noise will surely attract unwanted attention, you quickly draw your sword and bring it down in a clean stroke, severing the creature's head from its shoulders. Blessed silence falls over the passage, broken only by the sound of your own breathing.

choices.choiceText: Continue onward

title: Strike the guard with your sword
narrativeText: Your blade finds its mark with deadly precision, cutting cleanly through the guard's rotted neck. The creature's head separates from its shoulders and drops to the stone floor with a dull thud, whilst dark ichor spatters the flagstones. In its death throes, the guard releases its grip on the mystical stone, and immediately the gate begins to close. You watch in dismay as the precious key tumbles from nerveless fingers, strikes the floor edge-first, and begins to roll away from you. Despite your desperate lunge to retrieve it, the stone skitters beyond reach, disappearing into a crack between the ancient flagstones. You stare at the now-solid barrier before you, impenetrable and immovable without the stone to activate it. The realisation settles upon you like a physical weight: your path forward is permanently blocked. With heavy heart, you gather what lessons this cursed place has taught you and begin the long journey back to the outside world.

title: Place your hand on the hand marking
narrativeText: You press your palm against the hand-shaped depression carved into the stone's surface. The mystical runes seem to pulse faintly at your touch, and you hold your breath, waiting for some magical response. Long seconds pass in silence, but nothing occurs. The stone remains inert in your grasp, its arcane markings offering no hint of power or purpose. You flex your fingers against the carved outline, ensuring full contact with every ridge and groove, but still the artefact refuses to respond to your touch. The chamber around you maintains its oppressive silence, broken only by the distant sound of water dripping somewhere in the passages beyond.

choices.choiceText: Take the stone and press it against the gate
Go back and examine the guard
Smash the Stone with your sword

title: Take the stone and press it against the gate
narrativeText: You grasp the stone firmly in your palm, its surface smooth and surprisingly warm against your skin despite the chill of the mountain air. Approaching the imposing gate, you press the mysterious object directly against the cold metal surface, applying steady pressure as you move it across various points where the marking might respond. The stone feels solid and substantial in your grip, its weight suggesting some significance, yet no matter where you place it against the barrier, no reaction occurs. The gate remains as unyielding as before, its dark metal surface reflecting nothing but your own determined efforts. The hand marking continues to stare back at you from the stones centre, as cryptic and unresponsive as ever. The silence stretches on, broken only by the faint whisper of air moving through the mountain passages around you.

choices.choiceText: Examine the guard
Smash the stone with your sword

title: Go back and examine the guard
narrativeText: You retrace your steps across the stone floor to where the guard lies motionless in its eternal rest. Kneeling beside the fallen figure, you carefully lift its cold, rigid hand, the ancient flesh feeling like leather beneath your fingers. The guard's limb moves with the stiffness of long death, joints resistant but yielding as you guide the skeletal appendage toward the stone's mysterious marking. When the dead palm makes contact with the carved symbol, the effect is immediate and startling. The edges of the hand marking begin to glow with a soft, pulsing light that seems to emanate from within the stone itself, casting dancing shadows across the surrounding metalwork. Yet despite this promising display, the gate itself remains stubbornly closed. Acting on instinct, still holding the guard's hand in place, begin to gently twist the stone . The moment you apply rotational pressure, the entire gate undergoes a remarkable transformation. The solid barrier becomes fluid as quicksilver, its surface rippling and flowing like disturbed water, before the entire structure slides smoothly aside to reveal the passage beyond.

choices.choiceText: Continue onward

title: Smash the Stone with your sword
narrativeText: Frustration mounting with each failed attempt, you place the stone carefully upon the rough mountain floor, positioning it on a flat section of rock. Drawing your sword from its scabbard, you reverse your grip to hold the weapon by its blade, raising the heavy pommel high above your head. The metal weight catches what little light filters through the passage, gleaming dully as you bring it down with all your strength against the mysterious stone. The impact reverberates through your arms and echoes off the surrounding walls with a sharp, final crack. Fragments of the shattered stone scatter across the floor in all directions, some pieces skittering into the shadows beyond your vision. Standing amidst the debris of your impulsive action, the realisation settles upon you like a heavy shroud. If this stone was indeed the key to progressing further into the mountain's depths, then you have just destroyed your only means of advancement. The gate before you remains sealed, its secrets now permanently beyond your reach. With a mixture of regret and resignation, you gather what few insights you have gained from this expedition, shouldering your pack for the long journey back to the world outside. Though you leave no richer in gold or treasure than when you arrived, the lessons learned in these ancient halls may yet prove valuable in future endeavours.

title: Continue onward
narrativeText: Beyond the now-open gateway, an extensive corridor stretches into the mountain's depths, its length disappearing into shadows that seem to swallow the far end. Unnatural torches mounted in ancient iron brackets line both walls, their flames burning with an unsettling steadiness that casts no smoke yet provides an eerie, flickering illumination. The dancing light plays across elaborate murals that cover every inch of the corridor walls, their intricate carvings seeming to shift and writhe when glimpsed from your peripheral vision. These ornate depictions tell a chilling chronicle: the sequence begins with undead figures dragging screaming victims from lonely roads, progresses to show a small but disciplined army laying siege to peaceful villages, and culminates in a terrifying image of a monstrous figure. This creature appears only semi-human, its eyes wide with malevolent intelligence and its mouth grotesquely extended by hundreds of thin tendrils, many of which terminate in smaller human mouths, creating a nightmarish vision of consumed souls. The final mural shows vast armies spreading like a plague across the land, leaving nothing but devastation in their wake. As you navigate this gallery of horrors, the corridor eventually opens into a colossal archway, easily a hundred feet in height, its massive stone frame carved with symbols that seem to pulse with dormant energy. Through this monumental entrance, you glimpse a chamber beyond, dimly lit but vast in scale. Approaching the threshold, you can distinguish the ordered ranks of motionless figures arranged in perfect military formation. Each wears armour of burnished gold inlaid with obsidian, creating an opulent display that speaks of ancient wealth and power. All are unmistakably dead, yet they stand with disciplined precision. Three separate formations, each containing roughly two hundred soldiers, maintain their eternal vigil in complete silence, offering no acknowledgement of your presence despite your proximity to their domain.

choices.choiceText: Walk in and survey the scene
Step to the side and creep to the entrance.

title: Walk in and survey the scene
narrativeText: The moment your foot crosses over the threshold into the chamber, the oppressive silence shatters like broken glass. A thunderous command bellows from somewhere within the ranks: 'Haek Graat!' The foreign words echo off the vaulted ceiling with the force of a battle cry. Instantly, the front two rows of the nearest formation spring into terrifying motion, their ancient joints moving with mechanical precision despite centuries of death. Metal clangs against metal as these undead warriors ready their weapons, the sound of numerous blades being drawn creating a deadly chorus that reverberates through the vast space. Without hesitation, they begin their advance toward you, their golden armour catching the dim light as they march in perfect synchronisation. Each footfall strikes the stone floor with measured cadence, creating an ominous rhythm that speaks of military discipline transcending the boundaries between life and death. Their empty eye sockets fix upon you with unwavering focus, and their approach carries the inexorable quality of fate itself closing in around you.

choices.choiceText: Retreat imediately
Draw you sword and ready for combat

title: Step to the side and creep to the entrance.
narrativeText: Recognising the wisdom of caution, you press yourself against the cool stone wall and move laterally along the corridor, your footsteps muffled as you creep closer to the monumental archway. However, as you draw near enough to peer around the edge, you quickly realise that the architectural design offers no sanctuary for concealment. The archway consists only of two massive pillars supporting the overhead span, providing no cover whatsoever from the view of anyone within the chamber beyond. Reluctantly, you halt your advance and remain pressed against the corridor wall, straining to observe the scene from this limited vantage point. Beyond the disciplined ranks of motionless soldiers, you can make out the focal point of the entire chamber: an imposing throne carved from black stone, its surface polished to an obsidian sheen. The seat stands empty, yet it radiates an aura of dormant authority. Directly above this seat of power, embedded in the vaulted ceiling, hangs an enormous spherical gem that catches and reflects what little light filters through the chamber, casting prismatic patterns across the walls. Surrounding the throne in a perfect circle, raised formations of rock rise from the floor like ancient monoliths, each one crowned with a smaller circular gem that glows with its own inner light. Positioned directly before the throne, more prominent than the others, stands one particularly large crystal formation, its faceted surface pulsing with a rhythm that seems almost organic, as though it possessed some form of malevolent life.

choices.choiceText: Slip round the pillar and into the chamber

title: Retreat imediately
narrativeText: Recognising the overwhelming odds arrayed against you, you immediately pivot on your heel to sprint back toward the corridor from which you entered. However, as you turn to what should be your escape route, a grinding sound like massive stone wheels turning fills the air around you. To your horror, the archway through which you entered has transformed completely. Where moments before there had been an open passage, now stands an unbroken wall of solid stone, as though the opening had never existed. The surface is seamless, offering no hint of where the entrance once stood. Behind you, the steady tramp of armoured feet grows closer as the undead ranks spread out in a coordinated manoeuvre, their movements precise despite their ancient state. They encircle your position with military efficiency, their formation closing around you like the jaws of a trap until they come to a halt in a perfect semi-circle. You find yourself completely surrounded, with nowhere to run and no avenue of escape remaining. The silence that follows is more ominous than their march, as hundreds of empty eye sockets regard you with patient, eternal stillness.

choices.choiceText: Charge the soldiers
Wait and see what happens

title: Draw you sword and ready for combat
narrativeText: Your sword slides from its scabbard with a whisper of steel against leather, the familiar weight of the blade offering some comfort in these dire circumstances. As you raise your weapon into a defensive stance, the hairs on the back of your neck prickle with the sensation that something fundamental has shifted in the space behind you. Glancing quickly over your shoulder confirms your worst fears: where the archway once provided your means of entry and potential escape, now looms an impenetrable barrier of solid stone. The transformation appears seamless and absolute, as though the passage had been sealed by some ancient and powerful enchantment. The undead soldiers respond to your drawn weapon with their own tactical precision, spreading out in a coordinated movement that speaks of centuries of military training that death has not erased. They advance steadily, their golden armour reflecting the dim chamber light as they form a semi-circular formation around your position. With methodical discipline, they halt in perfect unison, creating an inescapable ring of animated death. Your blade gleams in the eerie light, but against such overwhelming numbers, even the finest steel seems woefully inadequate. The weight of their collective gaze, emanating from countless empty sockets, presses down upon you like a physical force as they await whatever command will determine your fate.

choices.choiceText: Charge the soldiers
Wait and see what happens

title: Charge the soldiers
narrativeText: With a warrior's cry that echoes through the vast chamber, you launch yourself forward into the ranks of undead soldiers, your blade leading the charge as you seek to carve a path through their formation. Your first strike rings true, connecting with the ancient armour of the nearest warrior with a resounding clang that reverberates off the vaulted ceiling. However, your initial success proves to be fleeting as the remaining soldiers respond with the coordinated precision of a military unit that has never forgotten its training. They close ranks around you immediately, their movements flowing together like parts of a single, deadly machine. From every direction, ancient weapons thrust and slash toward you, their edges still sharp despite the centuries. You fight with desperate courage, your blade dancing through the air as you parry and riposte, managing to land several more blows against your supernatural opponents. The sound of combat fills the chamber: the clash of steel against steel, the scrape of metal against bone, and your own laboured breathing as you struggle against impossible odds. Despite your valiant efforts and considerable skill, the sheer weight of numbers proves insurmountable. Gradually, inexorably, the tide of battle turns against you. More weapons slip past your guard with each passing moment, until finally your strength fails and your defences crumble. The last sensation you experience is the cold bite of ancient steel, and then darkness claims you forever.

title: Wait and see what happens
narrativeText: The encircling ranks of undead soldiers maintain their positions in absolute stillness, creating a tableau of patient menace that stretches for long, tense moments. Then, with the same coordinated precision they displayed in surrounding you, the formation begins to shift. The soldiers step aside in perfect unison, their movements creating a clear corridor that leads directly toward the empty throne at the chamber's heart. Through this newly opened passage, a figure emerges from the depths of the formation, distinguishing itself immediately from the common soldiers by both his bearing and its unmistakably living nature. This newcomer is clearly a commander, his posture radiating the confident authority of one accustomed to absolute obedience. Unlike the skeletal warriors surrounding you, this figure possesses the robust physicality of the living, though there is something deeply unsettling about his presence amongst so much death. The commander's flesh, whilst undoubtedly alive, bears the pallor of one who has spent too long in the company of the undead, and his eyes hold the cold calculation of someone who has willingly embraced service to dark powers. His movements are those of a seasoned military leader, each step measured and purposeful as he approaches your position, stopping just outside the reach of your weapon. When he speaks, its voice carries the authority of the living combined with something far more sinister—the casual confidence of one who serves a master of unimaginable power. 'Do we need a new Gaoler and high priest?' he asks, the question delivered with the matter-of-fact tone of one discussing routine administrative duties for its dark lord, Baelmat, though the implications hanging in the air suggest your fate may well depend upon your answer.

choices.choiceText: Say yes you do
Say a new guard as well
Tell it it needs to watch it's tongue
Say nothing

title: Say yes you do
narrativeText:
The commander's laughter fills the chamber like the sound of grinding millstones, a harsh and terrible noise that seems to amuse it greatly. He begins to pace before you with predatory grace, its heavy boots striking the stone floor in rhythmic beats that echo off the vaulted ceiling above.

'Skilful indeed,' it rasps, gesturing towards you with one gauntleted hand, 'to have dispatched both of those fools shows promise. Perhaps you possess value that my lord might appreciate.' The commander's tone suggests both grudging respect and calculating assessment.

Your natural response emerges before you can check it, asking whether this creature is not itself the lord of this place. The commander's laughter takes on an uneasy quality, and for a moment something almost like nervousness flickers across its decaying features.

'Lord? Me?' it says with false bravado, 'I am but a servant, though a valued one.' His burning gaze fixes upon you more intently now, 'Tell me, intruder, did you observe the carved tale upon the walls as you entered? And more importantly, what purpose drives you to seek this forbidden place?'

choices.choiceText: Seeking treasure of Murlore's Spike
You are seeking the truth of Murlore's Spike
You are here to end the torment of the villages

title: Tell it it needs to watch it's tongue
narrativeText:
The commander's posture shifts subtly, its hand moving closer to the pommel of its massive sword with practiced ease. The air itself seems to grow colder as his burning eyes narrow to burning slits, studying you with the intensity of a predator considering whether its prey is worth the effort of killing.

'Insolence,' the word emerges like a low growl from its throat, and several of the surrounding soldiers take half-steps forward in response to some unspoken signal. The commander raises one hand, halting their advance without breaking his gaze from yours.

'Under normal circumstances,' he continues, his voice carrying the promise of violence like winter carries frost, 'such words would see you carved into pieces small enough to feed to my lord's pets.' He pauses, and in that silence you can hear the subtle creaking of leather and metal as the undead ranks await their orders.

'However,' the commander's tone shifts slightly, acknowledging grudging respect, 'to have penetrated this far into our domain, to have bested both Gaoler and high priest, demands that you be heard before you are destroyed. Speak then, and provide the answers that might postpone your death.'

choices.choiceText: Say cut me down then
Stall for time ask about it's questions
Say that your being here are all the answers it needs

title: Say nothing
narrativeText:
Your silence clearly tests the commander's already limited patience. Though this figure retains much of its human appearance, unlike the clearly undead soldiers surrounding you, there is an unsettling quality to its features that speaks of something fundamentally changed. Its eyes narrow with visible irritation, and the air itself seems to grow heavy with menace.

'Why are you here?' The question emerges as both demand and threat, each word pronounced with careful precision that suggests violence lies just beneath the surface. The commander's fingers drum against the pommel of its sword in a rhythm that speaks of barely contained aggression.

The surrounding ranks of soldiers shift almost imperceptibly, their ancient armour creating a symphony of small metallic sounds that fill the otherwise silent chamber. You sense their readiness to act upon their commander's slightest gesture, whilst the intense gaze before you awaits your decision.

choices.choiceText: Say Nothing again
Tell him you are here to end the suffering of the villagers
Say you are here to learn the truth of this place
Say that you seek the treasure of Murlore's spike

title: Tell it to cut you down then
narrativeText:
The commander's reaction to your defiant words is immediate and intense. Its eyes widen slightly with what might be surprise, or perhaps anticipation, and a sound that could almost be construed as pleased rumbling emerges from its throat. The figure leans forward slightly, studying you with newfound interest.

'Your wish is to face me in combat?' it asks, and now there is no mistaking the satisfaction in its voice. The commander's hand moves away from its sword, but not in retreat, rather in the gesture of one who has received an unexpected gift.

'How delightfully refreshing,' the figure continues, its tone now carrying genuine enthusiasm, 'it has been far too long since anyone has shown such... spirit. This would indeed provide my lord with considerable amusement.' The commander straightens to its full, imposing height and calls out in a voice that carries clearly through the chamber.

'Bring him!' The command echoes off the stone walls, and immediately you hear the measured tread of approaching feet. It seems your challenge has been accepted, though perhaps not in quite the manner you had anticipated.

choices.choiceText: A meeting with the lord

title: Stall for time ask about it's questions
narrativeText:
Your diplomatic approach appears to intrigue the commander, and it pauses in its pacing to regard you with something approaching curiosity. The figure's intense eyes seem to evaluate your words, weighing them against your previous actions and finding some merit in your attempt at discourse.

'Questions,' it muses, the grinding quality of its voice taking on a more conversational tone, 'very well. Let us see what manner of individual seeks to parley with the servants of the dead.' The commander clasps its gauntleted hands behind its back in a gesture that speaks of military bearing long ingrained.

'Tell me then, bold intruder,' the figure's voice becomes more focused, more intent, 'in your journey through our domain, you encountered our Gaoler and High Priest, are they, no more?.

The way it phrases these questions suggests that it already suspects the answers, yet wishes to hear them spoken aloud.

choices.choiceText: Tell him that both betrayed him
Tell him you killed the Gaoler but bargained with the high priest
Tell him you have slain them

title: Say that your being here are all the answers it needs
narrativeText:
The commander receives your cryptic response with a gesture of profound dismissal, its shoulders rising and falling in what might charitably be called a shrug. The figure's eyes regard you with something between disappointment and contempt, as if you have failed to meet some unspoken standard it had hoped you might achieve.

'Here is my response then,' the commander states with finality, its voice carrying no trace of regret or hesitation. The figure's hand moves in a subtle gesture, and immediately the atmosphere in the chamber shifts from tense negotiation to imminent violence.

The commander nods once, sharply, and the signal galvanises the surrounding ranks into motion. The semi-circle of undead soldiers closes like the jaws of some vast trap, their weapons raised and their advance inexorable. You draw your weapon and prepare to sell your life dearly, but the mathematics of the situation are brutally simple.

You fight with the courage of the desperate and the skill of the experienced, your blade finding its mark more than once among the press of ancient armour and corrupted flesh. Yet courage and skill can only accomplish so much against such overwhelming numbers. In the end, they drag you down through sheer weight of bodies, and darkness claims your vision as their blades find their marks.

title: Seeking treasure of Murlore's Spike
narrativeText:
The commander's reaction to your admission is immediate and devastating. Its eyes narrow with unmistakable contempt, and when it speaks, each word drips with disdain for your mercenary motivations.

'Gold, gems, that is truly why you are here?' The figure's voice carries a mixture of disappointment and disgust, as if you have revealed yourself to be something far less than it had hoped. 'How utterly small you are, how predictably mortal in your petty greed.'

With a sharp gesture, the commander issues its command: 'Bring him!' The soldiers move to seize you with practiced efficiency, their iron grips fastening upon your arms with the strength of the grave. They march you forward between their ranks, your feet barely touching the stone as they carry you towards the throne.

As you pass the dias positioned before that seat of power, your medallion suddenly erupts into searing heat against your chest. The orb embedded within the obsidian pedestal begins to pulse with eldritch light, responding to the medallion's presence with increasing intensity. Through your pain, you notice something crucial: a narrow slot at the apex of the gem, precisely the size and shape of your medallion. A deep, resonant boom fills the chamber, shaking dust from the ancient stones above.

'Hold!' the commander roars, genuine fear and awe colouring its voice for the first time, 'The Lord Baelmat is called!'

The commander approaches the pulsing dias with reverent precision, pressing both gauntleted hands firmly against the obsidian surface. Words of power flow from its lips in a tongue that predates human language, each syllable causing the air itself to crackle with malevolent energy. The ritual builds to a crescendo that makes your bones ache with its intensity.

The great orb suspended above the throne ignites with sickly green radiance, casting everything in an otherworldly glow. From its depths pours a stream of black ichor that flows downward like liquid shadow, pooling upon the throne before beginning its terrible transformation.

Before your horrified gaze, this substance reforms into a massive humanoid shape that dwarfs even the imposing commander, standing twice the height of any mortal man. Baelmat's form radiates pure malevolence, its translucent skin rippling with veins of purple and green that pulse like a diseased heartbeat. Most terrible of all are its eyes: goat-shaped orbs that burn with liquid fire, containing such concentrated hatred that to meet their gaze feels like staring into the very essence of damnation itself.

This is hate given form, malice made manifest, evil so concentrated that the air around it seems to writhe with corruption.

'I sense the presence of the fragment,' the creature intones, its voice resonating through flesh and bone alike, carrying undertones that speak of aeons spent nurturing pure spite. Its terrible gaze fixes upon you as the commander hastily explains your presence as that of a minor treasure-seeker who has eliminated their Gaoler and high priest.

Baelmat's burning eyes bore into you with crushing intensity. 'You seek great fortune?' it asks, and in those words lies both promise and threat beyond mortal comprehension.

choices.choiceText: Tell it yes, vast ammounts
Ask who it is
