'The truth!' it exclaims with obvious relish, 'How wonderfully naive. Very well, seeker of truth, let us provide you with exactly what you desire.' The commander's voice takes on a mocking theatrical quality as it continues, 'So be it. Bring him!'

The ranks of soldiers surge forward at the command, overwhelming your attempts at resistance through sheer numbers. They drag you inexorably towards the throne, your struggles proving as futile as attempting to hold back the tide. As they force you before the seat of power, you observe the pedestal that contains one of the mystical orbs, encased within black obsidian. Baelmat's form radiates pure malevolence, its translucent skin rippling with veins of purple and green that pulse like a diseased heartbeat. This is hate given form, malice made manifest, evil so concentrated that the air around it seems to writhe with corruption.

'Why am I summoned, commander?' the creature inquires, its voice resonating through your very bones, carrying undertones that speak of aeons spent nurturing pure spite.

The commander presents you with obvious pride as one who has wrought considerable damage in penetrating their domain and has proven worthy through your victories. 'They seek the truth of this place,' it explains with evident satisfaction.

Baelmat leans forward to examine you more closely, and as it does so, your medallion radiates such intense heat and malevolent energy that you can barely endure its touch against your skin. The creature's burning, goat-shaped eyes fix upon you with crushing intensity.

'You are worthy,' it pronounces with finality, 'and I offer you knowledge beyond mortal comprehension. Cast aside that bauble around your neck, and I shall pass to you truths that will reshape your understanding of existence itself.'

You try to fight and pull back, rage, incomprehension, betrayal, helplessness are all you feel, and then pain as the spoon pierces your eye, and continues. Blackness, and a voice 'you had no power, it was always theirs' Your eyes open, you are a child, before you your grandfather sits in his chair by the fire, he is carving a wooden toy, it's a toy for you, You feel joy, watching him work was a wonder, the feeling is fleeting, dread comes upon you. 'Come see what I have made for you' he says Fear comes to you, you sit still, wanting to go to him, for it to be as it was, but your fear of it being what it may keeps you rooted. Hands close around your shoulders, you look up, it is your mother smiling. Behind her father is smiling at you. A wave of relief momentarily washes over you 'Come love, see what grandpa has made you' Relief turns to dread as she pushes you relentlessly toward him, you struggle but your young frame has not the strength to stop her She stops at his feet, the old man has carved a magnificent horse, 'A few more cuts to finish' he says, and brings his blade to your cheek, and begins to cut away your skin, all the while your mothers grip keeps you pinned. Helpless rage burns in you. A voice 'They had the power, it should have been yours, do you wish the power to stop them' The rage in you rises, rises, rises, it is not fair, they are betraying you again and again, you were too young, you could not stop them, be damned to them, be they damned for this, 'Yes!' you cry. 

Blackness,  Your eyes open, you are a warrior lord, in fine armour, the strength of Baelmats hate courses through you, your mother , father, and grandfather stand before you, 'Come my little one, I feel your love, I would hold it close to me', you mother says, holding her arms out to you with rage and hatred you draw your blade and cut her down, then your father, then finally your grandfather. 'Mine is the power you scream' Blackness Your eyes open, you stand garbed in the fine armour, before the hamlet, behind you the soldiers of Baelmat. You issue a command, the soldiers march on the hamlet, you will end their torment, by offering them the blessing of Baelmat, and they will accept it..

title: Remove the medallion from beneath your shirt
narrativeText: The soldiers' grip upon your arms loosens at some unspoken command, allowing you freedom of movement within the circle of their formation. You reach beneath your travel-worn jerkin, fingers closing around the familiar weight of the medallion that has protected you throughout your journey into these accursed depths. As you slowly draw the sacred talisman into the sickly green light that bathes the throne chamber, the change is immediate and dramatic. The medallion's surface begins to shimmer with an inner radiance that seems to pulse in rhythm with your heartbeat. Vibrations run through the metal chain, and you can feel the holy power contained within the ancient artifact responding to the overwhelming evil that permeates this place. The medallion grows warm against your palm, not with the gentle heat of a blessing, but with the fierce intensity of a forge preparing for battle. Around you, the undead soldiers shift uneasily, and even Baelmat's terrible gaze focuses entirely upon the small piece of blessed metal that now blazes like a star in the darkness of its domain.

choices.choiceText: Cast it aside
Wrap it around your sword hand and attack Baelmat
Attempt to dive to the stone pedestal and push it into the opening

title: Cast it aside
narrativeText: The weight of Baelmat's presence presses upon your mind like a physical force, and visions of power beyond imagination flood your thoughts. You see yourself ruling over vast armies, commanding the fear and respect of entire kingdoms, your name spoken in whispers across the known world. The medallion feels suddenly heavy in your hand, a burden that has limited your potential for far too long. With a gesture that feels both inevitable and liberating, you cast the sacred talisman into the shadows beyond the throne chamber's flickering torchlight. The moment the medallion leaves your grasp, Baelmat's expression transforms into one of terrible joy. 'Come to me, child,' the creature intones, its voice now carrying notes of false affection. 'I can sense your love for me, your desire for the power I offer. Allow me to embrace that devotion.' You find your feet carrying you forward without conscious thought, drawn by promises whispered directly into your mind. Baelmat's massive hands encircle your head with surprising gentleness, and as its mouth opens wide, tendrils of black ichor emerge like seeking fingers. The dark substance flows into your mouth and nose, carrying with it visions of your glorious future. Your breath leaves your body as the corruption takes hold, and for a moment you teeter on the very edge of death. Then Baelmat's blessing surges through your veins, filling you with a sense of pure power,beyond mortal comprehension, you accept it and as you do you feel tendrils gently weaving around something intangible in you, a place in you where love and hope, peace and beauty reside, and the tendrils smother them, a voice 'mine' and then, a dread sensation of liquid poison, distilled hate, fear, dispair, subservience, and vile war flows into that space, killing everything and filling your soul with their foetid imperative to bring them to others, blackness. When consciousness returns, you stand once more before the hamlet of Whitevale, but now you wear armour of gold and obsidian, and behind you wait the patient ranks of your undead army, waiting to bring the people to the Lord, and he will bestow upon them his blessing, and they will accept it.

title: Wrap it around your sword hand and attack Baelmat
narrativeText: Acting as if to cast the medallion aside, holding it aloft causing all eyes to watch it, you draw your sword with lighting speed, releasing it to fly to the medallioned hand, catching it with a lifetimes worth of practice you quiclky wrap the medalion around the pommel and grip it tightly. The sacred talisman's power surges up through your blade like lightning seeking the earth, and you can feel its righteous anger burning against your palm. Moving with desperate speed, you twist and strike the two nearest guards as they try to draw their weapons, flames burst from their bodies as your blade strikes them, sending them backewards into the nearest ranks caunsing mayheam and confusion. You turn and advance upon Baelmat with grim determination. The towering creature regards your approach with what might be amusement, its translucent features shifting in the green light that bathes the chamber. Behind you, the commander's voice booms out 'You Dare!' and you hear him advancing on you, Baelmat merely observes the unfolding confrontation with the patience of something that has existed for a very long time..

choices.choiceText: Fight the commander
Attempt to draw the commander to the side and then attack Baelmat

title: Attempt to dive to the stone pedestal and push it into the opening
narrativeText: With explosive speed born of desperation, you launch yourself across the throne chamber towards the obsidian pedestal. The commander's shout of alarm echoes behind you as you dive towards the dark orb embedded in the platform's surface. Your momentum carries you forward even as undead hands reach out to stop your advance, and you manage to drive the medallion deep into the narrow opening at the orb's apex. The moment the sacred metal makes contact with the ancient artifact, brilliant white light erupts from the connection point. The radiance spreads outward like cracks in ice, racing across the orb's surface and illuminating every corner of the vast chamber. Baelmat's roar of pain and fury shakes dust from the vaulted ceiling as the creature writhes in agony. Above the throne, the massive spherical gem blazes with purifying fire, and a concentrated beam of white energy strikes the evil lord with devastating force. The smell of burning corruption fills the air as Baelmat's form begins to disintegrate, its translucent flesh bubbling and smoking where the holy light touches it. Around you, the golden-armoured soldiers burst into flames that consume their undead forms in seconds, leaving only empty armour scattered across the stone floor. Only the commander remains standing, his human form trembling as he looks around the devestated chamber with what might be wonder. Slowly, he sheathes his ancient blade, and when he speaks, his voice carries none of the harsh authority it once possessed. 'I am free of the curse that bound me,' he says, and you hear something like tears in his voice. 'A thousand thanks to you, brave soul. My heart is heavy with the crimes that creature forced me to commit, and I feel great shame for what I was made to do.' The commander's posture straightens with newfound dignity. 'From this moment until the end of my days, my sword is yours to command. If you would show mercy, task me with some service that might repay even a portion of my debt.' He pauses, seeming to gather himself before continuing. 'I know where Baelmat's treasure is hidden, accumulated over centuries of evil. If you wish, I will take you to it.'

choices.choiceText: Agree
His crimes are too many, kill him

title: Fight the commander
narrativeText: Steel rings against steel as you engage the commander in single combat, your blades meeting in a display of martial skill that would be magnificent were the stakes not so dire. The commander fights with the accumulated experience of a lifetime spent in warfare, each parry and thrust executed with disciplined precision, whilst you rely on speed and desperation to match his supernatural strength granted by Baelmat's blessing. Sparks fly where your weapons clash, the sound echoing off the chamber walls like hammer blows on an anvil. You find an opening and strike true, your blade biting deep into the commander's neck. Flesh and sinew part before your steel, and the warrior collapses to the stone floor. Yet even as you draw breath to celebrate this victory, Baelmat's laughter fills the air like the sound of collapsing mountains. The towering creature leans forward with inhuman speed, its massive mouth opening to release tendrils of black ichor that streak through the air like living whips. The dark substance strikes the fallen commander's skull, and immediately the warrior springs back to his feet, renewed and strengthened by his master's power. Before you can react, the commander's blade flashes out in a deadly arc. You feel your sword torn from your grasp, and then there is only the sensation of cold steel passing through flesh and bone as he completes his strike.

title: Attempt to draw the commander to the side and then attack Baelmat
narrativeText: You engage the commander in a deadly dance of blades, each clash of steel against steel ringing out across the stone chamber like a blacksmith's hammer. Your strategy is born of desperation, using every parry and counter-attack to gradually manoeuvre yourself around the throne room's perimeter. The commander's skills are formidable, honed by years of warfare, but you fight with the cunning of desperation, slowly positioning yourself until your back faces Baelmat's towering form. In one fluid motion, you stamp your leading foot against the stone floor and use the leverage to spin with devastating force. Your blade arcs through the air in a silver streak, biting deep into Baelmat's leg. The creature's roar of pain shakes dust from the vaulted ceiling, a sound like the earth itself crying out in agony. Yet your moment of triumph is short-lived. You feel the commander's blade find its mark at your shoulder, the steel cleaving through flesh and bone with surgical precision. Your sword arm separates from your body in a spray of crimson, and the weapon clatters uselessly to the floor. Baelmat's massive hand closes around your head like a vice, lifting your feet from the stone as the creature's terrible strength raises you into the air. Its mouth opens wide, revealing row upon row of needle-sharp teeth, and black ichor pours forth like a tide of corruption. The substance flows into your mouth and nose, carrying with it visions of eternal torment that make your previous pain seem like gentle caresses, you feel the tendrils break in your body and become a host of insidious worms each digging deeper into your body bringing pain in every path they take, with perpetual inevitable relentlessness you feel some make their way to your brain, and the pain follows. As pure pain replaces consciousness, you hear Baelmat's voice resonating through your very bones: 'An eternity of agony for you.'

title: Agree
narrativeText: You consider the commander's offer carefully, weighing mercy against justice in the silence that follows Baelmat's destruction. The curse that bound this warrior to evil has indeed been lifted, and the genuine remorse in his voice suggests that redemption might be possible even for one who has committed such terrible acts. 'Your offer is accepted,' you say finally, and the commander's posture seems to straighten with something approaching relief. 'Your mercy will be remembered,' he responds, his voice carrying a note of humility. 'Come, good warrior', and he walks toward the rear of the chamber, 'I will show you where Baelmat's treasure is hidden.' The commander's footsteps echo through the vast chamber as he leads you to a concealed door set into the stone wall behind the throne. With a practiced motion, he presses a hidden latch, and the door swings open to reveal a narrow passageway that descends deeper into the mountain. 'This way,' he instructs, and you follow him into the darkness beyond. The air grows cooler and damper as you proceed, the sound of dripping water echoing off the stone walls. After several minutes of walking, you arrive at a small cavern illuminated by the flickering light of torches set into iron sconces. In the centre of the chamber lies a large wooden chest, its surface adorned with intricate carvings that depict scenes of conquest and plunder. The commander approaches the chest with reverence, his fingers tracing the designs as if they were sacred texts. 'Baelmat accumulated this wealth over centuries,' he explains, 'gathered from those unfortunate enough to cross his path. He had no real use for it, wealth was not his goal, only the search for artefacts that served his dark purposes.', it is yours now

choices.choiceText: Thank him and survey the wealth
Slay him for the crimes he committed

title: His crimes are too many, kill him
narrativeText: Your blade clears its sheath with the whisper of steel against leather, and you see the commander's eyes widen with something approaching surprise at the speed of your draw. Yet where you expected to see fear, you instead witness the return of the hatred that has sustained this man through years of service to evil. The commander's weathered features twist into an expression of malevolent rage, as if your refusal of his offer has awakened the very darkness you thought had been purged. Your strike is swift and true, the blessed steel of your weapon enhanced by contact with the sacred medallion. The blade passes through the commander's neck without resistance, separating head from shoulders in one clean motion. The warrior's body collapses in a cascade of battle-worn armour and spilled blood. With grim efficiency, you gather what treasures you can carry from the scattered wealth that lies about the chamber. Precious gems catch the dying light of the chamber's torches, and golden artifacts gleam with the promise of a comfortable life beyond these cursed halls. Yet even as you fill your travel pack with Baelmat's accumulated riches, you cannot shake the feeling that the commander's final expression held more than mere anger. Perhaps it was relief at being freed from the burden of redemption, or perhaps it was something darker still. With your grim business concluded, you turn your back on the throne chamber and begin the long journey back to the world of the living.

title: Agree
narrativeText: The commander bows his head in what might be gratitude, his voice carrying a note of genuine appreciation as he speaks. 'Your mercy in this dark hour will not be forgotten, and your name shall be remembered with honour.' The weathered warrior reaches into the folds of his tattered garments and withdraws a magnificent sceptre, its silver and gold surface gleaming despite the years. The artifact is crafted in the likeness of a serpent, its mouth agape to display a large black pearl that seems to pulse with its own inner darkness. 'This is yours by right,' the commander states as he presents the treasure. 'A token of the greater wealth that lies beyond.' He bows deeply stepping back into the corridor, as he does you hear a loud crack and solid iron bars plumet to the floor at the entrance crashing with an awful finality and sealing the entrance. You rush to the bars, your hands closing around the cold metal as you watch the commander move beyond your reach. His battle-scarred form turns back towards you one final time, and when he speaks, his voice carries the same mocking tone you heard from Baelmat himself. 'Enjoy your wealth,' he calls out, each word dripping with false courtesy. 'Write grand tales of your heroic deeds, and tell them to yourself for entertainment. You shall have some time for such stories for no other audience will ever hear them.'

title: Take the medallion out and throw it away
narrativeText: You draw the medallion from beneath your jerkin, holding the sacred talisman up to catch the sickly green light that bathes the throne chamber. The holy artifact vibrates with barely contained fury in your grasp, its metal surface growing warm as it reacts to the overwhelming evil that surrounds you. Yet the promises that Baelmat whispers directly into your mind are seductive beyond resistance. Knowledge of the ancients, secrets that could reshape the very foundations of the world, power that would make the gods themselves take notice of your existence. Surely such wisdom is worth more than any physical protection this simple medallion could offer. With a gesture that feels both momentous and inevitable, you cast the sacred talisman away into the shadows. The moment it leaves your hand, Baelmat's terrible joy becomes palpable, a wave of malevolent satisfaction that washes over you like a tide. 'Receive my knowledge then, willing pupil,' the creature intones as its massive hands descend to cradle your skull with surprising gentleness. Baelmat's mouth opens impossibly wide, revealing depths that seem to extend beyond the physical realm. From those dark recesses emerge tendrils of black ichor, seeking your mouth and nose with the purposeful movement of living things. The corruption flows into you, carrying with it the accumulated wisdom of ages beyond counting. Your mind expands to accommodate knowledge that no mortal was ever meant to possess, secrets of creation and destruction that burn themselves into your consciousness. Yet with this wisdom comes something else, something darker. Baelmat's hatred, refined over centuries of imprisonment, floods your thoughts like poison in a well. When the darkness finally lifts from your vision, you find yourself clothed in robes of pristine white, standing before the torture chamber where your journey through these depths began. Undead soldiers drag familiar figures from the hamlet of Whitevale into the room, their screams of terror echoing off the stone walls. Among them is the girl you once tried to save, recaptured and returned to face the fate she briefly escaped. She looks up at you with eyes that hold no hope, and the last remnant of her mortal will escapes in a single breath of absolute despair. 'You will teach me many things,' you say, your voice carrying none of the compassion it once possessed. Your hand reaches for one of the instruments that await your use.

title: Say it can tell you without you doing so
narrativeText:
You stand your ground before the towering entity, your voice steady despite the supernatural dread that permeates the air around Baelmat's presence. 'If knowledge is what you offer, then surely you can share it without requiring the surrender of my protection,' you state, gesturing towards the medallion that rests against your chest. Baelmat's response is a sound that might once have been laughter, though it now resembles the grinding of millstones more than any expression of mirth. The creature's translucent features shift and flow as it speaks, each word carrying the weight of centuries. 'Very well, mortal seeker. I shall grant you a glimpse of the truth you claim to desire.'

'I was forged in the crucible of the waste fires of the Gods, their dirt made manifest, as I rose from that pit, to claim right of existence among them, they merely laughed and cast me to earth, foul detritus of their hubris and pride.'

 'Here to the swamp beyond the mountain, cast and forgot, and so my journey on the earth began, small creatures, rats and vermin, snakes and all manner of creeping creatures my sustenance and army, men came, and I found them easy prey, my forms working into their minds as they slept upon the road they built, their bodies of use as I drove them to dig and build until my house under the mountain was made'

'The Gods looked down upon this place, so small to them, yet recognising their filth had purpose, they sent druids of great power here to bind me in this place, they were all consumed by my hate, but succeeded in binding me here, the last piece of their task, to send me back to the pit of filth, unforfilled as I rent their souls into a thousand pieces and poisoned every one with dispair for eternity. '

And so, for hundres of years I have taken more of the Gods people, made ready for what will come. My Army is almost ready to issue forth, take more by force, bring them here in lager numbers, to recieve my blessing, the blessings of hate, violence, and the putrid nature of the gods, their souls my nourishment.'

'And so it will be, that the children of the Gods shall become mine, the world of the Gods will burn, will suffer, will no longer be a home for the Gods but mine and for eternity'. With horror you begin to understand this is not simply a monster hoarding treasure in a mountain, but an ancient intelligence preparing to unleash devastation upon an unsuspecting world. When Baelmat finally falls silent, its attention focuses entirely upon you with uncomfortable intensity. 'You have heard much, mortal child,' it says, its voice carrying notes of false kindness. 'You may learn far more in the time to come, but your learning can proceed from a position of power and privilege, or from the depths of captivity and torment, brought forth to bear helpless withess before being returned to my own pit of dispair. The choice, as they say, remains yours to make.'

choices.choiceText: You cannot escape here with what you know, a position of power may offer a chance
Tell it you want no part of a pact

title: You cannot escape here with what you know, accept power
narrativeText: The weight of your situation settles upon you like a burial shroud as you consider the impossible odds arrayed against your escape. Baelmat's knowledge of your presence, combined with the army of undead soldiers that fills this chamber, makes any attempt at flight seem futile at best. Perhaps accepting the creature's offer of power represents your only chance of survival, and from a position of authority, you might find opportunities to limit the damage this evil could inflict upon the innocent. Your decision made, you incline your head towards the towering entity. 'Your offer has merit,' you concede, the words tasting of ash in your mouth. Baelmat's features shift into an expression that might be a smile, though it holds no warmth or genuine pleasure. 'Wisdom at last,' the creature responds, its voice carrying notes of false approval. 'Remove that trinket you wear and cast it aside, and you shall receive truths that will reshape your understanding of existence itself.' Your hand moves to the medallion beneath your jerkin, fingers closing around the familiar weight of the sacred talisman. The holy artifact seems to pulse with its own heartbeat, as if sensing the momentous choice that lies before you. Even now, in this moment of decision, the medallion's presence offers comfort and protection against the overwhelming evil that surrounds you.

choices.choiceText: Cast it from you
Place it on the pedistal
Hurl it at Baelmat

title: Tell it you want no part of a pact
narrativeText:
Your refusal rings out across the throne chamber with the clarity of a bell, each word chosen to leave no doubt about your position. 'I want no part of any pact with you,' you declare, meeting Baelmat's terrible gaze without flinching. The creature's response is immediate and decisive. With lightning speed its arms shoot forward and hands grip your shoulders, you cry out in pain as their uncanny strength crushes then, grindng broken bone into muscle and sinew, rendering your arms useless, pain overcomes you and the last thing you hear before losing consciousness is Bealmats voice 'You will bear witness, you will learn, you will dispair' it states with the finality of a judge pronouncing sentence.

  Darkness, when awareness returns, you find yourself shackled to a stone wall, horror fills you as it dawns on you that you are strapped naked by the chest to the wall of the conversion hall, your arms and feet surgically removed, the wounds tended, so that you may not die'

Baelmats voice suddenly seeps into your mind 'Children rarely enjoy the stillness required for lessons, I have ensured that will not be a concern for you. You have been kept in darkness for a long while, untill this time, now your school is ready, now learn'

You are raised, so that you can see all of the tables in front of you and as you survey this scene with dread the sound of heavy boots echoes through the doorway. 

 Through the doorway comes undead soldiers, dragging figures you quickly recognise, the people of Whitevale, beaten, broken, in abject terror. As they are forced to the tables, the young and old, one looks up at you, the herb woman, tears stream down her face, she cries out as she is shackled to a table, 'You were the one, you!, you, but you are not, and all are doomed', her words cut short as the clamp around her neck shuts.

'You would not be taught', Baelmats voice again resonates in your head, 'But I have another who would', A figure in white robes enters from behind the black chair, three dire-wolves at her side, and your heart breaks as you recognise the face beneath the hood. It is the girl you had recued, her face now a mask of corruption, hatred and malevolent purpose now sit there. 

She approaches one of the tables where a man lies bound, his face streaked with tears. Recognition briefly flashes accross his face 'Maen, my beautiful daughter!' he cries out, joy and relief warring with confusion in his voice. 'I thought you were lost forever!' Comprehension dawns in his eyes as he takes in her transformed appearance, and his cry of despair echoes throughout the chamber. The girl who was once innocent reaches for one of the instruments that await her use, and you understand that your education in the true nature of evil has only just begun.

title: Cast it from you
narrativeText: The visions that fill your mind are intoxicating beyond description, showing you a future where your power eclipses that of kings and emperors. Baelmat's promises whisper directly into your consciousness, painting pictures of glory and influence that no mortal ruler has ever possessed. The medallion in your hand feels suddenly heavy, a burden that has held you back from achieving your true potential throughout this entire journey. With a gesture that feels both liberating and inevitable, you cast the sacred talisman into the shadows beyond the throne chamber's flickering torchlight. The moment the holy artifact leaves your grasp, Baelmat's expression transforms into one of terrible, ancient joy. 'Come to me, my child,' the creature coos, its voice now carrying false notes of parental affection. 'I can feel the love you bear for me, the desire for the power I can grant. Allow me to embrace that devotion fully.' Your feet carry you forward without conscious decision, drawn by promises that seem to resonate in your soul. Baelmat's massive hands encircle your head with surprising gentleness, and as the creature's mouth opens to reveal impossible depths, tendrils of black corruption emerge like seeking fingers. The dark substance flows into your body through every opening, carrying with it sensations of power that no mortal frame was ever meant to contain. Your breath leaves your lungs as the transformation begins, and for a terrifying moment you hover on the very threshold between life and death. Then Baelmat's blessing surges through your veins like molten hate, filling every cell with supernatural vitality. When consciousness returns the dim light of dawn surrounding you, you find yourself standing once more in the peaceful valley where your adventure began, before you, sleeping before the morning rise, the hamlet you left all that while ago, but now you wear armour of gold and obsidian, and behind you wait the patient ranks of your undead army. In the distance, you see the single small hut where the old lady had met you, in the dawn light you see her, she stands on the path outside and looks upon you, and falls to her knees, it is a short while before you hear her wail of dismay. The villagers of Whitevale rest unaware of what is coming, unaware that you will bring their torment, and Baelmats blessing shall end it forever.

title: Place it on the pedistal
narrativeText: You remove the medallion from around your neck with deliberate ceremony, allowing the sacred chain to pool in your palm as you kneel before the obsidian pedestal. The holy artifact pulses with its own inner light, as if sensing the momentous nature of what is about to occur. Your gaze darts to the slot in the orb and your muscles tense, ready to dive.  A cry of alarm rings out from the commander as he recognises your true intention, but his warning comes far too late. In one fluid motion, you launch yourself to the orb and drive the medallion deep into the narrow opening that crowns the dark orb embedded in the platform's surface. The moment blessed metal touches corrupted stone, brilliant white radiance explodes from the connection point like a miniature star being born. The light spreads outward in racing lines that follow the orb's surface, illuminating every carving and symbol with purifying fire. Baelmat's roar of agony shakes the very foundations of the mountain, a sound like the earth itself crying out in pain. Above the throne, the massive spherical gem blazes with holy fire, and from its surface emerges a concentrated beam of white energy that strikes the evil lord with devastating precision. The stench of burning corruption fills the air as Baelmat's translucent flesh begins to bubble and smoke where the divine light touches it. All around you, the golden-armoured soldiers burst into flames that consume their undead forms in heartbeats, leaving only empty metal shells scattered across the stone floor. When the purifying fire finally subsides, only the commander remains standing, his human form trembling as he surveys the transformed chamber with what might be wonder. Slowly, deliberately, he sheathes his ancient blade and turns to face you with new dignity. 'I am free of the curse that bound me to darkness,' he says, and you hear something like tears in his voice. 'A thousand thanks to you, brave soul. My heart is heavy with the terrible things that creature forced me to commit, and I feel great shame for the evil I was made to do.' The commander straightens, his posture reflecting newfound nobility. 'From this moment until the end of my days, my sword is yours to command. If you can find it within your mercy to task me with some service, perhaps I might repay even a small portion of my debt to the innocent.' He pauses, seeming to gather courage before continuing. 'I know where Baelmat's treasure lies hidden, wealth accumulated over centuries of evil. If you wish it, I shall take you there.'

title: Hurl it at Baelmat
narrativeText: With desperate strength born of righteous fury, you hurl the sacred medallion directly at Baelmat's towering form. The holy artifact blazes with purifying light as it arcs through the air, trailing radiance like a falling star. Yet the commander's reflexes, honed by years of combat, prove faster than your desperate gambit. The warrior intercepts your throw with a leap that carries him between you and his master, hands closing around the medallion even as divine fire begins to consume his mortal form. White flames engulf the commander's body, reducing flesh and bone to ash in mere moments. His final scream echoes throughout the chamber, a sound of such agony. When the purifying fire finally subsides, nothing remains of the commander save a small pile of grey ash upon the stone floor and his melted armour. Baelmat glances at the remnants of its most trusted servant barely acknowledging the sacrifice, then turns its attention back to you with renewed interest. 'How inconvenient,' the creature muses, its voice carrying notes of false sorrow. 'I find myself in need of a new commander, and you have demonstrated precisely the sort of initiative I need in my servants.' Before you can react, Baelmat's arms streak toward you, hands grasping your head, a searing pain causing you to open your mouth to scream, but nothing comes out. Baelmat's maw opens and black tendris streak from within and force themselves into you. It's will crashes against your mind like a crushing wave, overwhelming your defences with contemptuous ease. Visions flood your consciousness, twisted memories that feel more real than your own experiences. You see yourself as a child, cowering before family members whose faces have been transformed into masks of cruelty and hatred. Your life is redrawn, every success turned to failure, every love to hate, every achievement to dirt and shame, the false memories burn themselves into your psyche with searing intensity, rewriting your past and reshaping your identity according to Baelmat's design. Your shame, and failure are overbearing, you were meant for other things, high adventure, mastery, lordship, not this filth that you are. Baelmats voice breaks through to you. 'I am the one who can set this right, I am the lord of the mountain, lord of all, I can give you your worth and more, give your spirit to me and it shall be so!' With fury you cry yes! and darkness falls for you. You awake, it is the dim light of dawn, you find yourself clad in fine armour, behind you Baelmats undead Army awaiting your order, behind them carts to carry prisoners, you turn to the hamlet, they shall feel your glory, it will be carried in the blades of the undead, they will know shame, they will know fear and futility as you had once, and Baelmat shall set them free. 'Take them all' you command.

title: Tell it yes, vast ammounts
narrativeText: Your voice carries clearly across the throne chamber as you answer Baelmat's query with unflinching honesty. 'Yes, I seek wealth beyond imagination, riches that would make kings envious and merchants weep with desire.' The towering creature's features shift in what might be approval, its translucent skin pulsing with internal light as it considers your response. 'Then it becomes merely a matter of fair exchange,' Baelmat states, its voice carrying the tone of a merchant discussing the price of grain rather than the corruption of a mortal soul. 'Cast aside that trinket you wear hidden beneath your jerkin, and you shall possess wealth beyond the dreams of any mortal ruler. Gold and gems in quantities that would fill entire treasuries, precious artifacts that have not seen daylight for a thousand years.' The creature gestures towards a dark opening in the chamber's floor, a refuse pit that descends into unknown depths. 'Such a small thing to surrender for such magnificent reward. That medallion offers you nothing but the illusion of protection, whilst I offer you the reality of power.' Baelmat's gaze fixes upon you with uncomfortable intensity, and you sense that this moment will determine not only your fate, but potentially the fate of everyone you encountered during your journey to this place. The decision rests entirely in your hands.

choices.choiceText: Agree and cast the medallion into the hole
Refuse

title: Ask who it is
narrativeText: The towering entity regards your question with something that might be amusement, its translucent features shifting in the sickly green light that bathes the throne chamber. 'I am Baelmat, Lord of this mountain and all that lies within its shadow,' it responds, each word carrying the weight of centuries. 'Soon I shall be Lord of these lands, and in time, ruler of all lands beneath the sky.' The creature's voice grows more persuasive as it continues, taking on the tone of a merchant presenting an irresistible bargain. 'If wealth is truly what you seek, then I offer you a trade that will satisfy both our desires. That trinket you wear hidden beneath your jerkin serves you poorly in this place.' Baelmat gestures with one massive hand towards a dark opening in the chamber floor, a refuse pit whose depths disappear into impenetrable shadow. 'Discard that worthless bauble, cast it down into the waste where it belongs, and in return you may take all the treasure you can carry. Gold and gems in quantities sufficient to make you a legend wherever you might travel.' The creature's gaze fixes upon you with uncomfortable intensity, and you sense that its offer contains no deception. Whatever wealth lies hidden in this mountain's depths, Baelmat seems genuinely willing to part with a portion of it in exchange for your medallion.

choices.choiceText: Remove the medallion and drop it down the hole
Refuse

title: Remove the medallion and drop it down the hole
narrativeText: Your decision made, you reach beneath your jerkin and withdraw the sacred medallion that has protected you throughout your journey into these accursed depths. The holy talisman feels heavier than usual in your palm, as if reluctant to leave your possession after so many trials shared together. Yet the promise of wealth beyond imagination proves more compelling than sentimental attachment to a simple piece of blessed metal. With a gesture that feels both momentous and liberating, you cast the medallion into the dark refuse pit, listening as it disappears into the depths with a faint echo. Baelmat's expression transforms immediately into one of satisfied triumph, its features radiating pleasure at this turn of events. 'Excellent,' the creature purrs, its voice now carrying notes of genuine warmth. 'You have chosen wisely, mortal seeker.' At Baelmat's gesture, the commander approaches and beckons for you to follow. Your footsteps echo hollowly as you are led through a hidden passage behind the obsidian throne, emerging into a chamber that takes your breath away. The room beyond is filled with wealth accumulated over centuries of conquest and corruption. Mountains of golden coins rise like hills across the floor, whilst precious gems of every conceivable colour catch and reflect the torchlight in brilliant cascades of rainbow fire. Ancient artifacts of incredible workmanship line shelves carved into the living rock, each piece worth more than most people see in a lifetime. You fill your travel pack and every available pocket with as much treasure as your frame can carry, selecting only the most valuable and portable items from the vast hoard. When you finally emerge from Murlores Spike and return to the world of the living, you do so as one of the wealthiest individuals in all the known lands. Your fortune will sustain not only your own lifetime, but many generations of your descendants as well. As you make your way back to civilisation, Baelmat's voice echos in your mind, 'Lord of this mountain, soon to be lord of these lands, then lord of all lands beneath the sky.' You pause, the wealth you carry, you realise, will never be enough to stop what is coming.

title: Refuse
narrativeText: Your refusal to surrender the medallion hangs in the air between you and the towering entity, and Baelmat's expression shifts subtly from merchant-like patience to something far more predatory. 'Then there can be no trade between us,' the creature states with the finality of a judge pronouncing sentence. 'Yet you possess so little of value, mortal child. That trinket around your neck is the only thing you carry that holds any worth in these halls, save perhaps your service.' Baelmat's voice takes on a tone of false reasonableness, as if explaining simple mathematics to a slow student. 'If you were to serve me willingly, you would possess wealth beyond your wildest imagination and wield power that kings would envy for the remainder of your natural life. Such a bargain has been accepted by many who came before you.' The creature's massive form leans forward slightly, bringing its terrible presence closer to where you stand. 'Yet I find myself growing impatient with negotiation. Your options have narrowed to these three alone: accept death with the dignity of defiance; choose service and gain great wealth and power; or walk from this place carrying what riches you can manage, though all paths require you to discard that precious bauble you clutch so tightly.' Baelmat's gaze fixes upon you with predatory intensity. 'Choose swiftly, for my patience wears thin, and the consequences of delay grow ever more severe.'

choices.choiceText: Walk away with great wealth
Death
Service and great wealth and power

title: Death
narrativeText: You will not be a slave to Baelmat. You refuse. Soldiers drag you to the waste hole and force you to your knees. The commander draws his sword and cleaves your head from your shoulders. Your head falls down the hole, and your body follows.

title: Service and great wealth and power
narrativeText:
After weighing all the terrible options before you, this choice seems somehow the least objectionable path amongst uniformly grim alternatives. With trembling hands you reach beneath your shirt and move the medallion, the sacred artifact that has protected you through so many trials and dangers. Its surface feels warm against your palm one final time before you cast it away into the shadows, hearing the soft clink as it strikes stones somewhere in the darkness beyond the throne. 
You step forward to stand before Baalmat and the Dark Lord reaches down with both massive hands to grasp your head, holding you immobile while its mouth opens impossibly wide. Black tendrils of corruption snake forth like living things forcing their way past your lips and down your throat. Your breath is drawn from you until you teeter on the very edge of death, lungs burning, vision darkening. Then just as oblivion seems certain, Baalmat's will flows into the void, left by your departing life force, filling you with power beyond anything you ever imagined possible. The sensation is overwhelming, terrible and exhilarating in equal measure. Darkness claims you completely. 
It is morning, a year since you accepted Baalmat's blessing for wealth and glory. Dawn light filters weakly through the morning mist as you crouch motionless behind an outcropping of rock, your body perfectly still in the manner of a predator awaiting prey. Upon your form rests armour crafted from the finest metals, gold and silver worked into intricate patterns that catch and reflect the pale light. Whilst a magnificent sword rests at your side, its hilt encrusted with precious gems that would ransom a dozen kingdoms. You are adorned in wealth beyond the dreams of any common man, a walking testament to power and privilege. 
The sharp crack of springing metal alerts you to your success. Rising from your concealment, you approach the small trap you set the previous evening, within which a single rat runs in terrified circles, seeking escape from its wire prison. You reach in and grasp the creature, its teeth scraping uselessly against your bejeweled gauntlet as it attempts to defend itself. 
Opening your mouth, you release the tendrils of Baalmat's blessing, watching with detached satisfaction as they streak into the rat's body, draining its natural life and replacing it with undead animation. 
Another small addition to the ever-growing army of undead vermin within Merlore's spike. Your power over such creatures is absolute and unquestionable. This is your existence now. The reality of the choice you made that year ago in that throan room, you wealth beyond measure, armor fit for royalty, power over the undead realm of rats and other small creatures. You are Baalmat's glorified rat catcher, dressed in finery whilst performing the most menial of tasks, forever bound to serve the Dark Lord's wins in his mockery of the greatness you were promised.

title: Tell it you are here to free the valley from its torment
narrativeText:
You declare that you're here to free the valley from its torment. Belmat leans forward on his throne, and the sickly green radiance from the orb above intensifies. The undead soldiers holding you remain perfectly still, cold seeping through your clothing. Belmat's translucent skin shifts like oil on water. The medallion, hidden beneath your jerk and suddenly blazes with heat. 
'You are worthy'. Belmat's voice grinds through the chamber. 'I will permit you to end their torment if you willingly accept my blessing. Power and glory will follow. Oh, you will end the torment of all'. The creature's massive face leans mere feet from your own. 'Remove that useless baule from beneath your jerkin and cast it aside'. What will you do?

choices.choiceText: Remove the medallion from within your shirt
Tell it you are not its servant

title: Tell it you are here seeking knowledge
narrativeText: You state, with humitlity, that you are seeking knowledge that would otherwise be hidden. Baelmat leans over and looks at you. Your medallion radiates heat and a sensation of hate as it gets closer. Baelmat's eyes in turn blaze with hatred, but also curiosity as it comes closer. The medallion begins to get hotter, to the point of being painful. Then,You feel a brief stab of agony in your head which slowly subsides as if it were water flowing from a bowl, you sense memories flowing with it. Baelmat leans back and speaks 'You, are, worthy' It says. 'I offer you knowledge, knowledge of great power, of great magic and lore, knowledge that will grant you power over all you wish be it people or things. But, it cannot be passed to you whilst you wear that symbol of lies. It stands in your way, and with no gain or favour to you, take itfrom around your neck and cast it from you so that you may achieve all you desire.'

choices.choiceText: Say it can tell you things without you doing that
Cast the medallion away

title: Tell it you seek treasure
narrativeText: 'I sense a presence of the fragment' It says. The commander looks at you. 'You seek great fortune?' it asks.

choices.choiceText: Ask who it is
Tell it yes you seek vast wealth

title: Try to move silently to the opening
narrativeText:
You begin your ascent with deliberate care, placing each foot with calculated precision upon the stone steps to minimise any sound that might betray your presence. The stairs rise before you at a gentle incline, their surfaces worn smooth by centuries of passage, though whether by the living or the undead remains uncertain in this cursed place. 
Your lantern swings gently at your side as you climb, its light joining the flickering illumination cast by the ancient sconces that line the walls at irregular intervals. The flames in these wall-mounted brackets burn with an unnatural steadiness, never guttering or wavering despite the cold air that flows through the chamber. Each step upward brings you closer to the flat landing area ahead, and you angle your approach towards an opening on the left of it. 
The entrance is not large, barely wider than a doorway, its threshold dark against the surrounding stone. As you draw level with this side passage, you pause and lean forward cautiously, positioning yourself to peer into the space beyond whilst remaining as concealed as possible should anything within be watching. Your eyes adjust to the different quality of light in the chamber beyond, and the scene that greets you speaks of ancient purpose and long abandonment. This was clearly a guardroom once, designed to house sentries who would control access to the ornate gates just after it. Against the far wall of this modest chamber, illuminated by a single guttering sconce, sits a figure that might once have been human. It remains slumped in what appears to be a seated position, its form encased in armour that, despite the passage of unknown years, has remained remarkably preserved. The metal plates show little sign of the corrosion you might expect, their surfaces still holding a dull gleam where the light touches them. In each of the figure's gauntleted hands rests a blade, the weapons held in a loose grip that suggests they might slide free at the slightest disturbance. 
What little you can see of the figure itself, visible through gaps in the armour at joints and neck, reveals skin that has been preserved through some unknown process, drawn tight across the underlying structure like leather stretched over a frame and dried to the consistency of ancient parchment. The overall impression is one of a guardian who has sat in patient vigil for so long that time itself seems to have forgotten about its presence.

choices.choiceText: Examine the room
Go to the gate

title: Listen for anything that may alert you to whats up there
narrativeText: You halt your progress upon the stairs, one foot still raised mid-step as you freeze in place and focus every sense upon the chamber above you. The medallion hanging against your chest beneath your clothing provides its customary warmth, though it offers no warning pulse that might indicate immediate supernatural danger. You strain your hearing, filtering out the sound of your own breathing, the faint creak of leather from your equipment, even the subtle whisper of air moving through the mountain's passages. The silence that surrounds you is so profound it seems almost tangible, pressing against your eardrums with the weight of centuries of stillness. No footfall disturbs the ancient stones above, no rustle of movement breaks the oppressive quiet, no whisper of conversation or challenge reaches down from the landing area ahead. Your eyes sweep the visible portions of the upper chamber, searching for any betraying flicker of shadow that might indicate a concealed presence, watching for the telltale shift of darkness that would suggest something moving just beyond the reach of the sconce light. The flames in their wall brackets burn with unchanging steadiness, casting no dancing shadows that might mask the movements of a hidden guardian. Even the air itself seems to have grown still, as though the very mountain holds its breath in anticipation of your next decision. The ornate gates ahead remain closed and silent, their dark metal surfaces reflecting nothing but the steady light from the ancient flames. To your left, the opening that leads to what might be a guardroom shows no signs of occupancy, no sounds emerging from its shadowed interior to suggest anything lurks within. The complete absence of any indication that you are not alone begins to feel almost reassuring, though a small voice of caution in the back of your mind reminds you that silence can be as much a trap as an ambush.

choices.choiceText: Be cautious anyway and try to be silent
Certain nobody is there, go to the gate

title: Nobody is there, go to the gate
narrativeText: With growing confidence that the chamber stands empty of any active threat, you proceed up the remaining stairs at a more normal pace, your footfalls echoing slightly off the ancient stone as you climb the thirty steps towards the flat landing area that dominates the upper level. The sconces continue to provide their steady, unnatural illumination, their flames casting a warm but somehow unwelcoming light across the worked stone surfaces that surround you. As you ascend the ornate metal gates that bar further progress gradually come into fuller view. They are impressive constructions, easily twice your height and wrought from dark iron that has been twisted and shaped into patterns that seem to shift and sway when viewed from different angles, as though the metal remembers some more fluid state from which it was forged. The gates are closed firmly across the passage beyond, their surfaces bearing no obvious lock or mechanism that might suggest a conventional means of opening them. You reach the flat section and pause to take stock of your surroundings, turning slightly to survey the entire chamber now that you stand at its highest accessible point. Your gaze is drawn almost instinctively to the opening on your left, the modest entrance that breaks the otherwise symmetrical design of this ceremonial space. From this elevated vantage point, you can see more clearly into what was obviously designed as a guardroom, a functional space intended to house those who would control access to whatever lies beyond the sealed gates. The chamber is small and austere, its purpose purely practical rather than ceremonial. Against the far wall, clearly visible now in the light from both your lantern and the chamber's sconces, sits the lone occupant of this ancient post. The figure remains slumped in what appears to be a position of rest or perhaps eternal sleep, its form sheathed in armour that shows a remarkable degree of preservation despite what must be centuries of abandonment. In each hand, the figure grasps a blade, the weapons held in a loose grip that suggests readiness rather than active threat. The armour itself, though marked by time, retains much of its original integrity, the metal plates still fitted together in their proper arrangement. What strikes you most forcefully is the figure's visible flesh, what little can be seen through gaps in the armoured coverage, skin drawn tight and dried to the texture of ancient leather, preserved by conditions or processes you can only guess at. The overall scene suggests a guardian who took up position at their post and simply never left, time itself seeming to have passed them by whilst they maintained their eternal vigil.

choices.choiceText: Try to open the gate
Go and examine the figure

title: Try to open the gate
narrativeText: Without further investigation or caution, you stride directly to the ornate gates and reach out to grasp their cold metal framework with both hands, fully intending to test their weight and perhaps force them open through simple physical strength. Your fingers close around the dark iron, and in that instant, everything changes. Rather than encountering the expected resistance of solid metal, your hands sink into the gate's surface as though you had plunged them into thick, wet mud. The sensation is profoundly wrong, the metal yielding in a way that no natural substance should yield, offering no resistance whatsoever to your penetration of what appeared to be an impenetrable barrier. Before you can even process what is happening, before you can think to pull away, the gates' surface undergoes a transformation as swift as it is terrible. The metal solidifies around your entrapped hands with shocking speed, hardening back to its original state in the span of a single heartbeat. You are now firmly caught, held as securely as if your hands had been encased in stone, unable to pull free despite your immediate and instinctive struggles. The trap has been sprung with perfect efficiency. A sound reaches your ears from the guardroom to your left, the unmistakable noise of movement where moments before there had been only stillness and silence. Metal grates against stone and you turn to see an aromoured figure rising to its feet with jerking, unnatural movements. The undead warrior stands fully upright now, its preserved form animated by whatever dark force has kept it at its post through the long years. It grasps twin blades, one in each hand that gleam with deadly purpose as it begins to advance towards you with measured, deliberate steps. When it speaks, its voice carries the grinding quality of stone scraping against bone, harsh and terrible but perfectly comprehensible. 'The gatekeeper must be seen first,' it declares with the absolute certainty of one stating an immutable law. 'Only the gatekeeper grants passage. Only the gatekeeper holds authority over the sacred gates.' The creature continues its inexorable advance, raising both weapons as it approaches your helpless, trapped form. You are completely defenceless, unable to reach your sword, unable to move, unable to do anything but watch as the undead guardian closes the distance between you. The blades begin their work with awful precision, falling again and again without mercy or hesitation. The ancient warrior performs its duty exactly as it was commanded to do countless years ago, eliminating all who would bypass proper protocol and attempt to force passage without permission. The cold stone floor grows slick beneath you as consciousness begins to fade, the last sensation being one of profound regret that you did not exercise greater caution in this place where every action carries potentially fatal consequences.

title: Pull out your medallion and demand it halt
narrativeText: In desperate hope, you tear the medallion from beneath your shirt and thrust it high above your head like a talisman against the darkness. The ancient silver disc catches what little light exists in the chamber, but remains stubbornly cold and lifeless in your grasp. Your voice rings out with all the authority you can muster, commanding the undead guardian to halt its advance and acknowledge the power you believe the medallion represents. The creature's response is a continuation of its relentless approach, showing no recognition whatsoever of your sacred symbol. Its hollow eye sockets remain fixed upon you with the same mechanical determination, whilst its twin blades continue their hypnotic rotation. The medallion might as well be any common piece of jewellery for all the effect it has upon this ancient sentinel. The guardian's advance never wavers, each step bringing it closer with the inexorable certainty of fate itself, forcing you to consider more desperate measures.

choices.choiceText: Attempt a forward roll to unbalance it
Back out to the open area

title: Retreat to the open area
narrativeText: The whistling of spinning steel forces your decision, leaving retreat as your only viable option. You stumble backwards through the guardroom's entrance, seeking the tactical advantage of open ground. The undead guardian follows with predatory patience, its movements calculated and precise. Rather than pursuing directly, it begins to circle to your right, herding you like a shepherd guides sheep towards a pen. Each step forces you further backwards until you feel the cold touch of metal against your spine. The gates! In your desperate retreat, you have backed directly into the very barrier you sought to breach. The moment your body makes contact, the impossible happens. The solid metal yields like soft clay, and you feel yourself sinking into the gate's surface as if it were thick, wet mud. Panic floods through you as the metal hardens with supernatural speed, trapping you in its iron embrace. The guardian ceases its blade work and regards you with what might pass for contemplation in its undead features. 'We need a new gaoler and High Priest,' it declares with grim satisfaction. The pommel of one of its weapons descends towards your skull, bringing with it the promise of oblivion.

choices.choiceText: A sealed fate

title: Attempt a forward roll to unbalance it
narrativeText: Drawing upon skills honed through years of training, you execute a perfectly timed forward roll that carries you beneath the guardian's spinning blades. The ancient warrior, despite its supernatural reflexes, was not prepared for such an unconventional manoeuvre. Your rolling motion strikes its legs, knocking the creature off balance and disrupting the deadly rhythm of its weapon work. As you complete the roll and spring back to your feet, you seize the opportunity to reclaim your fallen sword, its familiar weight reassuring in your grip. The guardian staggers momentarily, its perfect poise shattered, before beginning to recover its stance. For the first time in this encounter, you hold the advantage. The creature now faces sideways to your position, its defensive posture temporarily compromised. This window of opportunity will not remain open long, as the guardian's supernatural nature means it will recover with inhuman speed. You must strike now, whilst the element of surprise remains on your side.

choices.choiceText: Strike for its head
Strike for its body
Strike for its legs

title: Strike for its head
narrativeText: You aim for the creature's skull, bringing your blade in a swift overhead arc towards where its head should be most vulnerable to your strike. The distance between you and the undead guard proves fatal to your strategy. Your sword whistles through empty air, falling several inches short of your intended target, the momentum of your missed attack pulling you dangerously off balance. The guard capitalises on your overextended position with the speed and precision of a warrior who has perfected its craft across centuries of undying service. Its far arm sweeps over and around in a devastating arc, the weight of its ancient blade combining with the momentum of your own failed strike to create an unstoppable force. The impact against your shoulder arrives like the blow of a hammer striking an anvil, and the sound that follows is a sickening crunch that echoes off the stone walls of the chamber, a noise like green wood being snapped across someone's knee. You feel rather than see your shoulder collapse inward, bone fragments piercing through muscle tissue as the joint gives way completely under the terrible force. Before you can even begin to register the full extent of the damage being done to your body, before the pain can fully manifest in your consciousness, the guard's second blade arcs upward from below in a perfectly executed counter-strike. The cold steel cleaves through your neck with the surgical precision of an executioner. Your last sensation, in the final moment before darkness claims you entirely, is the metallic taste of your own blood filling your mouth, warm and copper-sweet, as consciousness fades and the torch-lit chamber dissolves into absolute and eternal blackness.

title: Strike for its body
narrativeText: You swing your blade in a horizontal arc, targeting the guard's midsection where its armour appears weakest. Your steel connects solidly with the creature's torso, the impact reverberating up through your arms. However, the guard's ancient armour absorbs most of the blow's force, and worse still, your blade becomes trapped within the overlapping plates of its protection. The undead creature shows no reaction to what should have been a crippling wound, no grunt of pain or stumble backward. Instead, it capitalises on your weapon's entanglement. The guard twists sharply to its right, using your own blade as an anchor point to pull you off balance. The rotational force drags you around in a wide arc, your feet scrambling for purchase on the stone ground as you're spun outward from the sheltered area into the exposed courtyard beyond the gates.

choices.choiceText: Continue the rotation and smash the guard into the gates
Drive the blade deeper into the guard's side
Pull your blade out and set a new attack stance

title: Strike for its legs
narrativeText: You bring your sword arcing downward in a devastating strike that severs the guardian's leg completely, the ancient bone and sinew parting like rotten wood beneath your blade. The creature collapses to the stone floor with a resonating crash, its armour clattering against the unforgiving surface. Despite its grievous wound, the undead warrior attempts to twist its torso to face you, driven by the same relentless purpose that has animated it for centuries. You reset your fighting stance, raising your sword high above your head with both hands gripping the hilt. The second blow falls with surgical precision, your blade cleaving through the guardian's neck in a single, decisive cut. The ancient head separates from its shoulders and rolls across the stone floor before coming to rest against the chamber wall. The body shudders once, then lies perfectly still. The oppressive sense of watchful menace that had filled this place begins to dissipate, leaving behind only the echo of your laboured breathing and the distant sound of dripping water somewhere in the depths beyond.

choices.choiceText: Examine the guard for a key
Examine the guardroom

title: Examine the guard for a key
narrativeText: You kneel beside the fallen guardian, carefully searching through its ancient armour and tattered garments for anything that might serve as a key to the mysterious gates. Your hands explore every crevice and pocket, feeling along the creature's belt and examining each piece of corroded metal that adorns its form. The search proves frustrating, your fingers finding nothing more significant than rust flakes and the occasional shard of deteriorated leather. Whatever secrets this guardian may have possessed, it carried no obvious key upon its person. The creature's empty eye sockets seem to mock your efforts as you complete your thorough examination. With growing certainty that the solution lies elsewhere, you turn your attention to the guardroom itself, knowing that ancient fortifications often concealed their most important mechanisms within the very walls that housed their sentries.

choices.choiceText: Examine the guardroom

title: Continue the rotation and smash the guard into the gates
narrativeText: Rather than resist the spinning motion, you lean into the rotation, shifting your weight forward and adding your own momentum to the deadly dance. A calculated push sends additional force through the turn, and now the guard itself becomes caught up in the centrifugal motion, your embedded sword acting as the pivot point that binds you both together. The creature spins helplessly, trapped by its own armour's grip on your blade. Your combined momentum carries both of you toward the massive iron gates with tremendous force. At the moment of impact, the guard's body strikes the ancient barrier and something extraordinary occurs. The gates surface ripple like disturbed water, and the guard's form sinks immediately into what should be impermeable iron. The gates solidify around the creature's torso within seconds, transforming back to unyielding metal that holds the guard fast. Only its head and thrashing limbs remain free, and it releases a bone-chilling howl of rage that reverberates through the enclosed space.

choices.choiceText: Demand it tell you how to get past the gate and you will release it
The noise will alert others, slay it

title: Drive the blade deeper into the guard's side
narrativeText: You attempt to drive your blade deeper into the guard's side, hoping to find some vital point that might fell the undead creature. However, your momentum from the spinning motion works against you, making it impossible to generate the force needed to penetrate further into its armoured form. The guard takes immediate advantage of your compromised position. Its right arm arcs over in a swift, practiced motion, bringing its weapon down toward your exposed neck. The sensation of cold steel meeting flesh is mercifully brief. The guard's blade finds its mark with lethal precision, severing through bone and sinew in one decisive stroke. Your final moment is a flash of realisation that your tactical gamble has failed, followed by the swift embrace of darkness.

title: Pull your blade out and set a new attack stance
narrativeText: The spinning momentum works in your favour as you wrench your blade free from the guard's armour, the steel sliding out with surprising ease. However, this same rotational force proves to be a double-edged advantage. The sudden release sends you spinning backward, unable to control your trajectory as you're flung toward the massive iron gates behind you. The moment your body makes contact with the ancient barrier, you feel an unnatural sensation as the supposedly solid metal gives way like thick mud, your form sinking into its face. The gates solidify around you with terrifying speed, the iron hardening back to its natural state within heartbeats. You are now completely immobilised, trapped within the gate itself with only your head free to witness what comes next. The guard approaches your helpless form with deliberate steps. It pauses before you, and you can almost see the thought processes working behind its hollow eyes. 'We need a new Gaoler and High Priest,' it intones in a voice like grinding stone. The pommel of its blade descends toward your skull, and consciousness abandons you to merciful blackness.

choices.choiceText: A sealed fate

title: His crimes are too many slay him
narrativeText: The commander's words of remorse hang in the air between you, but something in his manner strikes you as false, a performance carefully constructed to elicit sympathy and trust. You have witnessed too much evil within these mountain depths to accept such a convenient transformation at face value, and the long centuries he spent enforcing Baelmat's will cannot be so easily washed away by tears and professed shame. Your eyes move across the chamber floor, taking in the scattered remains of the undead army that served the dark lord, their golden armour now reduced to puddles of cooling metal amongst piles of ash that were once animate corpses. Among this debris, you spot a sword that has fallen from a guard's grasp during the purifying fire, its blade still intact despite the destruction that claimed its wielder. You move towards it with careful deliberation, bending to retrieve the weapon whilst keeping the commander in your peripheral vision. The hilt feels cold and heavy in your grip, its balance unfamiliar but adequate for what you know must be done. Rising to your full height with the sword now held loosely at your side, you turn to face the freed commander and speak with false agreement. Show me the way to this treasure you mentioned, you tell him, allowing a note of greed to colour your voice, making it seem as though material wealth matters more to you than justice or caution. The commander's expression brightens with what appears to be genuine relief at your acceptance of his offer, and he turns with apparent eagerness to lead you from the throne chamber into the passages beyond. It is in that moment of turning, when his back presents itself as an undefended target, that you strike with swift and terrible finality. The blade arcs through the smoke-tinged air and cleaves through the commander's neck in a single decisive blow, separating his head from his shoulders before he can register the betrayal. His body collapses forward onto the ash-strewn floor whilst his head rolls several feet before coming to rest against a puddle of melted armour, his expression frozen in a look of surprise rather than pain. You stand over the fallen form, breathing heavily, knowing that you have just executed someone who claimed to be freed from evil's grip, yet unable to shake the certainty that his contrition was merely another form of deception, one final trap laid by Baelmat's most loyal servant. With the immediate threat ended, you begin to search the throne chamber and the passages beyond for the treasure that brought you to this cursed place so many trials ago. The sconces continue to burn with their steady light as you explore corridor after corridor, chamber after chamber, finding only empty rooms and abandoned halls. There is no treasure room filled with gold and gems, no vault overflowing with the wealth of ages. Perhaps such riches never truly existed, or perhaps they were consumed long ago to fuel Baelmat's dark rituals. What you do discover, however, as you make your way back through the mountain's depths towards the distant daylight beyond, is something that weighs less than gold but carries greater significance. The oppressive atmosphere that has pervaded every passage and chamber has lifted completely, replaced by nothing more sinister than the natural chill of stone and earth. The supernatural dread that made every shadow a potential threat has dissipated like morning mist before the sun. You emerge from Murlore's Spike empty-handed in terms of material wealth, but carrying the certain knowledge that the darkness which has plagued this region for untold years has finally been brought to an end. The villages in the valleys below will sleep safely in their beds, the roads will carry travellers without fear, and the mountain itself can begin the long process of becoming merely stone and earth once more, rather than a fortress of evil. Whether you made the right choice regarding the commander's fate remains a question that will likely haunt your thoughts for years to come, but at least the greater evil has been destroyed, and that, perhaps, is treasure enough.
